text: Patients with a {{c1::probable UIP}} HRCT pattern can be diagnosed with IPF via {{c2::multidisciplinary discussion (MDD)}} without biopsy in the appropriate clinical context.
extra: Table 3 — HRCT patterns in IPF; Figure 10 — Diagnostic algorithm for IPF

text: Genetic testing for FH-I (CYP11B1/CYP11B2 chimera) is offered when PA onset is <{{c1::20}} years or there is a family history of PA/stroke <{{c2::40}} years.
LR-M lesions are {{c1::93–100%}} likely to be malignant, but only {{c2::29–44%}} are specifically HCC.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Diagnosis section / LI-RADS algorithm

text: All patients with SLE/lupus nephritis should receive {{c1::hydroxychloroquine}} unless contraindicated.
DKA requires acidosis defined as pH {{c1::<7.3}} and/or bicarbonate {{c2::<18 mmol/L}}.
In CKD G5D, calcitriol, vitamin D analogs, or calcimimetics should be {{c1::reduced or stopped}} if iPTH falls below {{c2::2×}} the upper limit of normal.
For initial treatment of cancer-associated VTE (0–10 days), {{c1::LMWH}} is preferred over UFH due to lower mortality and recurrence.
extra: Appendix 6 Q1 Conclusions — Initial treatment of VTE; Hokusai-VTE Cancer, CARAVAGGIO

text: The preferred initial eGFR equation in KDIGO 2024 is the {{c1::2021 CKD-EPI creatinine}} equation, which is {{c2::race-free}}.

Naltrexone-bupropion ER dose adjustments: maximum {{c1::1 tablet BID}} in moderate-severe renal impairment; maximum {{c2::1 tablet daily}} in moderate-severe hepatic impairment; avoid entirely in {{c3::ESRD}}.
In classic GERD (heartburn, regurgitation) without alarm features, first-line therapy is an {{c1::8-week}} PPI trial taken {{c2::30-60 minutes}} before the first meal of the day.
extra: GERD Medical Management / Proton pump inhibitors — PPI dosing, timing

text: Montreal E1 (proctitis) = inflammation ≤{{c1::15 cm}}; E2 (left-sided) = up to {{c2::splenic flexure}}; E3 (extensive) = proximal to splenic flexure.
extra: Diagnosis, Assessment, Monitoring, and Prognosis of UC — Montreal classification

text: Dietary supplements including {{c1::fish oil, omega-3 fatty acids, vitamins, antioxidants, and calcium}} are NOT recommended for cardiovascular event reduction in CCD.
extra: Top 10 Take-Home Messages — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Follow-up duodenal biopsy to assess mucosal healing in adults should be considered ~{{c1::2 years}} after starting a gluten-free diet (median time to mucosal healing in adults is ~{{c2::3 years}}).
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diet and long-term outcomes / Figure 2

text: In pediatric Graves' disease, MMI is trialed for {{c1::1–2 years}}, with remission rates of approximately {{c2::20–30%}} at 2 years.
extra: Recommendations 58–72 (Sections O–R) — pediatric Graves' disease management

text: When initiating ACEi or ARB for albuminuria in SCD, start at a {{c1::lower dose}} if GFR is <{{c2::45}} mL/min/1.73m².

Rheumatic fever secondary prophylaxis uses penicillin G benzathine {{c1::1.2 million U IM every 4 weeks}} (every 3 weeks in high-risk patients) for ≥{{c2::10 years}} or until age 40 with residual VHD.
For dabigatran-associated ICH, the preferred reversal agent is {{c1::idarucizumab 5 g IV}} (given as two 2.5-g boluses).
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: In the ADA Standards of Care evidence-grading system, a Grade {{c1::A}} recommendation is supported by {{c2::well-conducted randomized controlled trial (RCT)}} evidence.
extra: Table 1, ADA evidence-grading system — definitions of A, B, C, E levels

text: HHS is distinguished from DKA by β-hydroxybutyrate {{c1::<3.0 mmol/L}}, pH {{c2::≥7.3}}, and bicarbonate ≥15 mmol/L.
CEA (preferred over CAS) is indicated for symptomatic carotid stenosis of {{c1::70–99%}} and is reasonable for {{c2::50–69%}}, provided perioperative stroke/death risk is <6%.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.1.2.1 (Extracranial carotid); NASCET, ECST, CREST

text: When nirmatrelvir/ritonavir is precluded by drug interactions in high-risk outpatients, the preferred alternative antiviral is {{c1::IV remdesivir (3-day course)}}.
extra: Remdesivir for Mild or Moderate COVID-19 (updated 10/14/2025)

text: Orthostatic hypotension is defined as a drop in SBP ≥{{c1::20}} mm Hg or DBP ≥{{c2::10}} mm Hg on standing (≥30 mm Hg SBP in patients with supine hypertension).
The 2024 USPSTF recommends biennial screening mammography for all women aged {{c1::40 to 74}} years, with a Grade {{c2::B}} recommendation.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — age range, interval, and grades

text: Once-daily aminoglycoside doses (normal renal function): gentamicin/tobramycin {{c1::5 mg/kg/d}}; netilmicin {{c2::6 mg/kg/d}}; amikacin {{c3::15 mg/kg/d}}.

The 2024 USPSTF average-risk mammography recommendation does NOT apply to women with {{c1::BRCA1/BRCA2}} mutations, prior {{c2::high-dose chest radiation at young age}}, or prior breast cancer/high-risk lesion on biopsy.
extra: Patient Population Under Consideration — exclusions (BRCA, prior chest RT, prior breast cancer/high-risk lesion)

text: AKI Stage 2: SCr {{c1::2.0–2.9×}} baseline; UO <0.5 mL/kg/h for ≥{{c2::12 h}}.
In congenital long QT syndrome, the preferred beta blockers are {{c1::nadolol}} or {{c1::propranolol}}; {{c2::mexiletine}} is added for LQT3.
In hospitalized adults on systemic glucocorticoids with rapidly progressing severe or critical COVID-19, add either {{c1::baricitinib}} or {{c2::tocilizumab}} as an additional immunomodulator.
The four pillars of GDMT for HFrEF are: {{c1::ARNi}} (preferred over ACEi/ARB), evidence-based {{c2::beta blocker}}, {{c3::MRA}}, and {{c4::SGLT2 inhibitor}}.
Lp(a) ≥250 nmol/L confers approximately {{c1::2×}} ASCVD risk; ≥430 nmol/L confers approximately {{c2::4×}} ASCVD risk.
extra: Section 4.2.10 / Table 4 — Lp(a) thresholds and ASCVD relative risk

text: Among FDA-approved cessation pharmacotherapies, {{c1::varenicline}} appears more effective than both NRT and bupropion SR.
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Tobacco Cessation — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: In CKD stage ≥3 with ASCVD, high-intensity statin ± ezetimibe ± PCSK9 mAb targets ≥50% LDL-C reduction and LDL-C <{{c1::55 mg/dL}}.
In DMARD-naive patients with moderate-to-high disease activity, {{c1::methotrexate monotherapy}} is strongly recommended as initial therapy.

In severe hypercholesterolemia (LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL) with clinical ASCVD, combination nonstatin therapy (ezetimibe, PCSK9 mAb, or bempedoic acid) added to maximally tolerated statin aims for LDL-C <{{c1::55 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.4.3 / Figure 7 — Severe hypercholesterolemia management with combination LLT

text: CHEST 2021 recommends against extended-phase anticoagulation for VTE provoked by a {{c1::major or minor transient risk factor}}.
ACR 2020 conditionally recommends HLA-B*5801 testing before starting allopurinol in patients of {{c1::Southeast Asian descent (Han Chinese, Korean, Thai)}} or {{c2::African American}} ethnicity.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 4 — HLA-B*5801 testing

text: Uncomplicated gonorrhea (cervix, urethra, rectum, pharynx) in patients <150 kg is treated with {{c1::ceftriaxone 500 mg IM}} as a single dose.
extra: Treatment of Gonococcal Infections — CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: The MMF induction target dose for lupus nephritis is {{c1::2–3 g/day}} orally for {{c2::6 months}}.
High-intensity statin options in CCD are {{c1::atorvastatin 40-80 mg}} or {{c2::rosuvastatin 20-40 mg}} daily.
extra: Section 4.2.6 Lipid Management — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: For active severe EGPA with cardiac involvement, {{c1::cyclophosphamide}} is the preferred induction agent (mepolizumab is not used for severe EGPA induction).
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 1–4 (EGPA); Table 3

text: Ticagrelor dosing: load {{c1::180 mg}}, maintenance {{c2::90 mg twice daily}}; aspirin maintenance should be ≤100 mg daily when used with ticagrelor.
For On-X mechanical AVR after 3 months with no risk factors, target INR is {{c1::1.5–2.0}} plus aspirin {{c2::75–100 mg}} daily.
The preferred biomarker for diagnosing or excluding acute myocardial injury in chest pain is {{c1::high-sensitivity cardiac troponin (hs-cTn)}}; {{c2::CK-MB and myoglobin}} should NOT be added.
In children after {{c1::biventricular congenital heart repair}}, the AABB 2023 recommended RBC transfusion threshold is Hgb <{{c2::7 g/dL}}.
Testosterone pellets are implanted SC at {{c1::600–1200 mg}}, providing androgen delivery for {{c2::3–6 months}}.
For STEMI requiring interhospital transfer for primary PCI, the acceptable FMC-to-device time is ≤{{c1::120 minutes}}.
In untreated CIS or relapsing MS without relapses in the preceding 2 years and no new MRI activity, serial MRI should be performed at least annually for the first {{c1::5 years}} (Level C).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 8

text: IDSA/SHEA recommends serial {{c1::procalcitonin}} measurements in adult ICU patients with suspected infection to guide antibiotic {{c2::discontinuation}}, but NOT to withhold initiation (weak, moderate).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XVIII, Recommendation 19

text: EGD should be performed within {{c1::12 hours}} of presentation in acute variceal hemorrhage.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Figure 4

text: A minor transient risk factor (e.g., surgery with GA <30 min, estrogen use, pregnancy/puerperium, prolonged travel) must have occurred within {{c1::2 months}} before the VTE to qualify as the provoking event.
The PHQ-9 at a standard cutoff of ≥{{c1::10}} has a sensitivity of {{c2::0.85}} and specificity of {{c2::0.85}} for major depression.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests

text: ACR 2020 strongly recommends initiating ULT in gout patients with ≥1 subcutaneous tophus, {{c1::radiographic damage from gout}}, or {{c2::≥2 flares/year}}.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 1 — Indications for pharmacologic ULT

text: Acquired {{c1::second-degree Mobitz II, high-grade, or third-degree AV block}} not from reversible or physiologic causes warrants permanent pacing regardless of symptoms.
extra: Section 6.4.4/Figure 7 — chronic AV block management algorithm

text: In NAFLD-related cirrhosis, HCC surveillance should be performed {{c1::biannually (every 6 months)}}.
KDIGO 2022 recommends at least {{c1::150 minutes/week}} of {{c2::moderate-intensity}} physical activity in patients with diabetes and CKD, or as tolerated (1D).
The three FDA-approved tobacco cessation pharmacotherapies are {{c1::nicotine replacement therapy (NRT)}}, {{c2::bupropion SR}}, and {{c3::varenicline}}.
After an initial Tdap dose, adults should receive a Td or Tdap booster every {{c1::10 years}}; {{c2::1 dose Tdap}} is also indicated during each pregnancy.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Tdap/Td, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Recombinant zoster vaccine (RZV) is given as a {{c1::2-dose}} series to all adults ≥{{c2::50}} years.
extra: Notes — Zoster recombinant (RZV) ages ≥50, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The 2020 NTCA/CDC guideline recommends {{c1::short-course rifamycin-based regimens (3HP, 4R, or 3HR)}} as preferred therapy for LTBI over isoniazid monotherapy, due to equivalent efficacy, lower hepatotoxicity, and higher completion rates.
MMR vaccine (1–2 doses) is indicated for adults born in {{c1::1957 or later}} who lack evidence of immunity.
extra: Notes — MMR vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The ACR/NPF 2018 guideline recommends using a {{c1::treat-to-target}} strategy in patients with active PsA.
For CI-AKI prevention, KDIGO 2012 recommends using {{c1::iso-osmolar or low-osmolar}} contrast media and the {{c2::lowest possible}} contrast dose (1B).
In older adults, statin therapy should be considered if life expectancy ≥{{c1::2.5 years}} and generally discontinued if life expectancy <{{c2::1 year}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — Statin use in older adults

text: In intermediate or borderline-risk adults, CAC ≥{{c1::100}} Agatston units or ≥{{c1::75th percentile}} prompts LLT initiation.
Neurosyphilis (and ocular/otosyphilis) is treated with aqueous crystalline penicillin G {{c1::18–24 million units/day IV}} (3–4 million units q4h) for {{c2::10–14 days}}.
extra: Syphilis — Neurosyphilis section, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: High-dose oral glucocorticoids are defined as prednisone {{c1::1 mg/kg/day}} up to a maximum of {{c2::80 mg/day}}.
extra: Table 1 — definitions of disease states and glucocorticoid dose categories

text: For active Class III/IV LN, induction therapy consists of glucocorticoids plus either {{c1::mycophenolate mofetil (MMF)}} or {{c2::low-dose IV cyclophosphamide (Euro-Lupus regimen)}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Treatment of Class III/IV LN

text: For axial/spondylitis PsA failing NSAIDs, first-line biologic therapy is a {{c1::TNFi}}; IL-17i is second-line; {{c2::IL-12/23i}} should NOT be used (failed in axial SpA trials).
CI-AKI risk is elevated at eGFR <{{c1::60 mL/min/1.73 m²}}; some evidence supports a threshold of <{{c2::45 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
ASH 2018 recommends against routine VTE prophylaxis in {{c1::chronically ill or nursing home patients}} and in {{c2::outpatients with minor provoking factors}} (immobility, minor injury, infection).
The extended-pulsed fidaxomicin regimen for CDI is 200 mg PO twice daily for 5 days, then {{c1::once every other day on days 7–25}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: When aspirin is used in combination with ticagrelor, the maintenance dose of aspirin should be limited to ≤{{c1::100 mg/day}}.
Diuretics should {{c1::not}} be used to prevent AKI (1B) and should not be used to treat AKI itself (2C); they may be used only to manage {{c2::volume overload}}.
Initial pharmacotherapy for GOLD Group A (low symptoms, 0 exacerbations) is {{c1::a single bronchodilator}}.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.7 — Initial pharmacological treatment algorithm

text: In adults ≥40 on GCs who are not on OP medication, BMD should be reassessed every {{c1::1–3 years}}.
Head-to-head, {{c1::vedolizumab}} is superior to {{c2::adalimumab}} and is preferred in TNF-naive patients with UC.
For refractory TAK, a {{c1::TNF inhibitor}} is preferred over tocilizumab as add-on therapy.
USPSTF recommends beginning cervical cancer screening at age {{c1::21}} using {{c2::cytology (Pap smear) alone}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table; Clinical Considerations: Timing of Screening — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: Brugada type 1 ECG pattern requires ST elevation ≥{{c1::2 mm}} with type 1 morphology in ≥1 right precordial lead ({{c2::V1–V2}}).
CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::against}} routine use of graduated compression stockings to prevent {{c2::post-thrombotic syndrome}} after acute DVT (weak, low-certainty).
Anticoagulation duration in isolated HIT is until platelet recovery and generally not beyond {{c1::3 months}}; for HIT with thrombosis (HITT), treat for {{c2::3–6 months}}.
For obstructive intraventricular hemorrhage (IVH) with hydrocephalus and depressed consciousness, place an EVD and add intraventricular {{c1::alteplase}} to reduce mortality (CLEAR III trial).
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Sections 6.1.1–6.1.4; CLEAR III

text: Outpatient remdesivir dosing for high-risk COVID-19: {{c1::200 mg IV on day 1}}, then {{c1::100 mg IV on days 2 and 3}} (3-day course); no renal dose adjustment required.
extra: Remdesivir for Mild or Moderate COVID-19 (updated 10/14/2025)

text: When immunotherapy is contraindicated in advanced HCC, first-line systemic therapy should be {{c1::sorafenib}} or {{c2::lenvatinib}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Figure 16 / Tables 7–8

text: In a patient with LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL and HeFH or other risk factors (but no clinical ASCVD), the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<70 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.4.3 / Figure 7 — Tiered LDL-C goals for severe hypercholesterolemia

text: For acute spontaneous ICH with SBP 150–220 mm Hg, the target SBP is {{c1::130–<140 mm Hg}} maintained for ≥{{c2::7 days}}; antihypertensives should be stopped if SBP falls <130 mm Hg.
Four-strata PAH risk BNP cutoffs (low/intermediate-low/intermediate-high/high) are: <{{c1::50}}, {{c1::50–199}}, {{c1::200–800}}, >{{c1::800}} ng/L.
extra: Section 6.2.7 / Tables 16 and 18 — four-strata risk assessment model

text: Surgical evacuation is recommended for cerebellar ICH with volume {{c1::>15 mL}}, neurologic deterioration, brainstem compression, or hydrocephalus.
In diagnosing acute pancreatitis, {{c1::lipase}} is preferred over {{c2::amylase}} because it is more sensitive and specific.
After radical prostatectomy, approximately {{c1::1 in 5}} men develop long-term urinary incontinence and approximately {{c2::2 in 3}} develop long-term erectile dysfunction.
extra: Discussion: Potential Harms of Treatment — USPSTF 2018

text: Catheter-directed thrombolysis is effective for arterial or graft occlusion of <{{c1::14 days}} duration.
When corticotropin testing is unavailable, a morning cortisol {{c1::<140 nmol/L (5 μg/dL)}} plus plasma ACTH >2× the upper limit of normal is presumptive evidence of PAI.

Triptans and ergots are contraindicated in {{c1::coronary artery disease, peripheral vascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, or significant vascular risk}}; gepants, ditans, and neuromodulation are preferred alternatives in these patients.
extra: AHS 2021 — Acute Treatment: Indications and Principles

text: Carvedilol dosing for portal hypertension: start {{c1::6.25 mg/day}}, titrate to {{c2::12.5 mg/day}} if tolerated and systolic BP ≥90 mm Hg.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH?

text: If a biologic is needed in a patient with NTM lung disease, {{c1::abatacept}} is preferred and TNF inhibitors should be {{c2::avoided}}.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: Digoxin toxicity causing bradycardia is treated with {{c1::digoxin-specific Fab fragments}}.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: In axial SpA with recurrent uveitis or coexisting IBD, prefer TNFi {{c1::monoclonal antibodies (adalimumab, infliximab)}} over {{c2::etanercept}} and over IL-17 inhibitors.
Intraarticular glucocorticoid injections are strongly recommended for knee and hip OA; {{c1::ultrasound guidance}} is required for {{c2::hip}} injections.
Routine {{c1::polysomnography}} is not recommended in asymptomatic SCD patients; a sleep study is obtained based on symptoms such as snoring, witnessed apneas, or unexplained desaturation.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Sleep-Disordered Breathing: Screening

text: For ASCVD patients on statin with TG {{c1::150–499 mg/dL}} and LDL-C 41–100 mg/dL, consider icosapent ethyl {{c2::4 g/day (2 g twice daily)}}.
OCS for adult asthma exacerbation: {{c1::prednisone/prednisolone 40–50 mg/day}} for {{c2::5–7 days}} (no taper if course <2 weeks).
In asymptomatic {{c1::postmenopausal women and men}} with IDA, the AGA recommends bidirectional endoscopy (EGD + colonoscopy) performed at the same setting (strong recommendation, moderate quality).
DOACs should be avoided in cancer-associated thrombosis patients with {{c1::luminal GI cancers}}, unresected GU lesions, strong {{c2::P-gp/CYP3A4}} modulators, or severe thrombocytopenia.
extra: Appendix 6 Q1/Q2 Conclusions; Appendix 6 Q8 Conclusions

text: A CAC score ≥{{c1::100 AU}} or ≥{{c1::75th percentile}} supports initiating lipid-lowering therapy (LDL-C goal <70 mg/dL); ≥{{c2::1000 AU}} supports a goal of <55 mg/dL.
extra: Section 4.2.3.6 — CAC scoring thresholds and management

text: A long-acting GLP-1 RA should be added in T2D with CKD when {{c1::glycemic targets are not met on metformin + SGLT2i}}, or when those agents cannot be used (1B).
Per CLSI M39, stratified antibiograms require a minimum of {{c1::30}} isolates per organism per stratum.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section on diagnostics/microbiology

text: For moderate-to-severe Crohn's, combination of {{c1::infliximab + azathioprine}} is superior to monotherapy for induction and maintenance (SONIC trial).
extra: Moderate-to-severe disease/Anti-TNF agents — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: In PAD patients without high bleeding risk, add rivaroxaban {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}} to aspirin {{c2::81 mg daily}} to reduce MACE and MALE (COMPASS/VOYAGER regimen).
extra: Section 5.1 — COMPASS and VOYAGER PAD trial evidence; Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: For indeterminate FNA cytology categories {{c1::AUS/FLUS}} or {{c1::FN/SFN}}, molecular testing (7-gene panel or gene expression classifier) may be used to refine malignancy risk.
For women with dense breasts and an otherwise negative mammogram, the 2024 USPSTF assigns an {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement to supplemental screening with {{c2::ultrasound or MRI}}.
extra: I statements — women ≥75 and supplemental screening for dense breasts

text: Prone positioning for > {{c1::12 hours}}/day is recommended in ARDS when PaO2/FiO2 < {{c2::150}}.
extra: Ventilation / Protective Ventilation in ARDS; SSC 2021

text: IV magnesium sulfate for severe pediatric asthma exacerbation (≥2 years): {{c1::40–50 mg/kg (max 2 g)}} over {{c2::20 minutes}}.

For bleeding cardiofundal varices, TIPS is preferred when MELD-Na <{{c1::20}} with ascites or large esophageal varices; BRTO is preferred when MELD-Na >{{c1::20}} or hepatic encephalopathy is present.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Gastric and ectopic varices

text: The 2026 ACC/AHA guideline recommends measuring Lp(a) {{c1::at least once}} in every adult for ASCVD risk assessment.
extra: Section 3.4 / Recommendation — Universal once-in-lifetime Lp(a) measurement

text: The estimated malignancy risk for a high-suspicion sonographic pattern is {{c1::>70–90%}}, compared with {{c2::10–20%}} for an intermediate-suspicion pattern.
Refractory celiac disease is defined as persistent malabsorptive symptoms with villous atrophy despite a strict GFD for >{{c1::12 months}}.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Medical device use / Figure 3

text: Treatment of subclinical hyperthyroidism is reasonable to consider when TSH is {{c1::0.1–0.4 mU/L}} in patients aged ≥65 years or those at high risk.
extra: Recommendations 73–76, Table 10 — subclinical hyperthyroidism treatment thresholds

text: When vonoprazan-clarithromycin triple therapy is used, the regimen is vonoprazan {{c1::20 mg}} + clarithromycin {{c2::500 mg}} + amoxicillin {{c3::1,000 mg}}, all given BID for 14 days.
Inpatients with {{c1::severe}} CAP reaching clinical stability should receive {{c2::≥5 days}} of antibiotics rather than <5 days ({{c3::strong}} recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 3

text: In PsA patients with diabetes, the preferred first-line agent is an OSM {{c1::other than methotrexate}} (due to {{c2::MTX hepatotoxicity risk with fatty liver}}) over a TNFi.
In HBV/HIV coinfection, antiretroviral therapy should contain two HBV-active drugs: {{c1::tenofovir (TAF or TDF)}} plus {{c2::lamivudine or emtricitabine}}.
For an acute gout flare, ACR 2020 recommends {{c1::oral colchicine, NSAIDs, or glucocorticoids (oral/IA/IM)}} as first-line agents, over IL-1 inhibitors or ACTH.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 6 — Gout flare management

text: AAN 2018 recommends offering {{c1::ocrelizumab}} to ambulatory patients with primary progressive MS unless risks outweigh benefits (Level B).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 17

text: In PsA with concomitant active IBD, the guideline STRONGLY recommends a {{c1::monoclonal antibody TNFi (not etanercept) or IL-12/23i}} over {{c2::IL-17i}}, because IL-17i can worsen IBD.
Aminoglycoside dosing for HAP/VAP: amikacin {{c1::15–20 mg/kg IV q24h}}; gentamicin/tobramycin {{c2::5–7 mg/kg IV q24h}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3

text: The LDL-C reduction goal with high-intensity statin therapy in CCD is ≥{{c1::50%}}.
extra: Section 4.2.6 Lipid Management — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: KDIGO 2021 recommends complementing standardized office BP with {{c1::ABPM or HBPM}} to identify {{c2::white-coat and masked hypertension}} (2B).
extra: Chapter 1, Recommendation 1.2 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Teriparatide is a {{c1::recombinant PTH}} analogue used as an anabolic agent in osteoporosis.
MART with budesonide-formoterol 160/4.5 mcg for adults: Step 3 = {{c1::1 inh BID}} + 1 inh as needed; Step 4 = {{c2::2 inh BID}} + 1 inh as needed.
Add antibiotics to I&D for surgical site infection when: erythema/induration >{{c1::5 cm}} from wound edge, T >{{c2::38.5°C}}, HR >{{c2::110}}, or WBC >{{c2::12,000}}/µL.
Colonoscopy is performed every {{c1::10 years}} for average-risk colorectal cancer screening.
True normal ALT upper limits: {{c1::29–33}} U/L in men and {{c2::19–25}} U/L in women; chronic ALT/AST >30 U/L suggests hepatic injury.
Finerenone requires a baseline serum K⁺ ≤{{c1::4.8}} mmol/L to initiate; it should be held if K⁺ rises >{{c2::5.5}} mmol/L and restarted when K⁺ falls to ≤5.0 mmol/L.
Pancreatic cancer should be suspected as an underlying etiology in patients with idiopathic acute pancreatitis who are older than {{c1::40 years}}, especially if recurrent.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Etiology; Thresholds & Doses

text: In HCM, a major SCD risk factor warranting consideration of primary prevention ICD is LV wall thickness ≥{{c1::30 mm}}.
extra: Section 7.4, Table 8 — HCM SCD risk factors

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::CRRT}} rather than intermittent RRT for hemodynamically unstable patients (2B) and for patients with {{c2::acute brain injury or raised intracranial pressure}} (2B).
Management of CPVT with arrhythmic syncope includes {{c1::beta-blockers ± flecainide}} plus {{c2::exercise restriction}}.
Empiric broad-spectrum therapy should be de-escalated based on {{c1::culture results}}; antibiotic discontinuation should be guided by {{c2::PCT}} plus clinical criteria (not CPIS alone).
Cilostazol tolerability should be assessed at {{c1::2–4 weeks}}; clinical benefit is assessed at {{c2::3–6 months}}.
Dofetilide is dosed at {{c1::500 μg BID}} for CrCl >60, {{c2::250 μg BID}} for CrCl 40–60, {{c3::125 μg BID}} for CrCl 20–40, and is contraindicated for CrCl <20 mL/min.
GINA 2026 recommends against treating asthma with {{c1::SABA alone}} in any patient ≥6 years; all patients must receive {{c2::ICS-containing therapy}}.
A CRP of ≤{{c1::0.5 mg/dL}} yields approximately 1% probability of IBD in suspected IBS-D.
extra: Recommendation on fecal calprotectin/CRP — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: The 2020 ACC/AHA VHD guideline stages valve disease as: {{c1::A}} (at risk), {{c2::B}} (progressive), {{c3::C}} (asymptomatic severe), and {{c4::D}} (symptomatic severe).
AVR is indicated for symptomatic severe AS (Stage {{c1::D}}) and asymptomatic severe AS with LVEF {{c2::<50%}} (Stage C2).
MPAA maintenance therapy after active lupus nephritis induction should continue for a total immunosuppression duration of ≥{{c1::36 months}}.
In critically ill patients, KDIGO 2012 recommends targeting plasma glucose {{c1::110–149 mg/dL}} with insulin (2C).
In adults 40–75 with diabetes without ASCVD, prescribe {{c1::moderate}}-intensity statin (LDL-C <100 mg/dL); escalate to {{c2::high}}-intensity statin if multiple risk factors (LDL-C <70 mg/dL).
extra: Section 4.2.3.7 — Primary prevention algorithm by risk category

text: In HBV-associated glomerulonephritis, antiviral therapy with nucleos(t)ide analogues is indicated when HBV DNA is >{{c1::2000 IU/ml}}.
Standard enoxaparin dosing in ACS is {{c1::1 mg/kg SC q12h}}, reduced to {{c2::1 mg/kg SC daily}} if CrCl <30 mL/min.
The low-dose colchicine regimen for an acute gout flare is {{c1::1.2 mg}} at onset, then {{c2::0.6 mg}} one hour later.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 6 — Colchicine flare dosing (FDA-approved)

text: In CKD G1–G4 with atrial fibrillation, {{c1::NOACs}} are preferred over {{c1::warfarin}} for anticoagulation (1C).
Add {{c1::5–10% dextrose}} to IV fluids when glucose falls below {{c2::250 mg/dL}} in DKA, and reduce insulin to 0.05 units/kg/h.
The blood pressure target in CCD patients with hypertension is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}, with optimal DBP of {{c2::70-80 mm Hg}}.
extra: Section 4.2.7 Blood Pressure Management; Table 12 — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Abatacept for COVID-19 (alternative immunomodulator): {{c1::10 mg/kg IV (max 1000 mg)}} as a single dose plus standard of care.
Pemivibart should be administered at least {{c1::2 weeks}} after COVID-19 vaccination; its notable infusion risk is {{c2::anaphylaxis}} (rate ~0.6%).
Allopurinol should be started at ≤{{c1::100 mg/day}} in patients with normal renal function, and ≤{{c2::50 mg/day}} in CKD stage ≥3, then titrated to target.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2 — Low starting doses

text: Dexamethasone is avoided as glucocorticoid replacement in adrenal insufficiency due to risk of {{c1::Cushingoid}} side effects.
AF duration classification: paroxysmal ≤{{c1::7 days}}; persistent >{{c2::7 days}}; long-standing persistent >{{c3::12 months}}.
If a bacteriuric older patient develops fever or sepsis without a localizing source, initiate {{c1::empiric broad-spectrum therapy covering both urinary and nonurinary sources}} pending cultures.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section V (Older patients with delirium/falls)

text: CRT is indicated for LVEF {{c1::≤35%}}, sinus rhythm, LBBB with QRS ≥{{c2::150 ms}}, and NYHA class {{c3::II–IV}} on GDMT.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Thresholds & Doses / CRT section

text: KDIGO 2021 advises ≥{{c1::150 minutes per week}} of {{c2::moderate-intensity}} physical activity, individualized to cardiovascular and physical tolerance, in CKD (2C).
extra: Chapter 2, Recommendation 2.2.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: EPDS cutoffs of {{c1::11–12}} yield sensitivity of 81–90% and specificity of 83–88% for perinatal depression.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests

text: Inferior petrosal sinus sampling (IPSS) is indicated in ACTH-dependent Cushing's syndrome when no clear pituitary lesion {{c1::>6 mm}} is visible, to distinguish pituitary from ectopic source.
Compared with deferred treatment, immediate AED therapy reduces absolute 2-year recurrence risk by approximately {{c1::35%}} (95% CI 23%–46%) (Level B).
extra: Table 2 — Short-term recurrence rates: immediate vs deferred AED treatment

text: In secondary prevention at very high ASCVD risk, the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<55 mg/dL}} and the non–HDL-C goal is {{c2::<85 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.6 / Figures 10–11 — Secondary prevention, very-high-risk criteria

text: For ACS patients undergoing PCI, {{c1::radial}} access is preferred over {{c2::femoral}} access to reduce bleeding, vascular complications, and mortality.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Top Take-Home Messages

text: The 2026 guideline recommends {{c1::against}} using dietary supplements (fish oil, red yeast rice, garlic, cinnamon, plant sterols, turmeric) to lower LDL-C or triglycerides.
Cat scratch disease in patients >45 kg: azithromycin {{c1::500 mg}} on day 1, then {{c1::250 mg}} × 4 days; in patients <45 kg: {{c2::10 mg/kg}} day 1, then {{c2::5 mg/kg}} × 4 days.
Clopidogrel loading dose for DAPT in minor stroke/TIA is {{c1::300 mg}} (CHANCE trial) or {{c2::600 mg}} (POINT trial), followed by 75 mg daily.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.19; CHANCE and POINT trials

text: The PHQ-2 at a standard cutoff of ≥{{c1::2}} has a sensitivity of {{c2::0.91}} and specificity of {{c2::0.67}} for major depression.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests

text: The guideline recommends AGAINST prolonged high-pressure recruitment maneuvers, defined as airway pressure ≥{{c1::35 cmH2O}} held for ≥{{c2::1 minute}}.
The treatment phase of anticoagulation for all acute VTE lasts {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}}, after which each patient should be reassessed for extended-phase therapy.
In IPF, antacid medication {{c1::should not}} be used solely to improve respiratory outcomes; antacids remain appropriate for {{c2::GERD}}-related indications (conditional, very low quality).
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of IPF — antacid medication conditional recommendation against

text: Mepolizumab dose: {{c1::100 mg SC q4 weeks}} for patients ≥12 years; {{c2::40 mg SC q4 weeks}} for children 6–11 years.
Molnupiravir is reserved for high-risk outpatients with {{c1::no other treatment option}}, and is not authorized in {{c2::patients <18 years or in pregnancy}}.
extra: How to Approach a Patient with Mild to Moderate COVID-19 — antiviral selection algorithm

text: Milan criteria for liver transplantation in HCC: {{c1::1 lesion 1–5 cm}} OR {{c1::2–3 lesions each 1–3 cm}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Liver Transplantation / Table 3 / Figure 12

text: In severe UC, use {{c1::flexible sigmoidoscopy}} rather than full colonoscopy to reduce {{c2::perforation}} risk.
extra: Diagnosis, Assessment, Monitoring, and Prognosis of UC — endoscopic diagnostic approach

text: Evidence-based short-course antibiotic durations: intra-abdominal infection {{c1::4 days}} post-source control; vertebral osteomyelitis {{c2::6 weeks}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XIII, Recommendation 14 and Table 2

text: High-intensity statin regimens for ACS include {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} or {{c2::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}}, targeting ≥50% LDL-C reduction.
Adult outpatients {{c1::without comorbidities}} with CAP and a positive respiratory virus test: empiric antibiotics should {{c2::NOT}} be prescribed (conditional recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 2

text: In adult {{c1::orthopedic surgery}} patients or those with preexisting {{c1::cardiovascular disease}}, an RBC transfusion threshold of Hgb <{{c2::8 g/dL}} is acceptable per AABB 2023.
In secondary (central) hypothyroidism, LT4 is titrated to maintain {{c1::free T4 in the upper half of the reference range}}; TSH should NOT be used as the therapeutic target.

All adults with resistant hypertension should be screened for primary aldosteronism using {{c1::aldosterone-to-renin ratio}}, regardless of {{c2::potassium level}}, while continuing most antihypertensives except MRAs.
Immunocompromised adults ≥{{c1::19}} years should receive the 2-dose RZV series (lower age threshold than the general population).
extra: Notes — Zoster recombinant (RZV) immunocompromised, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Gadolinium-based contrast is contraindicated for nephrogenic systemic fibrosis (NSF) if GFR <{{c1::30 mL/min/1.73 m²}}, with caution if GFR {{c2::30–60 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
extra: KDIGO 2012 AKI — Section 4, NSF risk guidance

text: Routine serial spine radiographs (e.g., every {{c1::2 years}}) are {{c2::not recommended}} for monitoring axial SpA, despite detecting change in 20–35% of AS patients over that interval.
AKI Stage 3 is defined by SCr ≥{{c1::3×}} baseline, or SCr ≥{{c2::4.0 mg/dL}}, or initiation of RRT, or eGFR <35 mL/min/1.73 m² in patients <18 y.
For antianginal therapy in CCD, either a {{c1::beta blocker}} or {{c1::calcium channel blocker}} is acceptable as first-line treatment.
extra: Top 10 Take-Home Messages — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: eGFRcr-cys (creatinine + cystatin C) should be used when {{c1::greater accuracy is needed}} or when {{c2::eGFRcr is unreliable}} (1B).

In long-distance travelers at substantially increased VTE risk (e.g., recent surgery, prior VTE, active malignancy), preferred prophylaxis is {{c1::graduated compression stockings or prophylactic LMWH}}; {{c2::aspirin}} is used only if those are not feasible.
Didactic education {{c1::should NOT}} be relied upon as a standalone ASP strategy; it should only {{c2::complement}} active stewardship interventions (weak, low).

For ambulatory adults with mild-to-moderate COVID-19 at high risk of progression, IDSA strongly recommends {{c1::nirmatrelvir/ritonavir}} as first-line antiviral, started within {{c2::5 days}} of symptom onset.
In moderate-to-severe UC, do not use {{c1::corticosteroids, thiopurines, or methotrexate}} for induction or as monotherapy maintenance.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC

text: Five-year VTE recurrence risk after stopping anticoagulation is approximately {{c1::3%}} for surgically provoked, {{c2::15%}} for nonsurgical transient, and ~30% for unprovoked VTE.
The 2025 guideline recommends {{c1::potassium-based salt substitutes}} to lower BP, except in patients with {{c2::CKD}} or those taking {{c2::potassium-sparing drugs}}.
Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention increases intracranial bleeding with a Peto OR of {{c1::1.31}} (95% CI 1.11–1.54).
In ACS managed with PCI, the preferred P2Y12 inhibitors for DAPT are {{c1::ticagrelor or prasugrel}}, rather than {{c2::clopidogrel}}.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 4.3.2 (Table 7); Top Take-Home Messages

text: Preferred antihypertensive drug classes for secondary stroke/TIA prevention include {{c1::diuretics}}, {{c2::ACE inhibitors}}, and {{c2::ARBs}}.
For low-risk DTC requiring RAI remnant ablation, the recommended activity is {{c1::~30 mCi}} 131I.

Progressive pulmonary fibrosis (PPF) in non-IPF fibrotic ILD is defined as ≥{{c1::2}} of {{c1::3}} criteria (worsening symptoms, physiologic progression, or radiologic progression) occurring within {{c2::1 year}}, with no alternative explanation.
extra: Table 4 — Definition of Progressive Pulmonary Fibrosis

text: The Euro-Lupus cyclophosphamide induction regimen consists of {{c1::500 mg IV every 2 weeks for 6 doses}} (total {{c2::3 g}}).
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Treatment of Class III/IV LN; Thresholds & Doses

text: Tenecteplase {{c1::0.4 mg/kg}} is NOT recommended for AIS due to no added benefit and possible harm.
Initial oral prednisone for PMR should be within {{c1::12.5–25 mg/day}}, using the minimum effective dose.
In CCD with AF requiring anticoagulation post-PCI, use a {{c1::DOAC plus P2Y12 inhibitor}} (drop aspirin within 1-4 weeks); avoid {{c2::triple therapy}} beyond 30 days.
extra: Section 4.3.1 Antiplatelet Therapy — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Fluid restriction to <{{c1::1000 mL/day}} is recommended for moderate hyponatremia (serum Na 120–125 mEq/L) in cirrhosis.
To confirm UC diagnosis, perform full colonoscopy with {{c1::ileal intubation}} and biopsies of both {{c2::inflamed and endoscopically normal mucosa}} (including rectum).
extra: Diagnosis, Assessment, Monitoring, and Prognosis of UC — endoscopic diagnostic approach

text: CSPH can be excluded when LSM is <{{c1::15 kPa}} AND platelets are >{{c2::150 K/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: All patients with PAD should receive {{c1::high-intensity statin}} therapy targeting ≥{{c2::50%}} LDL-C reduction.
Primary therapy for cutaneous abscesses, furuncles, and carbuncles is {{c1::incision and drainage (I&D)}}; antibiotics are not required for simple abscess without {{c2::SIRS}}.
The 2-month regimen of {{c1::rifampin plus pyrazinamide}} must not be used for LTBI due to {{c2::unacceptable hepatotoxicity}} risk.
GOLD spirometric severity by FEV1 % predicted: GOLD 1 ≥{{c1::80%}}, GOLD 2 {{c2::50–79%}}, GOLD 3 {{c3::30–49%}}, GOLD 4 <{{c4::30%}}.

In active GPA/MPA with glomerulonephritis or alveolar hemorrhage, plasma exchange should {{c1::not be routinely added}} to remission induction; it is reserved for patients at {{c2::high risk of ESKD or anti-GBM overlap}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 2–4 (GPA/MPA); Table 2

text: Hypertriglyceridemia is considered an etiology of acute pancreatitis when serum triglycerides exceed {{c1::1,000 mg/dL}}.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Etiology; Thresholds & Doses

text: Initial fluid resuscitation in DKA/HHS uses 0.9% saline or balanced crystalloid at {{c1::500–1,000 mL/h}} for the first {{c2::2–4 hours}}, with the full deficit replaced over 24–48 h.
IDSA/SHEA recommends against {{c1::antibiotic cycling}} as an ASP strategy (weak, low).

AGA 2022 suggests preferential use of phentermine-topiramate ER in patients with comorbid {{c1::migraines}}, while avoiding it in {{c2::cardiovascular disease}}, uncontrolled hypertension, and pregnancy.
Treatment is indicated for HBeAg-negative CHB when HBV DNA is ≥{{c1::2,000}} IU/mL and ALT is ≥{{c2::2×}} ULN.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Figure 1 / Treatment Algorithms

text: Icosapent ethyl {{c1::2 g twice daily}} is recommended in patients with ASCVD and triglycerides {{c2::135–499 mg/dL}} with LDL-C 41–100 mg/dL on statin to reduce recurrent vascular events.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 4.3.2; REDUCE-IT trial

text: To avoid delay, the SSC 2021 recommends initiating vasopressors {{c1::peripherally}} (proximal to the antecubital fossa), then transitioning to central access when feasible.

Routine {{c1::continuous NMBA infusion}} is NOT recommended for moderate-to-severe ARDS; it is reserved for selected patients such as those with severe asynchrony, high transpulmonary pressures, or risk of pneumothorax.
extra: Domain 8 / ROSE and ACURASYS trial discussion — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: Rapid aortic growth is an indication for surgery in sporadic TAA if diameter increases ≥{{c1::0.5 cm}} in 1 year or ≥{{c1::0.3 cm}}/year over 2 consecutive years; threshold is ≥{{c1::0.3 cm}} in 1 year for HTAD or BAV.
RAI should be avoided in children <{{c1::5 years}} of age; it is acceptable for ages 5–10 years only if the calculated dose is <{{c2::10 mCi (<370 MBq)}}.
extra: Recommendations 58–72 (Sections O–R) — pediatric Graves' disease management

text: The therapeutic serum concentration target for digoxin in AF is {{c1::0.5–0.9 ng/mL}}; concentrations ≥{{c2::1.2 ng/mL}} are associated with increased mortality.
Once initiated, SGLT2 inhibitors should be continued even if eGFR falls below {{c1::20}} ml/min/1.73 m², until {{c2::dialysis or transplant}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Thresholds & Doses (SGLT2i)

text: Clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH) surrogates include platelets {{c1::<100,000/µL}}, varices, splenomegaly, or HVPG ≥{{c2::10 mmHg}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Surgical Resection / Figure 10

text: A primary-prevention ICD is indicated for LVEF {{c1::≤35%}} with NYHA class {{c2::II–III}} symptoms on GDMT.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Thresholds & Doses / ICD section

text: Upadacitinib maintenance: {{c1::15 or 30 mg daily}}; the {{c2::30 mg}} dose is preferred in TNF-experienced patients.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: Virologic failure is defined as inability to achieve or maintain HIV RNA {{c1::<200 copies/mL}}; isolated blips of {{c2::20–200 copies/mL}} do not require a regimen change.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 3 — management of virologic failure

text: Alteplase for AIS is dosed at 0.9 mg/kg IV (max 90 mg), with {{c1::10%}} given as a bolus over 1 minute and the remainder infused over {{c2::60 minutes}}.
TSH suppression target for high-risk DTC or structural incomplete response is {{c1::<0.1 mU/L}}.
The recommended screening test for primary aldosteronism (PA) is the {{c1::aldosterone-to-renin ratio (ARR)}}.
Awake prone positioning is suggested in non-intubated {{c1::COVID-19}} AHRF to reduce intubation risk; there is insufficient evidence to make this recommendation in {{c2::non-COVID}} AHRF.
Quality benchmarks for AIS include door-to-imaging ≤{{c1::25 minutes}} and door-in-door-out for transfers ≤{{c2::120 minutes}}.
Myxedema coma is treated with an IV LT4 loading dose of {{c1::200–400 μg}}, followed by maintenance at 1.6 μg/kg/d (×0.75 if IV).
In elderly patients and those with known CAD, LT4 should be initiated at {{c1::12.5–25 μg/d}} with gradual titration, rather than full replacement dose.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 4b; Thresholds & Doses — Initial LT4 in CAD/elderly

text: In thyroid storm, hydrocortisone is given as a {{c1::300 mg IV}} loading dose, then {{c2::100 mg every 8 hours}}.
extra: Recommendations 34–35, Tables 6–7 — thyroid storm multimodality treatment

text: For AS patients {{c1::<65 years}} or with life expectancy >20 years, {{c2::SAVR}} is preferred over TAVI.
HIV RNA should be checked {{c1::4–6 weeks}} after ART initiation, then every {{c2::3 months}} until suppressed, then every {{c3::6 months}} (every 12 months if stable >5 years).
Hyponatremia is defined as serum sodium {{c1::<136 mmol/L}}.
KDIGO 2021 RAS inhibitor recommendations apply to CKD stages {{c1::G1–G4}}; there are no RCT data to support their use in {{c2::CKD G5}}.
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendations 3.2.1–3.2.3 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: CMR with gadolinium is contraindicated when GFR is <{{c1::30 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
After IV thrombolysis for ischemic stroke, BP should be maintained at <{{c1::180/105 mm Hg}} for {{c2::24 hours}}.
Primary hypogonadism is distinguished from secondary hypogonadism by measuring {{c1::LH and FSH}}.
Tofacitinib maintenance dose is {{c1::5 or 10 mg BID}}; the {{c2::10 mg BID}} dose is preferred in TNF-experienced patients.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: Candidemia without metastatic complications should be treated for {{c1::14 days}} after documented bloodstream clearance and resolution of symptoms.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section I, Recommendations 1–12; Thresholds & Doses

text: Warfarin embryopathy risk is >{{c1::30%}} if dose >5 mg/d in the first trimester, but <{{c2::3%}} if dose ≤5 mg/d.
In cancer-associated thrombosis with luminal GI malignancy, CHEST 2021 prefers {{c1::apixaban or LMWH}} over edoxaban or rivaroxaban due to increased GI bleeding risk.
Periprocedural LR for ERCP: {{c1::3 mL/kg/hr}} during the procedure, then a {{c2::20 mL/kg}} bolus after, followed by 3 mL/kg/hr × 8 hours.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Preventing Post-ERCP Pancreatitis; Thresholds & Doses

text: Fibrinolytic therapy is indicated in STEMI if PPCI cannot be achieved within {{c1::120 minutes}} and symptom onset is <{{c2::12 hours}}.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 5.2.1; Top Take-Home Messages

text: In adult kidney transplant recipients, KDIGO 2021 targets BP {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}} (1C).
extra: Chapter 4, Recommendation 4.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Natalizumab PML risk at 25–36 months (no prior immunosuppression) with JCV antibody index >1.5 is approximately {{c1::3/1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 16

text: A Padua Prediction Score of ≥{{c1::4}} indicates high VTE risk (~11% incidence without prophylaxis) and is the threshold at which pharmacologic prophylaxis is indicated.

HBV reactivation in an HBsAg-positive patient is defined as a ≥{{c1::2 log}} rise in HBV DNA, or HBV DNA ≥{{c2::3 log}} IU/mL if previously undetectable, or ≥{{c3::4 log}} IU/mL if baseline was detectable.
KDIGO 2021 advises caution with mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (MRA) use when eGFR is {{c1::<45 ml/min/1.73 m²}} due to the risk of {{c2::hyperkalemia}}.
extra: Chapter 3, Thresholds & Doses — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Anti-GBM plasma exchange uses {{c1::40–50 ml/kg}} ideal body weight daily against {{c2::5% albumin}} until antibodies are undetectable (~14 days).
The standard adult dose of TAF for chronic hepatitis B is {{c1::25 mg}} PO daily; no dose adjustment is required unless CrCl is <{{c2::15}} mL/min.
In acute PE, systemic thrombolytics are reserved for hypotensive patients defined as SBP {{c1::<90 mmHg}} for ≥{{c2::15 minutes}} without high bleeding risk.
Mifepristone is used for hypercortisolism-related {{c1::diabetes or glucose intolerance}} in non-surgical candidates; cortisol {{c2::cannot guide dosing}}, so titration is clinical.
Severe hypercholesterolemia is defined as LDL-C ≥{{c1::190 mg/dL}}, non–HDL-C ≥{{c2::220 mg/dL}}, and/or apoB ≥{{c3::140 mg/dL}}.
6 months of daily isoniazid (6H) is {{c1::strongly}} recommended for HIV-negative persons and {{c2::conditionally}} recommended for HIV-positive persons unable to take a preferred regimen.
Pre-capillary PH requires: mPAP >20 mmHg + PAWP {{c1::≤15 mmHg}} + PVR {{c2::>2 WU}}.
extra: Section 3.1 Definitions, Table 5 — updated hemodynamic definitions of PH

text: To reduce CIED infection risk in high-risk IE patients, consider use of {{c1::antibacterial-impregnated envelopes (WRAP-IT)}} at the time of implantation.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Future Considerations (Ref 24)

text: HIV PEP consists of a {{c1::3-drug}} ART regimen taken for {{c2::28 days}}, initiated within {{c3::72 hours}} of substantial exposure.
Upadacitinib induction: {{c1::45 mg PO daily}} for {{c2::8 weeks}}.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: Patients with HFimpEF should {{c1::continue}} HFrEF GDMT because withdrawal leads to relapse in approximately {{c2::40%}} of cases.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: A key MDR risk factor for HAP/VAP is receipt of IV antibiotics within the prior {{c1::90 days}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Table 2, MDR risk factors

text: Multitarget stool DNA + FIT (sDNA-FIT) is performed every {{c1::1-3 years}} for colorectal cancer screening.
Glucocorticoid therapy for IgAN should be avoided if eGFR is <{{c1::30 ml/min/1.73 m²}} or BMI >{{c2::30}}.

If an oral bisphosphonate is not appropriate, the preferred sequence is: IV bisphosphonate → {{c1::teriparatide}} → {{c2::denosumab}}; raloxifene is reserved for postmenopausal women when no other agent is appropriate.
In acutely ill medical inpatients, {{c1::LMWH or fondaparinux}} is preferred over {{c2::UFH}} for pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis.
The 2019 ACR/AF guideline {{c1::strongly recommends}} exercise (walking, strengthening, neuromuscular, or aquatic) for hand, hip, and knee OA, with supervised programs being more effective.
extra: Table 1 / Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body approaches — exercise recommendation

text: In STEMI with multivessel disease, complete revascularization is recommended, preferably in a {{c1::single setting}}; culprit-only PCI is recommended in {{c2::cardiogenic shock}}.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 7.4.1; Top Take-Home Messages

text: Calcium-based phosphate binders should have their dose {{c1::restricted}} in {{c2::all}} adult CKD G3a–G5D patients receiving phosphate-lowering treatment, regardless of calcium or PTH level.
Greater confidence in a positive bronchodilator response is warranted when FEV₁ increases by ≥{{c1::15%}} and ≥{{c2::400 mL}}.
After LIMA-to-LAD, the preferred second conduit for CABG is the {{c1::radial artery}} (over saphenous vein) to improve patency, MACE, and survival.
extra: Section 12.2 / Table 12 — radial artery as preferred second conduit

text: In recurrent vasovagal syncope refractory to conservative measures, beta-blockers are a reasonable pharmacologic option in patients aged {{c1::≥42 years}}.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Section 5.1 and Figure 4

text: Controlled ileal-release budesonide {{c1::9 mg/day}} is used for induction in mild-to-moderate {{c2::ileocecal}} Crohn's disease.
In suspected celiac disease, the initial serologic test on a gluten-containing diet is {{c1::TTG IgA}} plus {{c2::total IgA}}.
Carotid sinus hypersensitivity is defined by a pause ≥{{c1::3 seconds}} and/or SBP drop ≥{{c2::50 mm Hg}} during carotid sinus massage.
LI-RADS category LR-5 carries a probability of HCC of {{c1::95–99%}}, while LR-4 carries approximately {{c2::75%}} and LR-3 approximately {{c3::30%}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Diagnosis section / LI-RADS algorithm

text: CHEST 2021 recommends against IVC filter placement in addition to anticoagulation; filters should be reserved for patients with {{c1::contraindications to anticoagulation}} and removed promptly once anticoagulation can begin.
Before ambulatory reflux monitoring off therapy (e.g., prior to antireflux surgery), PPIs should be stopped for {{c1::7 days}}.
extra: Reflux monitoring section — on vs off PPI testing

text: Per ATA 2015, a {{c1::pure cyst}} on ultrasound does not require FNA regardless of size.
In STEMI with multivessel disease, staged PCI of significant non-infarct arteries should be performed {{c1::in-hospital or within 45 days}} of the index event in stable patients.
extra: Section 5 / Figures 3-4 — STEMI non-infarct artery revascularization

text: When a patient presents with anaphylaxis and asthma simultaneously, give {{c1::IM (or intranasal) epinephrine}} FIRST, then {{c2::bronchodilator therapy}}.

After TNFi monotherapy failure, the preferred next step is to switch to a {{c1::different TNFi}}; switching drug class is recommended if there was {{c2::primary TNFi failure or a serious adverse event}}.
extra: Table 3 / Figure 5 — Recommendations after TNFi monotherapy failure

text: For recurrent ulcer bleeding after initial endoscopic hemostasis, the preferred next step is {{c1::repeat endoscopy}} rather than surgery or transcatheter arterial embolization.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Recurrent ulcer bleeding section / Table 8 (Lau 1999 RCT)

text: The AASLD/IDSA simplified HCV test-and-treat pathway is NOT appropriate for patients with prior {{c1::DAA}} exposure, {{c2::HBsAg positivity}}, {{c3::HCC}}, or decompensated cirrhosis.
extra: Initial Visit algorithm — exclusion criteria triggering specialist referral

text: Recommended exercise in NAFLD is ≥{{c1::150 minutes/week}} of moderate activity ({{c2::5}} sessions); vigorous exercise may be needed to improve NASH/fibrosis.
On ambulatory reflux monitoring, a Symptom Index (SI) is considered positive at ≥{{c1::50%}}, and a Symptom Association Probability (SAP) is positive at >{{c2::95%}}.
MRE is preferred over CTE in Crohn's patients who are {{c1::<35 years}} old or who will need {{c2::serial imaging}}, to avoid cumulative radiation.
The CDC-harmonized lower limit of normal total testosterone in healthy nonobese young men is {{c1::264 ng/dL}} ({{c1::9.2 nmol/L}}).
Oral anti-MRSA options for SSTI include {{c1::TMP-SMX}} 1–2 DS tabs BID, {{c1::doxycycline}} 100 mg BID, and {{c1::clindamycin}} 300–450 mg QID.
Rifaximin for IBS-D is dosed at {{c1::550 mg PO TID for 2 weeks}}; up to {{c2::2 retreatments}} are allowed for symptom recurrence (NNT 9).
extra: Recommendation on rifaximin — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: The minimum future liver remnant (FLR) for resection is {{c1::>30%}} in noncirrhotic patients and {{c2::>40%}} in cirrhotic patients.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Surgical Resection / Figure 10

text: {{c1::Fidaxomicin}} is NOT recommended for fulminant CDI.
Per ATA 2015 sonographic pattern-based FNA framework, the size threshold for FNA of a low-suspicion nodule is {{c1::≥1.5 cm}}, and for a very-low-suspicion nodule it is {{c2::≥2 cm}} (or observe).
For primary PCI, bivalirudin is dosed as a {{c1::0.75 mg/kg}} IV bolus followed by {{c2::1.75 mg/kg/h}} infusion, continued for 2–4 h post-PCI.
First-line treatment for mildly-to-moderately active proctitis or left-sided UC is {{c1::rectal 5-ASA}}; add {{c2::oral 5-ASA ≥2 g/day}} for left-sided disease.
extra: Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC — 5-ASA dosing

text: Pre-endoscopic erythromycin is given as {{c1::250 mg IV}} infused over {{c2::5–30 minutes}}, administered {{c2::20–90 minutes}} before endoscopy to improve visualization.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Pre-endoscopic medical therapy / Table 4

text: Vancomycin for MRSA SSTI is dosed at {{c1::30 mg/kg/day}} IV in 2 divided doses, targeting a trough of {{c2::15–20 µg/mL}} in severe infection.
In HF with AF, anticoagulation is indicated when CHA₂DS₂-VASc score is ≥{{c1::2}} in men or ≥{{c2::3}} in women, with {{c3::DOAC}} preferred over warfarin.
A resting ABI of {{c1::≤0.90}} is diagnostic of peripheral artery disease.
Fecal calprotectin <{{c1::50–60 μg/g}} suggests deep remission; <{{c2::187 μg/g}} predicts mucosal healing; >{{c3::321 μg/g}} in clinical remission predicts relapse.
extra: Diagnosis, Assessment, Monitoring, and Prognosis of UC

text: Dialysis is usually initiated at eGFR {{c1::5–10}} ml/min/1.73 m².
Adult inpatients with {{c1::severe}} CAP should receive systemic corticosteroids, EXCEPT when severe CAP is caused by {{c2::influenza}} (conditional recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 4

text: After achieving SVR in non-cirrhotic patients, HCV-related follow-up is only needed if there is ongoing reinfection risk, in which case {{c1::HCV RNA}} is repeated {{c2::yearly}}.
The guideline recommends against relying on {{c1::cuffless or smartwatch}} BP devices; validated {{c2::oscillometric upper-arm}} devices with averaged readings should be used.
Sick-day rules: patients should {{c1::double}} their oral glucocorticoid dose for fever >38°C and {{c2::triple}} it for fever >39°C until recovery.
Flecainide pill-in-pocket dosing: {{c1::200 mg}} if weight <70 kg; {{c2::300 mg}} if weight ≥70 kg, given as a single oral dose.
For minor AIS/high-risk TIA, clopidogrel is loaded at {{c1::300–600 mg}} then continued at {{c2::75 mg/day}} in combination with aspirin.
extra: Section 4.8 and Table 9 — DAPT regimen; Thresholds & Doses

text: Among risk-enhancing factors that may favor statin initiation, hs-CRP ≥{{c1::2.0 mg/L}}, Lp(a) ≥{{c2::50 mg/dL}} (or ≥125 nmol/L), and apoB ≥{{c3::130 mg/dL}} are key biomarker thresholds.
extra: Section 2.2 Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk; Table 3

text: For shock-refractory VF or pulseless VT, {{c1::IV amiodarone}} is preferred over {{c1::lidocaine}} as antiarrhythmic therapy.
extra: Section 6, Figure 2 — acute management of VT/VF

text: Adjuvant atezolizumab plus bevacizumab for {{c1::12 months}} may be offered after resection or ablation in high-risk patients (tumor >5 cm, >3 tumors, vascular invasion, or poor differentiation) based on the {{c2::IMbrave050}} trial.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Tables 7–8

text: Patients with vasovagal syncope occurring >6 episodes/year should {{c1::not drive}} until episodes resolve; those with 1–6 episodes/year require a {{c2::1-month}} symptom-free interval.
{{c1::DTG/3TC}} is the only recommended {{c2::2-drug}} initial ART regimen.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 2 — recommended initial ART regimens

text: KDIGO 2021 recommends restricting dietary sodium to {{c1::<2 g/day}} (<90 mmol/day; <5 g NaCl/day) in patients with high BP and CKD, except in {{c2::salt-wasting nephropathy}} (2C).
extra: Chapter 2, Recommendation 2.1.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: US H. pylori levofloxacin resistance is approximately {{c1::~40%}}, necessitating documented susceptibility before use.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Antibiotic susceptibility testing; Thresholds & Doses

text: Acute hepatitis B should be treated with entecavir, TDF, or TAF only if the patient develops {{c1::acute liver failure}} or a protracted severe course (bilirubin >{{c2::3 mg/dL}}, INR >{{c3::1.5}}, encephalopathy, or ascites); peg-IFN is contraindicated.
Worsening CKD-related anemia in SCD is treated with combined {{c1::hydroxyurea}} and {{c2::erythropoiesis-stimulating agents}}, particularly when both hemoglobin and absolute reticulocyte count fall.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Hydroxyurea and Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents

text: The 4R regimen uses rifampin daily for 4 months (120 doses): adults {{c1::10 mg/kg}}, children {{c2::15–20 mg/kg}}, max 600 mg.
Diagnostic paracentesis fluid should be sent for {{c1::PMN count}}, {{c2::total protein and albumin (SAAG)}}, and bedside inoculation into {{c3::blood culture bottles}} before starting antibiotics.
{{c1::Pioglitazone}} may be considered for NASH in patients with {{c2::T2DM}}.
Natalizumab PML risk at 25–36 months (no prior immunosuppression) with JCV antibody index ≤0.9 is approximately {{c1::0.2/1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 16

text: In the Thornhill 2022 English hinge-model study (n=14,731 IE cases), the highest OR for IE following a GI/respiratory NDIP was for {{c1::bronchoscopy}} (OR {{c2::1.33}}).
extra: Thornhill et al 2022 (Ref 4) / Table 3 — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Adults aged ≥60: USPSTF 2022 assigns {{c1::Grade D}} — do {{c2::not initiate}} low-dose aspirin for primary prevention of CVD.
The 2025 AHA/ACC guideline sets a universal BP treatment goal of {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}} for all adults with hypertension, including those with CVD, diabetes, or CKD.
extra: Section 5.2.7 — BP goal for treated hypertension; Top Take-Home Message #4

text: For remission induction in severe GPA/MPA, a {{c1::reduced-dose}} glucocorticoid regimen is preferred over a standard-dose regimen.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 2–4 (GPA/MPA); Table 2

text: TSH suppression target for intermediate-risk DTC or biochemical incomplete response is {{c1::0.1–0.5 mU/L}}.
Alosetron ({{c1::0.5–1 mg PO BID}}) is indicated for {{c2::women with severe chronic (>6 months) refractory IBS-D}} under a REMS program.
extra: Recommendation on alosetron — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: By transient elastography, an LSM of {{c1::<10 kPa}} rules OUT cACLD (compensated advanced chronic liver disease).
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Stages of cirrhosis; LSM cutoffs

text: After catheter ablation for AF, oral anticoagulation should be continued for ≥{{c1::2 months}} regardless of CHA2DS2-VASc score.
In PMR, the initial taper target is {{c1::10 mg/day}} prednisone equivalent, reached within {{c2::4–8 weeks}} of starting therapy.
In adults, 30-day mortality with a restrictive vs liberal RBC transfusion strategy is equivalent: RR {{c1::1.00}} (95% CI 0.86–1.16), with a baseline mortality of {{c2::8.3%}}.
extra: AABB 2023 — Table 2, adult summary of findings

text: High-risk features warranting postoperative Crohn's prophylaxis include {{c1::smoking}}, {{c2::penetrating disease}}, and {{c2::≥2 prior resections}}.
extra: Postoperative Crohn's Disease — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: After discharge from a DKA/HHS episode, follow-up should occur within {{c1::2–4 weeks}} to prevent recurrence.
In membranous nephropathy, a positive {{c1::anti-PLA2R}} serology allows diagnosis without kidney biopsy in nephrotic patients.
The prevalence of celiac disease in first-degree relatives is {{c1::5-20%}}, with the highest rate seen in {{c2::siblings}}.
Beta-adrenergic blockade is recommended in all symptomatic thyrotoxic patients, especially those with HR >{{c1::90}} bpm or cardiovascular disease.
In Marfan syndrome, aortic root replacement is indicated at ≥{{c1::5.0 cm}}, or at ≥{{c2::4.5 cm}} with family history of dissection, rapid growth ≥0.3 cm/year, or before planned pregnancy.
extra: Section 6.1.2; Section 8 — 2022 ACC/AHA Aortic Disease Guideline

text: Edoxaban dose is reduced to 30 mg daily if CrCl {{c1::15–50 mL/min}}, weight {{c2::≤60 kg}}, or concomitant strong P-gp inhibitor.
Prescription icosapent ethyl {{c1::4 g/day}} is supported for CCD patients with triglycerides {{c2::150-499 mg/dL}} already on statin therapy (REDUCE-IT).
extra: Section 4.2.6 / Thresholds & Doses — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: For extended-phase anticoagulation, the reduced-dose regimen for apixaban is {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}}.
Sustained CCB response in PAH is confirmed at 3–6 months by WHO-FC {{c1::I/II}}, mPAP <{{c2::30 mmHg}}, and PVR <{{c2::4 WU}}.
extra: Recommendation Table 7 — vasoreactivity testing and CCB therapy criteria

text: Preferred imaging modality for pyomyositis is {{c1::MRI}}; empiric therapy is {{c2::vancomycin}} with drainage, transitioning to cefazolin or antistaphylococcal penicillin once MSSA is confirmed.
MRSA HAP/VAP should be treated with {{c1::vancomycin}} or {{c1::linezolid}}; {{c2::tigecycline}} should not be used for Acinetobacter HAP/VAP.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Sections XV–XX, pathogen-specific therapy

text: Before RAI administration following thyroid hormone withdrawal, the target TSH level is {{c1::>30 mIU/L}}; LT4 should be withheld for {{c2::3–4 weeks}} (LT3 substitution acceptable for the first 2 weeks).
Vasoactive therapy (e.g., octreotide, somatostatin, or terlipressin) should be continued for {{c1::2–5 days}} after acute variceal hemorrhage.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Table 5

text: Pulmonary rehabilitation should be initiated within {{c1::4 weeks}} of hospital discharge for exacerbation to reduce readmission and mortality.
After completing anabolic therapy (romosozumab or teriparatide) for osteoporosis, patients should be transitioned to {{c1::a bisphosphonate}}.
In BAV patients, replace the ascending aorta when diameter ≥{{c1::5.5 cm}}, or ≥{{c2::5.0 cm}} with risk factors (family history of dissection, growth ≥0.5 cm/y, coarctation).
The 2022 ESC/ERS guideline defines pulmonary hypertension as mPAP {{c1::>20 mmHg}} at rest (lowered from the prior >25 mmHg threshold).
extra: Section 3.1 Definitions, Table 5 — updated hemodynamic definitions of PH

text: When chlamydia has not been excluded in a patient treated for gonorrhea, add {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg BID × 7 days}}.
extra: Treatment of Gonococcal Infections, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Free testosterone measurement is indicated when total testosterone falls in the borderline zone of approximately {{c1::200–400 ng/dL}} or when SHBG is altered.
IDSA suggests against remdesivir in critically ill COVID-19 patients on {{c1::invasive mechanical ventilation or ECMO}}.
extra: Remdesivir for Severe or Critical COVID-19

text: In asymptomatic adults with IDA and plausible celiac disease, the AGA recommends obtaining {{c1::celiac serology}} first and pursuing small-bowel biopsy only if serology is positive, rather than performing routine duodenal biopsy (conditional, very low quality).
After bilateral adrenalectomy, Nelson's syndrome is monitored with annual {{c1::ACTH levels}} and {{c1::pituitary MRI}}.
Severe DKA is defined as pH {{c1::<7.0}}, bicarbonate {{c2::<10 mmol/L}}, or β-OHB >6.0 mmol/L.

Pemivibart pre-exposure prophylaxis is suggested for {{c1::moderately/severely immunocompromised}} patients ≥{{c2::12 years}}: {{c3::4,500 mg IV every 3 months}} when circulating variants remain susceptible.
In pregnant women with mechanical valves on LMWH, target anti-Xa peak of {{c1::0.8–1.2 U/mL}} measured {{c2::4–6 hours}} post-dose with weekly monitoring is required.
Low-dose {{c1::dopamine}} should not be used to prevent or treat AKI (grade {{c2::1A}}).
For atrial-based pacing in SND, {{c1::atrial or dual-chamber pacing}} is preferred over single-chamber ventricular pacing to reduce {{c2::AF and pacemaker syndrome}}.
extra: Section 5.4.4/Figure 6 — chronic SND management algorithm and pacing indications

text: In febrile neutropenia with SSTI, add empiric antifungal therapy if fever persists for {{c1::4–7 days}}.
Lymphocytic duodenosis is defined as ≥{{c1::25}} intraepithelial lymphocytes per {{c2::100}} epithelial cells.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diagnosis: Evidence and rationale

text: In mild acute pancreatitis, oral feeding with a {{c1::low-fat, low-residue soft diet}} should begin {{c2::as soon as tolerated}}, without requiring pain resolution or normalized enzyme levels.

Routine {{c1::pulmonary function testing}} is not recommended in asymptomatic SCD patients; comprehensive PFT (spirometry, DLCO, lung volumes) is reserved for symptomatic patients or those with recurrent {{c2::acute chest syndrome}} or PE.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Function Screening

text: IVT should NOT be given for {{c1::mild, non-disabling}} AIS deficits within 4.5 hours; use {{c2::DAPT (clopidogrel + aspirin)}} instead.
extra: Key Recommendations — IVT vs DAPT for minor stroke

text: Erenumab should be used cautiously in patients with {{c1::latex allergy, constipation, or preexisting hypertension}} due to reported severe constipation and blood pressure elevation.
extra: AHS 2021 — Preventive Treatment: Developing Treatment Plans

text: Before initiating PAH-specific therapy in SCD, {{c1::right-heart catheterization}} is required alongside a multidisciplinary approach.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — PAH Treatment / Figure 2

text: Patients with IPF {{c1::should not}} be referred for antireflux surgery solely to improve respiratory outcomes (conditional, very low quality).
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of IPF — antireflux surgery conditional recommendation against

text: In SCD, a first provoked VTE should be anticoagulated for a defined period of {{c1::3–6 months}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — VTE Management table

text: The target serum digoxin level in HFrEF is {{c1::0.5 to <0.9 ng/mL}}; dose is reduced with age >70, renal impairment, or low lean body mass.
For mild-to-moderate ICH with presenting SBP 150–220 mm Hg, lower SBP to a target of {{c1::130–140 mm Hg}} with smooth, sustained control.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.1 (Acute BP Lowering); INTERACT2/ATACH-2

text: Vancomycin dosing for HAP/VAP: standard {{c1::15 mg/kg IV q8–12h}} targeting a trough of {{c2::15–20 mg/mL}}; a loading dose of {{c3::25–30 mg/kg}} is used for severe illness.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3, empiric VAP doses

text: In hospitalized UGIB patients with pre-existing cardiovascular disease, consider RBC transfusion at hemoglobin {{c1::<8 g/dL}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Red blood cell transfusion section / Table 3

text: For RAI-refractory, progressive, symptomatic metastatic DTC, first-line kinase inhibitor options include {{c1::sorafenib}} or {{c1::lenvatinib}}.
Testosterone enanthate or cypionate is dosed at {{c1::150–200 mg IM every 2 weeks}} or {{c1::75–100 mg IM weekly}}.

Preferred vascular access order for RRT dialysis catheters: 1st {{c1::right internal jugular}}; 2nd {{c2::femoral}}; 3rd {{c3::left internal jugular}}; last (avoid) {{c4::subclavian}} (dominant side).
CI-AKI prevention requires IV volume expansion with {{c1::isotonic saline or sodium bicarbonate}} (1A); oral fluids alone are {{c2::insufficient}} (1C).
For nonpurulent cellulitis without systemic signs, use an agent active against {{c1::streptococci}} (e.g., penicillin, cephalexin, dicloxacillin); routine {{c2::MRSA}} coverage is NOT required.
When invasive cultures are obtained, antibiotic withholding thresholds are: PSB <{{c1::10³ CFU/mL}} or BAL <{{c1::10⁴ CFU/mL}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Thresholds & Doses, invasive culture thresholds

text: Fluid restriction is indicated in cirrhotic ascites only if serum sodium is ≤{{c1::125 mmol/L}}.
ACP 2023 recommends against using {{c1::estrogen alone, estrogen plus progestin, or raloxifene (a SERM)}} to treat osteoporosis in postmenopausal females.
Tegaserod 6 mg PO BID is indicated for IBS-C in women {{c1::<65 years with ≤1 CV risk factor}}; it is contraindicated in patients with {{c2::>1 CV risk factor or a prior ischemic event}}.
extra: Recommendations on IBS-C secretagogues — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: The rate of severe systemic drug reaction requiring hospitalization with the 3HP regimen is approximately {{c1::0.1%}}.
Anticoagulation for LV thrombus post-MI is typically continued for {{c1::3 months}}, followed by reimaging.
In TAK, glucocorticoids may be tapered off after ≥{{c1::6–12 months}} of sustained remission.
Total/near-total thyroidectomy is indicated for DTC with any of the following: tumor {{c1::>4 cm}}, gross extrathyroidal extension, clinical N1, or {{c2::M1 (distant metastases)}}.
Pathologic esophageal acid exposure time (AET) is defined as {{c1::>4%}}; an AET {{c2::>12%}} strongly predicts surgical success in extraesophageal GERD.
extra: Reflux monitoring section; Extraesophageal GERD section / Figure 2

text: First-line treatment for Cushing's disease is {{c1::transsphenoidal adenomectomy (TSS)}}.
extra: Recommendation 3.1a–3.1c — First-line surgical approach by etiology

text: For intermediate-risk patients with no known CAD, CCTA is preferred in patients {{c1::<65 years}} not on optimal preventive therapy, while stress imaging is preferred in patients {{c2::≥65 years}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 4.1.2.1, Figure 9

text: Nirmatrelvir/ritonavir for eGFR >60 mL/min: {{c1::300 mg/100 mg}} PO every 12 hours × 5 days.
MART for children 6–11 years uses budesonide-formoterol {{c1::80/4.5 mcg}} (100/6 metered); seek care if >{{c2::8}} inhalations in 24 hours.
For isolated subsegmental PE without proximal DVT and {{c1::low recurrence risk}}, CHEST 2021 recommends clinical surveillance over anticoagulation.
Modeling estimates that screening biennially from age 40-74 (vs. 50-74) averts approximately {{c1::1.3}} additional breast cancer deaths per 1,000 women over a lifetime.
extra: Table 2 — modeling estimates of benefits/harms biennial 40-74 vs 50-74

text: The recommended adult prednisone dose for initial ITP therapy is {{c1::0.5–2 mg/kg/day}}.
extra: Recommendation 4 — prednisone vs dexamethasone dosing in adults

text: In biologic-naive moderate-to-severe UC, {{c1::infliximab + azathioprine}} combination is preferred over monotherapy.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC

text: Rivaroxaban dosing for HITT: {{c1::15 mg BID}} for the first 3 weeks, then {{c2::20 mg daily}}; for isolated HIT: 15 mg BID until platelet recovery.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: CHEST 2021 supports outpatient treatment of low-risk PE when home circumstances permit; low risk is defined as original PESI {{c1::<85}} or simplified PESI {{c1::= 0}}.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #14; Thresholds & Doses

text: In COVID-19 acute hypoxemic respiratory failure, {{c1::CPAP/NIV}} may be considered instead of HFNO to further reduce intubation risk; no equivalent recommendation exists for {{c2::unselected non-COVID AHRF}}.
For NDIPs in high-risk IE patients, the preferred prevention strategy is {{c1::strict sterility and infection-prevention practices}} rather than repeated or long-term antibiotic prophylaxis.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Future Considerations

text: Dietary sodium restriction in glomerular disease is recommended at <{{c1::2.0 g/d}} (<90 mmol/d).
Treat cellulitis for {{c1::5 days}}, extending only if the infection is not improving.
CT aortic valve calcium score thresholds for severe AS: ≥{{c1::1300}} AU in women and ≥{{c2::2000}} AU in men.
AGA 2022 suggests preferential use of naltrexone-bupropion ER in patients with {{c1::depression}} or those attempting {{c2::smoking cessation}}.
In adults ≤65, SSRI use is associated with a suicide attempt rate of ~{{c1::0.7%}} vs ~{{c1::0.3%}} with placebo (OR 1.53).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Harms — suicide attempt OR 1.53 in adults ≤65

text: Medication-overuse headache is defined by use of triptans, ergots, opioids, or combination analgesics on ≥{{c1::10}} days/month, or nonopioid analgesics/NSAIDs/acetaminophen on ≥{{c2::15}} days/month.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 5: ICHD-3 criteria for medication-overuse headache

text: TBLC complication rates include pneumothorax in approximately {{c1::9%}} and any bleeding in approximately {{c2::30%}} of cases.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF — TBLC complication rates

text: The ALT upper limit of normal used for HBV management decisions is {{c1::35}} U/L in men and {{c2::25}} U/L in women.
The loading dose of fosphenytoin/phenytoin for status epilepticus is {{c1::20 mg PE/kg}} IV, with an additional {{c2::5–10 mg/kg}} allowed if needed.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Urgent control therapy section; Thresholds & Doses

text: {{c1::High-resolution manometry (HRM)}} must be performed before any antireflux surgery or endoscopic procedure to exclude {{c2::achalasia and absent contractility}}.
Mifepristone for Cushing's syndrome is started at {{c1::300 mg/day}} and titrated up to a maximum of {{c2::1200 mg/day}} by clinical response.
extra: Table 1 — Medical treatment of CS: agents, doses, adverse effects

text: In PAD, if LDL-C remains ≥{{c1::70 mg/dL}} on maximally tolerated statin, add {{c2::ezetimibe or a PCSK9 inhibitor}}.
extra: Section 5.2, Table 11 — lipid-lowering with LDL-C targets

text: Dupilumab (anti-IL4Rα) for severe asthma (≥12 years) is dosed at {{c1::200 or 300 mg SC every 2 weeks}}; for ages 6–11, dosing is {{c2::weight-based}}.
In IBS-D, serologic testing to rule out celiac disease should include {{c1::IgA tTG}} and {{c2::quantitative IgA}}.
extra: Recommendation on celiac screening — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Gut-directed psychotherapy options recommended as adjuncts for IBS across subtypes include {{c1::cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)}} and {{c2::gut-directed hypnotherapy}}.
extra: Recommendation on TCAs and gut-directed psychotherapy — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: {{c1::Olezarsen}} 80 mg SC monthly is used for {{c2::familial chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS)}}.
extra: Section 4.2.9 — Hypertriglyceridemia management including olezarsen for FCS

text: Very high-dose GC use is defined as prednisone ≥{{c1::30 mg/day}} with a cumulative dose ≥{{c2::5 gm}} in 1 year.
KDIGO 2022 recommends an individualized HbA1c target of {{c1::<6.5% to <8.0%}} in non-dialysis CKD, based on severity, comorbidities, life expectancy, and hypoglycemia risk (1C).
First-line treatment for chronic SIAD is fluid restriction to {{c1::<1 L/day}}, tightened to {{c2::<500 mL/day}} if the urine-to-serum electrolyte ratio is <1.
extra: Treatment of Chronic Hyponatremia section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017

text: Remission in ITP is defined as a platelet count >{{c1::100 ×10⁹/L}} sustained at {{c2::12 months}} after diagnosis.
extra: Table 3 — definitions of ITP phases and response criteria

text: In the 3HP regimen, the adult isoniazid dose is {{c1::15 mg/kg}} (rounded to nearest 50/100 mg, max {{c2::900 mg}}).
The 2019 ACC/AHA guideline recommends ≥{{c1::150}} min/week of moderate-intensity OR ≥{{c2::75}} min/week of vigorous-intensity aerobic activity for primary prevention.
extra: Section 3.2 Exercise and Physical Activity; Table 4

text: Rivaroxaban is dosed at {{c1::20 mg daily}} for CrCl >50 mL/min and {{c2::15 mg daily}} for CrCl 15–50 mL/min, taken with the {{c3::largest meal}}.
Pregnancy is {{c1::strongly advised against}} in PAH; if pregnancy occurs, {{c2::ERAs, riociguat, and selexipag}} must be stopped.
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10 — PAH treatment algorithm; Section 2.1 'What is new'

text: Glucocorticoid-sparing agents recommended for frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent nephrotic syndrome in children include {{c1::cyclophosphamide, levamisole, MMF, rituximab, or CNI}}.
AKI Stage 1: SCr {{c1::1.5–1.9×}} baseline or ≥{{c2::0.3 mg/dL}} increase; UO <0.5 mL/kg/h for {{c3::6–12 h}}.
Eptinezumab is administered at {{c1::100 or 300 mg IV}} every {{c2::3 months}}.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 6 / Table 7: CGRP monoclonal antibody dosing

text: Standard epinephrine dosing in cardiac arrest is {{c1::1 mg IV/IO every 3–5 minutes}}; high-dose epinephrine is {{c2::not beneficial}}.
extra: Section 5.1, Table 7 — epinephrine in cardiac arrest

text: Primary prevention ICD is indicated in nonischemic cardiomyopathy with LVEF ≤{{c1::35%}} and NYHA class {{c2::II–III}} HF after ≥3 months of optimized GDMT.
extra: Section 7.2.1–7.2.2, Figure 6 — ICD in nonischemic cardiomyopathy

text: If LDL-C remains ≥70 mg/dL on high-intensity statin after atherosclerotic stroke/TIA, add {{c1::ezetimibe}} first, then a {{c2::PCSK9 inhibitor}} if still uncontrolled.
ADA recommendation grades reflect the strength of {{c1::evidence}}, NOT the strength of {{c1::recommendation}}.
extra: Grading of Evidence and Recommendation Development
