text: Active Class III/IV lupus nephritis is treated with glucocorticoids plus either {{c1::low-dose IV cyclophosphamide (Euro-Lupus)}} or {{c1::mycophenolic acid (MPAA)}}.
High-intensity statin therapy is recommended in HIV when 10-year ASCVD risk is >{{c1::20%}} or LDL is ≥{{c2::190 mg/dL}}.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 4 — ASCVD risk and statin recommendations

text: Mohee 2014 reported an OR of {{c1::8.21}} for {{c2::enterococcal}} IE following transurethral endoscopic procedures.
extra: Mohee 2014 — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory, Future Considerations

text: High-intensity statin options for secondary stroke prevention are {{c1::atorvastatin 80 mg}} daily or {{c2::rosuvastatin 20 mg}} daily.
Low-dose aspirin should be prescribed starting at {{c1::12 weeks gestation}} in pregnant individuals with hypertension to reduce the risk of {{c2::preeclampsia}}.
A mechanical valve is favored if age <{{c1::50}} years; a bioprosthetic valve is favored if age >{{c2::65}} years; shared decision-making applies for ages {{c3::50–65}} years.
Suppressive therapy for recurrent bacterial vaginosis consists of {{c1::metronidazole gel 0.75% twice weekly for ≥3 months}}.
extra: Bacterial Vaginosis, Recommended Regimens, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: IL-17 inhibitors (secukinumab, ixekizumab) are avoided in axial SpA with coexisting IBD because they can {{c1::worsen IBD}}.
A TBI of {{c1::≤0.70}} is abnormal; an absolute toe pressure of <{{c2::30}} mm Hg reflects severe ischemia.
Bariatric surgery criteria: BMI ≥{{c1::40}} kg/m² OR ≥{{c2::35}} kg/m² with comorbidities; NASH is increasingly accepted as a qualifying comorbidity.
Vonoprazan-amoxicillin dual therapy for H. pylori: vonoprazan {{c1::20 mg BID}} + amoxicillin {{c2::1,000 mg TID}} for 14 days.
Immunocompromised adults ≥{{c1::19}} years should receive RZV as a {{c2::2-dose}} series.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Zoster; Table 2, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: TBLC is relatively contraindicated when FVC is {{c1::<50%}} predicted.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF — TBLC relative contraindications

text: AKI is diagnosed when serum creatinine rises ≥{{c1::0.3 mg/dL}} within {{c2::48 hours}}.
Predictors of fluid restriction failure in SIAD are urine sodium {{c1::≥130 mmol/L}} or urine osmolality {{c2::≥500 mOsm/kg}}.
extra: Treatment of Chronic Hyponatremia section (Winzeler predictors) — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017

text: In treatment-naive PsA, {{c1::TNFi}} is preferred over IL-17i or IL-12/23i; however, IL-17i or IL-12/23i is preferred when {{c2::severe psoriasis is present or TNFi is contraindicated}}.
extra: Table 1 / Figure 3 — Recommendations for OSM- and treatment-naive active PsA

text: In hospitalized patients with severe hypertension (>180/120 mm Hg) and NO acute target organ damage, the guideline recommends against {{c1::intermittent IV or PO antihypertensives}}; these patients should be managed as {{c2::outpatients}}.
For ages 40–75 with a 10-year ASCVD risk of 5%–<20%, recommended moderate-intensity statins include {{c1::pitavastatin 4 mg}}, {{c1::atorvastatin 20 mg}}, or {{c1::rosuvastatin 10 mg}} daily.
Patients with diabetes and CKD should restrict dietary sodium to <{{c1::2 g/day}} (<{{c2::5 g}} NaCl/day) (2C).
Mild DKA is defined as pH {{c1::7.25–7.30}} or bicarbonate {{c2::15–18 mmol/L}}, with β-OHB 3.0–6.0 mmol/L.
HRS-AKI treatment response is defined as creatinine falling to {{c1::<1.5 mg/dL}} or within {{c2::0.3 mg/dL}} of baseline within 14 days.
For beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker overdose causing bradycardia, treatment includes {{c1::IV calcium}}, {{c1::glucagon}}, and {{c1::high-dose insulin}} therapy.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: An adequate NSAID trial before escalating to a TNFi requires ≥{{c1::2}} different NSAIDs at maximal doses over {{c2::1 month}}, OR incomplete response to ≥2 NSAIDs over {{c2::2 months}}.
The NELSON trial demonstrated an incidence rate ratio of {{c1::0.75}} for lung cancer mortality at 10 years with LDCT screening vs. no screening.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Benefits — NELSON IRR 0.75 at 10 years

text: Eluxadoline is dosed at {{c1::100 mg PO BID}}; a reduced dose of {{c2::75 mg BID}} is used in patients without a gallbladder or with hepatic impairment.
extra: Recommendation on eluxadoline — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Bezlotoxumab is dosed at {{c1::10 mg/kg IV}} as a single {{c2::60-minute}} infusion during the SOC antibiotic course.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: HFimpEF is defined as a previous LVEF {{c1::≤40%}} with follow-up measurement {{c1::>40%}}.
Due to poor specificity of HerpeSelect EIA at low index values, HSV-2 serologic diagnosis requires {{c1::two-step type-specific testing}} with confirmatory Biokit or Western blot.
extra: Diseases Characterized by Genital Ulcers — Genital Herpes, two-step HSV-2 serologic confirmation, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: In thyroid storm, PTU is dosed as a {{c1::500–1000 mg}} loading dose, then {{c2::250 mg every 4 hours}}.
extra: Recommendations 34–35, Tables 6–7 — thyroid storm multimodality treatment

text: After ACS on maximally tolerated high-intensity statin, a nonstatin agent (ezetimibe and/or PCSK9 inhibitor) should be added if LDL-C remains ≥{{c1::70 mg/dL}}; further intensification is reasonable at LDL-C {{c1::55 to <70 mg/dL}}.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 4.5 (Figure 5, Tables 11–12); Top Take-Home Messages

text: Clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH) is defined as HVPG ≥{{c1::10 mm Hg}}.
CHEST 2021 recommends inserting an IVC filter only when {{c1::anticoagulation is contraindicated}} in acute VTE, and removing it {{c2::promptly once anticoagulation can be started}}.
After ACEi/ARB initiation or dose change in CKD, BP, creatinine, and K⁺ should be rechecked within {{c1::2–4 weeks}}; the drug should be continued unless creatinine rises >{{c2::30%}} within 4 weeks.
In adults with newly diagnosed ITP who are asymptomatic or have minor mucocutaneous bleeding, treat with corticosteroids if platelet count is {{c1::<30 ×10⁹/L}}.
extra: Recommendations 1a–1b — corticosteroids vs observation thresholds in adults

text: Primary prevention ICD is indicated in ischemic cardiomyopathy with LVEF {{c1::≤35%}} and NYHA class {{c2::II–III}} HF on GDMT ≥3 months and ≥40 days post-MI.
extra: Section 7.1.2, Figure 3–4 — primary prevention ICD in ischemic cardiomyopathy

text: Dietary sodium should be restricted to <{{c1::2 g/d}} (<90 mmol/d) in CKD.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Thresholds & Doses (sodium)

text: USPSTF 2018 estimates that {{c1::20–50%}} of screen-detected prostate cancers represent overdiagnosis.
extra: Discussion: Potential Harms of Screening — USPSTF 2018

text: FIT (fecal immunochemical test) is performed every {{c1::1 year}} for colorectal cancer screening, with a positivity cutoff of {{c2::20 μg hemoglobin/g feces}} in CISNET modeling.
extra: Table 1 — Characteristics of recommended screening strategies

text: Low-risk hemodynamic targets include RAP <{{c1::8 mmHg}}, CI ≥{{c2::2.5 L/min/m²}}, SVI >38 mL/m², and SvO2 >65%.
extra: Section 6.2.7 / Tables 16 and 18 — four-strata risk assessment model

text: FNA of a suspicious cervical lymph node is indicated when the smallest diameter is ≥{{c1::8–10 mm}} (central) or ≥{{c2::10 mm}} (lateral), when the result would change management.
For USPSTF 2023 depression screening, the postpartum period is defined as the first {{c1::12 months}} after delivery.
extra: Practice Considerations: Patient Population; Thresholds & Doses

text: Severe asthma exacerbation criteria include RR >{{c1::30}}/min in adults or >{{c2::40}}/min in children ≤5, SpO₂ <92%, PEF <50% predicted, or inability to speak in sentences.
High-intensity statin therapy consists of {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} or {{c2::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}}, targeting ≥50% LDL-C reduction.
For active PsA despite an OSM, the preferred next step is to switch to a {{c1::TNFi biologic}} rather than another OSM, IL-17i, IL-12/23i, abatacept, or tofacitinib.
extra: Table 2 / Figure 4 — Recommendations after inadequate response to an OSM

text: For GERD in pregnancy, management begins with {{c1::lifestyle measures}}, then {{c2::antacids, alginates, or sucralfate}}; H2RAs and PPIs (except omeprazole) are category B.
First-degree relatives of patients with aortic root/ascending aneurysm or aortic dissection should be screened with {{c1::aortic imaging}}, regardless of age.
extra: Top 10 Take-Home Messages — 2022 ACC/AHA Aortic Disease Guideline

text: Fecal calprotectin >{{c1::100 µg/g}} predicts postoperative endoscopic recurrence in Crohn's disease (sensitivity 89%).

In females aged {{c1::≥65}} years with low bone mass (osteopenia), the decision to initiate pharmacologic treatment should be {{c2::individualized}} based on patient preferences, fracture risk, benefits/harms, and costs.
Remission maintenance in AAV with rituximab or azathioprine plus low-dose glucocorticoids is continued for {{c1::18 months to 4 years}}.

After initiating or adjusting lipid-lowering therapy, recheck the lipid profile in {{c1::4–12 weeks}}, then every {{c2::6–12 months}}; routine CK and LFT monitoring are {{c3::not indicated}} absent symptoms.
In HFpEF, routine use of {{c1::nitrates}} and {{c1::PDE-5 inhibitors}} is Class 3 (no benefit / harm) and should be avoided.
Clinical volume assessment has a sensitivity of {{c1::50–80%}} and specificity of {{c2::30–50%}} for determining volume status in hyponatremia, supporting laboratory-first diagnostic algorithms.
Antibiotics for COPD exacerbations are prescribed for {{c1::5 days}} when sputum is purulent with ≥2 cardinal symptoms or mechanical ventilation is required.
Anidulafungin dosing for invasive candidiasis: {{c1::200 mg}} loading dose, then {{c2::100 mg}} IV daily.
For monitoring TAK, {{c1::noninvasive imaging (CTA, MRA, or PET)}} is preferred over {{c2::catheter angiography}}.
extra: Table 5 — TAK clinical/laboratory monitoring and vascular imaging recommendations

text: In an intermediate-risk adult with uncertain LLT decision, a CAC score of {{c1::0 AU}} supports {{c2::deferring statin therapy and repeating in 3–7 years}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.6 — CAC scoring thresholds and management

text: For low-risk DTC patients with an excellent response to therapy, the TSH target is {{c1::0.5–2 mU/L}} (low-normal range).
When initiating aspirin for primary prevention, USPSTF 2022 recommends a dose of {{c1::81 mg/day}}.
Four-strata PAH risk 6MWD cutoffs (low → high risk) are: >{{c1::440}}, {{c1::320–440}}, {{c1::165–319}}, <{{c1::165}} metres.
extra: Section 6.2.7 / Tables 16 and 18 — four-strata risk assessment model

text: Primary hrHPV testing as a stand-alone screening option is only recommended for women aged {{c1::30}} and older, because younger women have high rates of {{c2::transient HPV infection}} leading to overdiagnosis.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Timing of Screening; Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: The recommended dose of linaclotide for IBS-C is {{c1::290 μg}} PO daily (NNT {{c2::6}}).
extra: Recommendations on IBS-C secretagogues — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Nasal testosterone gel is dosed at {{c1::11 mg}} applied {{c2::two or three times daily}}.
Fecal calprotectin has a sensitivity of {{c1::93%}} and specificity of {{c2::96%}} for ruling out IBD in suspected IBS-D.
extra: Recommendation on fecal calprotectin/CRP — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Oral therapy for impetigo (numerous lesions or outbreaks) consists of dicloxacillin 250 mg QID or cephalexin 250 mg QID × {{c1::7 days}}.
PCSK9 monoclonal antibody dosing: alirocumab {{c1::75–150 mg SC every 2 weeks}}; evolocumab {{c2::140 mg SC every 2 weeks}}; both achieve {{c3::45–64%}} LDL-C reduction.
Before attempting to wean continuous IV anesthetic in refractory SE, maintain seizure suppression or burst-suppression for {{c1::24–48 hours}}.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Refractory SE section; Thresholds & Doses

text: In adults without T2D, an SGLT2 inhibitor is indicated in CKD with eGFR ≥20 if ACR ≥{{c1::200 mg/g}} or if the patient has {{c2::heart failure}} (1A).

USPSTF 2021 assigns an {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement to pharmacotherapy (NRT, bupropion SR, varenicline) for cessation in pregnancy; clinicians should use {{c2::shared decision-making}} based on severity of dependence.
extra: Suggestions for Practice Regarding I Statements — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: When assessing SVR after HCV therapy, {{c1::quantitative PCR}} is preferred; {{c2::POC qualitative}} HCV RNA testing is acceptable only when venipuncture is unavailable.
extra: Footnote *** — POC qualitative testing acceptable in select settings

text: 5-ASA/mesalamine and sulfasalazine are {{c1::not effective}} for induction or maintenance of Crohn's disease and should not be used as maintenance therapy.
extra: Mild-to-moderately severe disease/Mesalamine — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: For CLTI candidates with adequate single-segment {{c1::great saphenous vein}}, {{c2::surgical bypass}} is preferred over endovascular therapy as the initial revascularization strategy.
In TAK, imaging surveillance is recommended approximately every {{c1::3–6 months}} early in the disease course, with longer intervals once the disease is quiescent.
A prior brain insult (e.g., stroke, trauma) increases recurrence risk after a first unprovoked seizure with a relative rate of {{c1::2.55}} (95% CI 1.44–4.51) at 1–5 years (Level A).
extra: Analysis of Evidence: Risk of seizure recurrence — prior brain insult

text: Penicillin skin testing has a negative predictive value of {{c1::97–99%}} and a positive predictive value of approximately {{c2::50%}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XII, Recommendation 13

text: The Friedewald equation for LDL-C estimation is inaccurate and cannot be calculated when TG ≥{{c1::400 mg/dL}}.
extra: Table 5 — Lipid-lowering medication characteristics and dosing

text: Levothyroxine should be taken consistently {{c1::60 minutes before breakfast}} or at bedtime {{c1::(≥3 hours after the evening meal)}} to maximize absorption.
After LLT initiation or intensification, lipids should be rechecked in {{c1::4–12 weeks}}, then every {{c2::6–12 months}}, and annually once stable.
The 2025 guideline recommends sodium intake <{{c1::2300}} mg/day (ideal <{{c2::1500}} mg/day) and potassium intake of {{c3::3500–5000}} mg/day.
The minimum antibiotic duration after achieving clinical stability in CAP is {{c1::3 days}}.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 3

text: For AF in patients with bioprosthetic valves, NOAC or VKA per CHA₂DS₂-VASc is acceptable after {{c1::3 months}} post-implant.
AFP ≥{{c1::20 ng/mL}} triggers further diagnostic workup; AFP ≥{{c2::200–400 ng/mL}} is considered significantly elevated and warrants urgent workup.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Recall and Management of Surveillance Results / Figure 5

text: DOACs ({{c1::apixaban}}, {{c1::rivaroxaban}}, {{c1::edoxaban}}) are acceptable first-line alternatives to LMWH for initial cancer-associated VTE treatment in patients without high {{c2::GI bleeding}} risk.
extra: Appendix 6 Q1 Conclusions — Initial treatment of VTE

text: The 2021 SSC guidelines recommend {{c1::against}} using qSOFA {{c1::alone}} as a sepsis screening tool, favoring combinations such as SIRS, NEWS, or MEWS with clinical assessment.
extra: Screening and Early Treatment — qSOFA recommendation; SSC 2021

text: Hydroxychloroquine should be dosed at ≤{{c1::5 mg/kg/day}} to limit {{c2::retinal toxicity}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Special Populations

text: AGA 2022 recommends using Gelesis100 oral superabsorbent hydrogel only within {{c1::a clinical trial}} due to knowledge gaps.
After major open or laparoscopic abdominal/pelvic cancer surgery, extended LMWH prophylaxis should continue for {{c1::4 weeks}} postoperatively.
extra: Appendix 6 Q4 Conclusions — Surgical prophylaxis extended duration

text: For an initial CDI episode, {{c1::fidaxomicin}} is preferred over a standard course of oral vancomycin (conditional recommendation, moderate certainty).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 Focused Update — Recommendation I (Initial CDI)

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::bicarbonate}} rather than lactate as the RRT buffer, especially in {{c2::circulatory shock}} (1B) or {{c3::liver failure/lactic acidemia}} (2B).
Topical therapy for bullous and nonbullous impetigo is {{c1::mupirocin}} or {{c1::retapamulin}} BID × {{c2::5 days}}.
SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended in CCD patients with {{c1::type 2 diabetes}}, {{c2::HFrEF}}, or {{c2::HFpEF}} to reduce MACE and HF events.
extra: Section 4.2.8 SGLT2 Inhibitors and GLP-1 Receptor Agonists — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Corticosteroid dependence in ITP is defined as an ongoing need for prednisone >{{c1::5 mg/day}} or frequent courses to maintain platelets ≥{{c2::30 ×10⁹/L}}.
extra: Table 3 — definitions of ITP phases, response criteria, and corticosteroid dependence

text: In ACS patients who tolerate ticagrelor-based DAPT, aspirin can be dropped in favor of ticagrelor monotherapy ≥{{c1::1 month}} after PCI to reduce bleeding without increasing ischemic events.
In SCD, indefinite anticoagulation is indicated for a {{c1::first unprovoked VTE}} or {{c1::recurrent provoked VTE}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — VTE Management table

text: In all newly diagnosed GCA, {{c1::noninvasive large-vessel imaging}} (CTA, MRA, PET, or ultrasound) should be obtained to assess {{c2::extracranial involvement}}.
The 24-hour ambulatory BP monitoring (ABPM) threshold corresponding to an office BP of 130/80 mm Hg is {{c1::125/75 mm Hg}}; the daytime ABPM equivalent is {{c2::130/80 mm Hg}}.
The USPSTF 2021 recommends against using {{c1::sputum cytology}}, {{c1::chest radiography}}, or {{c1::biomarker measurement}} for lung cancer screening, as none have demonstrated benefit.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening Tests — LDCT recommended; alternatives not recommended

text: Rituximab induction dosing for AAV is {{c1::375 mg/m²/week × 4 weeks}}.
The USPSTF 2021 recommends providing concurrent {{c1::smoking cessation}} interventions to all eligible patients who currently smoke at the time of lung cancer screening.
In ASCVD patients on a statin with TG {{c1::150–499 mg/dL}} and LDL-C <100 mg/dL, icosapent ethyl {{c2::2 g PO twice daily with food}} is reasonable for additional ASCVD risk reduction.
After cardioversion, anticoagulation should be continued for ≥{{c1::4 weeks}} regardless of CHA2DS2-VASc score.
Metformin dose should be {{c1::reduced}} when eGFR falls below {{c2::45}} ml/min/1.73 m².
CHEST 2021 recommends treating {{c1::low-risk PE}} as an {{c2::outpatient}} over hospitalization if medications, follow-up, and home circumstances are adequate (strong, low-certainty).
ACR 2020 treat-to-target strategy aims for a serum urate of <{{c1::6 mg/dl}} with {{c2::indefinite}} continuation of ULT.
In patients with TAA or AAA, target {{c1::SBP <130 mm Hg}} and {{c2::DBP <80 mm Hg}}; intensive control to <120 mm Hg may benefit selected patients.
In GCA, glucocorticoids should {{c1::not be delayed}} while awaiting biopsy; the biopsy should be obtained within {{c2::2 weeks}} of starting steroids.
Nitrates must be avoided within {{c1::24 hours}} of sildenafil or vardenafil, and within {{c2::48 hours}} of tadalafil.
A positive vasoreactivity response requires mPAP drop ≥{{c1::10 mmHg}} to an absolute value ≤{{c2::40 mmHg}} with preserved or increased cardiac output.
extra: Recommendation Table 7 — vasoreactivity testing and CCB therapy criteria

text: In GCA or TAK with limb/organ ischemia, {{c1::immunosuppression should be escalated}} before considering surgery, and elective vascular intervention should be delayed until disease is {{c2::quiescent}}.

In the INBUILD trial, nintedanib reduced annual FVC decline by approximately {{c1::107 mL/yr}} overall, with greater benefit in patients with a {{c2::UIP}} pattern (~128 mL/yr vs ~75 mL/yr in non-UIP).
extra: Part II Evidence-based Recommendations — nintedanib in PPF (INBUILD trial)

text: ITP is classified as newly diagnosed if duration is {{c1::<3 months}}, persistent if {{c2::3–12 months}}, and chronic if {{c2::>12 months}}.
Cyclosporine rescue in ASUC: {{c1::2 mg/kg IV}} targeting serum level {{c2::200–400 ng/mL}}, then transition to {{c3::thiopurine}} maintenance.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: In asymptomatic {{c1::premenopausal}} women with IDA, bidirectional endoscopy is suggested over iron replacement alone, but with {{c2::shared decision-making}} given the low malignancy yield (conditional recommendation, moderate quality).
The typical RAI dose for Graves' disease is {{c1::10–15 mCi (370–555 MBq)}}.
extra: Recommendations 8–10 (Section D2) — RAI dosing for GD

text: First-line treatment for chlamydia is {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg orally twice daily for 7 days}}.
extra: Chlamydial Infections, Recommended Regimens — doxycycline as first-line over azithromycin, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Adjunctive {{c1::argatroban}}, {{c1::eptifibatide}}, or pre-EVT {{c1::tirofiban}} should NOT be combined with thrombolysis — no benefit and possible harm.
Per 1,000 men screened over 13 years: approximately {{c1::1.3}} prostate cancer deaths prevented and approximately {{c2::3}} metastatic cases prevented.
extra: Table: Estimated Effects After 13 Years of PSA Screening — USPSTF 2018

text: Alpha-1 antitrypsin augmentation therapy is typically considered when FEV1 is {{c1::35–60%}} predicted; all COPD patients should be {{c2::tested}} for deficiency.
extra: GOLD 2026, Appendix 3 — Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency management

text: In LQTS patients with syncope of suspected arrhythmic origin, the first-line pharmacologic therapy is {{c1::beta-blockers}}.
Long-acting cabotegravir PrEP is given as {{c1::600 mg (3 mL) gluteal IM}}; the first 2 injections are {{c2::4 weeks}} apart, then every {{c3::8 weeks}}.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Table 4 — biomedical HIV prevention options

text: The {{c1::EAST-AFNET 4}} trial showed that early rhythm control within {{c2::1 year}} of AF diagnosis reduces composite cardiovascular death, stroke, and hospitalization.
Statin or statin/ezetimibe is recommended for all adults aged ≥{{c1::50}} with eGFR <60 ml/min/1.73 m² (1A).
IV lorazepam dose for status epilepticus: {{c1::0.1 mg/kg}} IV (max {{c2::4 mg}}/dose), may repeat once.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Emergent initial therapy section; Thresholds & Doses

text: Spironolactone for PA is started at {{c1::12.5–25 mg/day}} and titrated to a maximum of {{c2::100 mg/day}}.
Virological breakthrough on therapy is defined as a >{{c1::1 log10}} rise in HBV DNA from nadir.
Prophylactic UFH or LMWH may be added {{c1::24–48 hours}} after ICH onset once hemorrhage stability is confirmed.
In symptomatic patients with a pretest probability of celiac disease >{{c1::5%}}, proceed to duodenal biopsy even if serology is negative.
Continuous high-dose PPI after endoscopic hemostasis: {{c1::80 mg IV bolus}} followed by {{c1::8 mg/hr infusion}} for {{c2::72 hours (3 days)}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Antisecretory therapy after endoscopic hemostatic therapy

text: Once started, an SGLT2i should be {{c1::continued even if eGFR subsequently falls below 20}} ml/min/1.73 m², unless {{c2::dialysis is initiated}}.
For DTC tumors {{c1::1–4 cm}} without extrathyroidal extension and cN0, either {{c2::lobectomy}} or total thyroidectomy is acceptable.
Methotrexate for Crohn's induction/maintenance is dosed at {{c1::15–25 mg SC/IM weekly}}.
In ICH, treat hypoglycemia at glucose <{{c1::40–60}} mg/dL and target hyperglycemia control at <{{c2::180}} mg/dL; avoid intensive glucose control (80–110 mg/dL).
After successful endovascular thrombectomy for ischemic stroke, SBP should NOT be lowered below {{c1::140 mm Hg}} in the first {{c2::24–72 hours}} due to harm.
In axial spondyloarthritis, the preferred first biologic is a {{c1::TNFi}} over secukinumab/ixekizumab; TNFi/secukinumab/ixekizumab are all preferred over {{c2::tofacitinib}}.
In adults with primary osteoporosis who have contraindications to or adverse effects from bisphosphonates, the recommended second-line agent is {{c1::denosumab}} (a {{c2::RANK ligand inhibitor}}).
Give tetanus booster if last dose was >{{c1::10}} years for clean wounds, or >{{c2::5}} years for dirty wounds; {{c1::Tdap}} is preferred over Td if not previously given.
An elevated lactate > {{c1::2 mmol/L}} supports the diagnosis of septic shock; lactate should be used to guide resuscitation toward {{c2::normalization}}.
Modeling estimates that biennial screening from age 40-74 produces approximately {{c1::1,376}} false-positive results and {{c2::14}} overdiagnosed cases per 1,000 women over a lifetime.
extra: Table 2 — modeling estimates of benefits/harms biennial 40-74 vs 50-74

text: In sepsis, start insulin when blood glucose reaches ≥ {{c1::180 mg/dL}}, targeting a glucose range of {{c2::144–180 mg/dL}}.
On the Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale (BWPS), a score of ≥{{c1::45}} indicates thyroid storm; {{c2::25–44}} indicates impending storm.
Early DNAR orders or baseline severity scores alone should {{c1::NOT}} be used to limit care; full guideline-concordant care should be provided for at least {{c2::the first day}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends energy intake of {{c1::20–30 kcal/kg/d}} in patients with AKI.

After the initial 3-day high-dose PPI course, high-risk patients should receive {{c1::twice-daily oral PPI (e.g., esomeprazole 40 mg BID)}} through {{c2::day 14}}, then transition to once-daily dosing.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Antisecretory therapy after endoscopic hemostatic therapy

text: Certolizumab pegol maintenance dosing for Crohn's disease is {{c1::400 mg SC every 4 weeks}}; target trough is ≥{{c2::20 µg/mL}}.
In critically ill COVID-19, dexamethasone doses up to {{c1::20 mg/day}} are acceptable; an equivalent alternative is methylprednisolone {{c2::32 mg}} daily.

Rule of Five for CSPH (mid-range bands):
LSM 20–25 kPa + platelets <{{c1::150 K/mm³}}, OR
LSM 15–20 kPa + platelets <{{c2::110 K/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: Prophylactic platelet transfusions should {{c1::NOT}} be given for antiplatelet-associated ICH unless {{c2::emergency neurosurgery (craniotomy)}} is planned.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.2; PATCH trial

text: A pretreatment FIB-4 of {{c1::>3.25}} triggers the cirrhosis evaluation and surveillance pathway in the AASLD/IDSA simplified HCV algorithm.
extra: Follow-Up Visit algorithm — FIB-4 >3.25 criterion for cirrhosis monitoring pathway

text: In hemodynamically stable hospitalized adults, the AABB 2023 guideline recommends a restrictive RBC transfusion threshold of Hgb <{{c1::7 g/dL}} (strong recommendation).
In knee/hip OA, weight loss of ≥{{c1::5%}} of body weight is associated with symptom and functional improvement, with benefits continuing to increase up to >20% loss.
extra: Reference 18 (Messier 2018) — dose-response for weight loss ≥5% in knee OA

text: A new baseline MRI should be obtained {{c1::3–6 months}} after starting a DMT to allow assessment of treatment effect.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendations

text: The AASM document titled 'AASM Clinical Practice Guidelines' (updated January 2025) is actually {{c1::an organizational bylaws document}}, not a clinical practice guideline — it contains no patient-care recommendations.
For MELD exception eligibility in HCC, if baseline AFP ≥{{c1::1000 ng/mL}}, it must decrease to <{{c2::500 ng/mL}} before liver transplantation.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Liver Transplantation / Table 3 / Figure 12

text: Post-MI ICD for primary prevention is indicated for LVEF ≤{{c1::30%}} (any NYHA class) or {{c1::31–35%}} (NYHA class II–III), at least {{c2::40 days}} after MI with expected survival ≥1 year.
A {{c1::6-month}} trial of DHEA {{c2::25–50 mg once daily in the morning}} may be considered in women with PAI who have persistent low libido, mood, or energy despite optimized replacement.
The NAFLD alcohol threshold is <{{c1::20}} g/day in women and <{{c2::30}} g/day in men; consumption above these levels excludes NAFLD diagnosis.
ACR 2020 conditionally recommends {{c1::against}} ULT for asymptomatic hyperuricemia, even when MSU crystal deposition is detected on imaging.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 1 — Indications for pharmacologic ULT

text: In septic shock, add hydrocortisone {{c1::200 mg/day}} IV when vasopressor (norepinephrine or epinephrine) requirement is ≥ {{c2::0.25 µg/kg/min}} for ≥ {{c2::4 hours}}.
extra: Additional Therapies / Corticosteroids; Hemodynamic Management — Figure 2; SSC 2021

text: After TSS, serum sodium should be monitored repeatedly during postoperative days {{c1::5–14}}, and free T4 and prolactin assessed within {{c2::1–2 weeks}}, to detect hypopituitarism.

For renal replacement therapy in acute HIT, acceptable anticoagulants include {{c1::argatroban, danaparoid, or bivalirudin}}; in subacute/remote HIT not otherwise anticoagulated, use {{c2::regional citrate}}.
In high-risk IPAH/HPAH/DPAH without cardiopulmonary comorbidities, initial therapy is ERA + PDE5i plus {{c1::IV/SC prostacyclin analogue}}.
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10, Figure 9 — PAH treatment algorithm and combination therapy

text: Moderate fracture risk (≥40 years) is defined as FRAX 10-yr major OP fracture {{c1::10–19%}} or hip fracture {{c1::>1% and <3%}}.
The emergent first-line therapy for status epilepticus is a {{c1::benzodiazepine}}, with {{c2::IV lorazepam}} as the preferred agent.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Emergent initial therapy section

text: In ARDS, use a tidal volume of {{c1::6 mL/kg}} predicted body weight and keep plateau pressure ≤ {{c2::30 cm H2O}}; tidal volume may be reduced to {{c1::4 mL/kg}} if plateau pressure exceeds this limit.
extra: Ventilation / Protective Ventilation in ARDS; SSC 2021

text: CHEST 2021 reserves systemic thrombolytic therapy for acute PE in patients with SBP {{c1::<90 mm Hg}} for ≥{{c2::15 minutes}} (hemodynamic deterioration/hypotension) without high bleeding risk.
Rituximab should {{c1::not}} be used routinely as first-line induction in LN; it is reserved for {{c2::refractory disease or when standard agents are contraindicated}}.
Low-dose aspirin reduces nonfatal MI in primary prevention with a Peto OR of {{c1::0.88}} (95% CI 0.80–0.96).
For refractory ascites, TIPS with a {{c1::<10 mm}} covered stent is preferred to reduce the risk of hepatic encephalopathy.
extra: Figure 3 — refractory ascites management algorithm; AASLD 2021

text: Truelove-Witts severe UC is defined by >{{c1::6}} BMs/day plus ≥1 of: fever, tachycardia, Hgb <{{c2::10.5 g/dL}}, or ESR >{{c3::30 mm/hr}}.
extra: Table 4 / ACG UC Disease Activity Index

text: Hold insulin and replace potassium at {{c1::10–20 mmol/h}} if K⁺ is {{c2::<3.5 mmol/L}}; add 10–20 mmol/L K⁺ to each liter of IV fluid if K⁺ is 3.5–5.0 mmol/L.
SABA over-use: ≥{{c1::3}} canisters (200-dose)/year increases exacerbation risk; ≥{{c2::12}} canisters/year is associated with markedly increased mortality risk.
Supplemental oxygen is indicated in acute asthma exacerbations only if SpO₂ is {{c1::<92%}}; the target SpO₂ on oxygen for adults/children 6–11 is {{c2::92–95%}}.
MR antagonists, K-sparing/wasting diuretics, and licorice should be withdrawn ≥{{c1::4 weeks}} before ARR measurement.
For suspected PE, use clinical pretest probability with {{c1::age-adjusted D-dimer}}; first-line imaging in stable patients is {{c2::CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA)}}.
LA grade {{c1::A}} esophagitis alone is NOT diagnostic of GERD; objective GERD diagnosis requires at minimum LA grade {{c2::B}} EE with typical symptoms and PPI response.
extra: Diagnosis of GERD / Endoscopy section — LA grading and criteria

text: Once remission is achieved in PMR, prednisone should be reduced by {{c1::1 mg}} every {{c2::4 weeks}} until discontinuation.
Recombinant zoster vaccine (RZV) is recommended as a {{c1::2-dose}} series for all immunocompetent adults ≥{{c2::50}} years.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Zoster, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: 3HP (isoniazid + rifapentine once weekly × 3 months) is strongly recommended for adults and children ≥{{c1::2}} years, including HIV-positive persons when antiretroviral drug interactions allow.
The REPRIEVE trial demonstrated a {{c1::36%}} reduction in MACE with pitavastatin 4 mg in people with HIV (HR {{c2::0.64}}, 95% CI 0.48–0.84).
Infliximab for COVID-19 (alternative immunomodulator): {{c1::5 mg/kg IV}} as a single dose plus standard of care.
In the immediate post-transplant period, serum calcium and phosphate should be measured at least {{c1::weekly}} until stable.
Severe hypercholesterolemia is defined as LDL-C ≥{{c1::190 mg/dL (4.9 mmol/L)}}.
Anemia is defined as hemoglobin <{{c1::13 g/dL}} in men and <{{c2::12 g/dL}} in nonpregnant women.

Subclinical hyperthyroidism should be treated when TSH is persistently <{{c1::0.1 mU/L}} in patients aged ≥65 years, postmenopausal women not on estrogen/bisphosphonate, or those with cardiac disease, osteoporosis, or hyperthyroid symptoms.
extra: Recommendations 73–76, Table 10 — subclinical hyperthyroidism treatment thresholds

text: High-intensity statin doses (≥50% LDL-C reduction) include atorvastatin {{c1::40–80 mg}} daily and rosuvastatin {{c2::20–40 mg}} daily.
extra: Table 5 — Characteristics and dosing of lipid-lowering medications

text: Topical NSAIDs are {{c1::strongly}} recommended for knee OA (before oral NSAIDs) but are {{c2::not recommended}} for hip OA due to joint depth.

Nonthyroidal illness syndrome should {{c1::not be treated}} with LT4 or LT3; thyroid hormone should also not be used to treat obesity, depression, urticaria, or nonspecific symptoms in euthyroid patients.
USPSTF 2018 assigns a {{c1::Grade D}} recommendation to PSA screening for men aged {{c2::≥70}} years — routine screening is not recommended.
Adults ≥40 at low fracture risk on GCs should receive {{c1::calcium and vitamin D}} alone — bisphosphonates, teriparatide, denosumab, and raloxifene are not recommended.
For very-high-risk secondary prevention, add ezetimibe and/or PCSK9 mAb to high-intensity statin to achieve LDL-C <{{c1::55 mg/dL}} and non-HDL-C <{{c2::85 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.6 / Figure 10–12 — Secondary prevention very-high-risk criteria and LDL-C <55 mg/dL goal

text: Patients with NAFLD and ≥{{c1::F2}} fibrosis should be counseled to abstain {{c2::completely}} from alcohol.
In the initial evaluation of any thyroid nodule, the first laboratory test to order is {{c1::serum TSH}}; if it is subnormal, obtain a {{c2::radionuclide (123I) scan}}.
After natalizumab discontinuation, increased relapse and MRI activity (rebound risk) may occur within {{c1::6 months}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Recommendation 9

text: Pulse IV methylprednisolone for ANCA vasculitis (adults): {{c1::500–1000 mg}} IV daily for {{c2::3–5 days}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 1 (drug dosing regimens)

text: Adding {{c1::belimumab}} or a {{c1::calcineurin inhibitor}} (voclosporin or tacrolimus) to standard induction is considered in LN patients with {{c2::high risk of progression or nephrotic-range proteinuria}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Treatment of Class III/IV LN

text: AKI is diagnosed when urine output is <{{c1::0.5 mL/kg/h}} for ≥{{c2::6 hours}}.
Aspergillus SSTI is treated with {{c1::voriconazole}} (6 mg/kg IV q12h × 2 doses, then 4 mg/kg q12h) for a duration of {{c2::6–12 weeks}}.

The AGA 2020 guideline recommends using a ferritin cutoff of {{c1::45 ng/mL}} (rather than 15 ng/mL) to diagnose iron deficiency in patients with anemia (strong recommendation, high-quality evidence).
A urine sodium {{c1::<30 mmol/L}} suggests low effective arterial blood volume, whereas ≥30 mmol/L suggests SIAD, diuretic use, or adrenal insufficiency.
VKA is reasonable for {{c1::3–6 months}} after bioprosthetic AVR, MVR, or mitral repair; lifelong VKA is only required if another indication exists.
SBP treatment failure is defined as a PMN drop of {{c1::<25%}} on repeat paracentesis at 48 hours.
An Lp(a) ≥{{c1::125 nmol/L (50 mg/dL)}} is considered a risk-enhancing factor for ASCVD.
extra: Section 4.2.10 / Table 4 — Lp(a) thresholds and ASCVD relative risk

text: For active axial spondyloarthritis, use {{c1::continuous}} NSAIDs; once disease is stable, switch to {{c2::on-demand}} NSAIDs.
extra: Table 2, Recommendation re: NSAID dosing strategy (AS); Table 3 (nr-axSpA parallel)

text: In children with CKD, KDIGO 2021 targets a 24-hour MAP by ABPM of ≤{{c1::50th percentile}} for age, sex, and height (2C).
extra: Chapter 5, Recommendation 5.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD (ESCAPE trial)

text: DAPT (aspirin + ticagrelor or prasugrel) should be continued for at least {{c1::12 months}} in ACS patients not at high bleeding risk.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 11.1 (Figure 11, Table 22); Top Take-Home Messages

text: Target INR for mechanical AVR with risk factors or mechanical MVR is {{c1::3.0}} (range {{c2::2.5–3.5}}).
In adult {{c1::cardiac surgery}} patients, an RBC transfusion threshold of Hgb <{{c2::7.5 g/dL}} is acceptable per AABB 2023.

Upper endoscopy should be performed within {{c1::24 hours}} of presentation for hospitalized patients with UGIB; performing it {{c2::<12 hours}} in high-risk patients has not shown benefit and may harm if resuscitation is incomplete.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Timing of endoscopy section / Table 6 (Lau 2020)

text: New-onset ANCA-associated vasculitis is induced with glucocorticoids plus either {{c1::rituximab}} or {{c1::cyclophosphamide}}.
After pacemaker implantation for syncope due to bradycardia or carotid sinus syndrome, the required symptom-free driving interval is {{c1::1 week}}.
Treatment is indicated for HBeAg-positive CHB when HBV DNA is >{{c1::20,000}} IU/mL and ALT is ≥{{c2::2×}} ULN.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Figure 1 / Treatment Algorithms

text: To prevent HRS in SBP, give albumin {{c1::1.5 g/kg on day 1}} and {{c2::1 g/kg on day 3}}.
CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::against}} routine use of compression stockings to prevent post-thrombotic syndrome after acute DVT.
In patients with NAFLD and normal LFTs without advanced fibrosis, methotrexate may be used with LFT monitoring every {{c1::4–8 weeks}}.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: Testosterone therapy should {{c1::NOT}} be prescribed to improve glycemic control in men with type 2 diabetes.
After {{c1::5 years}} of oral bisphosphonate therapy in moderate-to-high risk patients still on GCs, guidelines recommend continuing or switching active therapy rather than stopping.
{{c1::Alternating bundle branch block}} (LBBB and RBBB morphologies) warrants permanent pacing because of high risk of complete AV block.
extra: Section 6.4.4/Figure 7 — chronic AV block management algorithm

text: With a PSA biopsy threshold of ~3 ng/mL, the false-positive rate exceeds {{c1::15%}} over 10 years, and may reach up to {{c2::45%}} with repeated screening at low thresholds.
When both IBD and severe psoriasis are present in a PsA patient, {{c1::IL-12/23i}} is preferred over {{c1::IL-17i}} because IL-17i can worsen IBD.
extra: Table 1 / Figure 3; Table 5 — Concomitant IBD recommendations

text: For secondary stroke prevention in nonvalvular atrial fibrillation, {{c1::DOACs}} are preferred over {{c2::warfarin}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.4.1 (Atrial fibrillation)

text: When aminoglycosides must be used in AKI-risk patients, KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::once-daily}} dosing rather than multiple daily doses to reduce nephrotoxicity (2A).
Ventilator-associated tracheobronchitis (VAT) should {{c1::not}} be treated with antibiotics.
PVC-induced cardiomyopathy is typically suspected when PVC burden exceeds {{c1::10–15%}} of total beats (often >20%) or >{{c2::10,000–20,000}} PVCs/day.
For stable ASCVD, low-dose rivaroxaban {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}} plus aspirin may be used to reduce ischemic events (COMPASS trial).
extra: Section 4.3.1 Antiplatelet Therapy — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: IDSA 2019 recommends against screening or treating ASB in all of the following EXCEPT: healthy nonpregnant women, diabetic patients, spinal cord injury patients, long-term catheter patients, and {{c1::pregnant women}} (who SHOULD be screened and treated).
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Key Recommendations; Sections I, II, VI, XI

text: In sepsis, bicarbonate therapy may be considered in severe metabolic acidemia with pH ≤ {{c1::7.2}} and {{c2::AKIN stage 2–3}} acute kidney injury.
Low fracture risk (≥40 years) is defined as FRAX 10-yr major OP fracture {{c1::<10%}} and hip fracture {{c1::≤1%}}.
Clostridial myonecrosis is treated with emergent surgical debridement plus {{c1::penicillin}} PLUS {{c1::clindamycin}}; hyperbaric oxygen is {{c2::NOT recommended}}.
Prone positioning sessions should last ≥{{c1::16 consecutive hours}} and should be applied {{c2::early after intubation}}.
Low-dose oral glucocorticoids are defined as prednisone {{c1::≤10 mg/day}}.
extra: Table 1 — definitions of disease states and glucocorticoid dose categories

text: In the first 12 months post-transplant, antiresorptive therapy may be considered if eGFR is greater than approximately {{c1::30 mL/min/1.73 m²}} and BMD is low.
Standard LT4 replacement dose in adults is approximately {{c1::1.6 μg/kg/d}} actual body weight (1.6–1.8 μg/kg/d in athyreotic patients).
Eplerenone is indicated post-MI in patients with LVEF ≤{{c1::40%}} who have heart failure or diabetes, provided creatinine is ≤{{c2::2.5 mg/dL}} and potassium is ≤{{c2::5.0 mmol/L}}.
If the patient has not received heparin in the prior 30 days, platelet monitoring begins on day {{c1::4}} and continues until day {{c2::14}} or until heparin is stopped.
Bilateral adrenal PA is treated with a mineralocorticoid receptor (MR) antagonist; {{c1::spironolactone}} is first-line and {{c2::eplerenone}} is the alternative.

In pregnant women with current or prior Graves' disease, TRAb should be measured at the initial visit and at {{c1::18–22 weeks}} gestation (and at {{c2::30–34 weeks}} if elevated) to assess fetal/neonatal hyperthyroidism risk.
For adults ≥65 years, the preferred influenza vaccine formulations are {{c1::HD-IIV3}}, {{c1::RIV3}}, or {{c1::aIIV3}}.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Influenza, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The 2024 ADA/EASD consensus lowered the DKA glucose diagnostic criterion to {{c1::≥200 mg/dL}} (from the prior >250 mg/dL), or any known diabetes history (to capture euglycemic DKA).
extra: Figure 2 (Diagnostic Criteria); Thresholds & Doses — DKA glucose criterion

text: Finerenone dosing: {{c1::10 mg}} daily if eGFR 25–59; {{c2::20 mg}} daily if eGFR ≥60 ml/min/1.73 m².
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.8.1 and Figure 26

text: Microaxial flow pump use in AMI cardiogenic shock is considered for patients with SCAI stages {{c1::C–E}}, who are {{c2::non-comatose}} with adequate vascular access.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Section 8.2; Top Take-Home Messages

text: Cilostazol improves claudication symptoms and walking distance but is contraindicated in {{c1::heart failure of any severity}}.
extra: Key Recommendations — cilostazol use in PAD

text: When transitioning from IV to subcutaneous insulin, estimate TDD at {{c1::0.5–0.6 units/kg/day}} ({{c2::0.3 units/kg/day}} for frail, CKD, or elderly patients), overlapping SC basal insulin with the IV drip for 1–2 h.
Medications requiring screening for serious drug-drug interactions with nirmatrelvir/ritonavir include {{c1::clopidogrel}}, {{c2::calcineurin inhibitors}}, statins, and strong CYP3A inducers.
Before ARR testing, plasma potassium should be corrected to approximately {{c1::4.0 mmol/L}}.

{{c1::Supervised exercise therapy (SET)}} is the first-line referral for all patients with chronic symptomatic PAD/claudication; structured {{c2::community/home-based}} programs are an effective alternative.
extra: Section 6, Table 14 — exercise programs for claudication

text: For minor stroke or high-risk TIA, DAPT with aspirin + clopidogrel is continued for {{c1::21–90 days}}, then switched to single antiplatelet therapy; DAPT should {{c2::not}} be continued long-term.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.19; CHANCE (21 days) and POINT (90 days) trials

text: Lyme carditis causing AV block is almost always reversible with {{c1::antibiotics}} and rarely requires {{c2::permanent pacing}}.
Adults aged 40–59 with ≥10% 10-year CVD risk: USPSTF 2022 assigns {{c1::Grade C}}, recommending {{c2::individualized initiation via shared decision-making}} for low-dose aspirin.
HIV PrEP should be offered to persons diagnosed with {{c1::gonorrhea or syphilis}} within the prior {{c2::6 months}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — HIV PrEP recommendations

text: Initial pharmacotherapy for GOLD Group B (high symptoms, 0 exacerbations) is {{c1::LABA + LAMA}}.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.7 — Initial pharmacological treatment algorithm

text: In children with ITP unresponsive to first-line therapy, the preferred second-line option is {{c1::TPO-RA}}, favored over rituximab; rituximab is preferred over {{c2::splenectomy}}.
Denosumab should be avoided in {{c1::organ transplant recipients}} due to lack of safety data with concurrent immunosuppression.
In adults with clinical CVD, diabetes, CKD, or PREVENT 10-year CVD risk ≥7.5%, antihypertensive pharmacotherapy is initiated at BP ≥{{c1::130/80 mm Hg}}.
After ileocolonic resection for Crohn's disease, colonoscopy should be performed at {{c1::6 months}} postoperatively to assess for endoscopic recurrence.
extra: Postoperative Crohn's Disease — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: Routine colonoscopy is NOT indicated in IBS patients {{c1::<45 years}} without alarm features; consider it in {{c2::women >60 with diarrhea}} to evaluate for microscopic colitis.
extra: Recommendation on colonoscopy — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Intermittent pneumatic compression (IPC) should be started on the {{c1::day of admission}} for VTE prophylaxis in ICH; graduated compression stockings alone are {{c2::ineffective}}.
In syncope patients without {{c1::focal neurological findings}}, routine neuroimaging (CT, MRI, carotid ultrasound) and EEG should be avoided due to low yield.
Uncomplicated vulvovaginal candidiasis can be treated with a single oral dose of {{c1::fluconazole 150 mg}}; recurrent disease requires induction then {{c2::fluconazole 150 mg weekly for 6 months}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section XV–XVII, Recommendations 115–140

text: DoxyPEP is recommended for {{c1::MSM and transgender women}} who have had a bacterial STI in the past {{c2::year}}.
For primary prevention at intermediate 10-year risk (5–<10%), at least moderate-intensity statin targets LDL-C <{{c1::100 mg/dL}} and non-HDL-C <{{c2::130 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.7 — Primary prevention algorithm by risk category with LDL-C and non-HDL-C goals

text: CT alone misclassifies approximately {{c1::38%}} of PA cases, which is why AVS is required for lateralization in surgical candidates.
For ASD shunt closure: PVR <{{c1::3 WU}} is recommended; PVR {{c1::3–5 WU}} — consider; PVR >{{c1::5 WU}} — generally not recommended.
extra: Section 3.1 / Recommendation Tables — congenital heart disease PH thresholds

text: KDIGO 2024 recommends treating {{c1::all}} patients with lupus nephritis with {{c2::hydroxychloroquine}} unless contraindicated.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Top 10 Takeaways; Chapter on Special Populations

text: Surgical resection for HCC is reserved for Child-Pugh {{c1::A}} patients with a {{c2::single tumor}} and without {{c3::clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH)}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Surgical Resection / Figure 10

text: Per ERSPC data, the number needed to screen (NNS) to prevent 1 prostate cancer death was {{c1::781}} men aged 55–69 screened over {{c2::13}} years.
extra: Table: Estimated Effects After 13 Years of PSA Screening — USPSTF 2018

text: HDV-RNA–positive patients should be treated with {{c1::peg-IFN-α}} for {{c2::12 months}}, with an NA added if HBV DNA is elevated.

AGA 2022 suggests {{c1::against orlistat}} due to modest benefit (~2.8% TBWL) and high GI adverse effect burden; users should take a daily multivitamin containing fat-soluble vitamins ≥{{c2::2 hours}} apart from the drug.
In cancer-associated thrombosis with luminal GI malignancy, CHEST 2021 prefers {{c1::apixaban or LMWH}} over other oral Xa inhibitors due to bleeding risk.
In the 3HP regimen, rifapentine dosing for patients ≥50 kg is {{c1::900 mg}} once weekly.
Ezetimibe 10 mg daily reduces LDL-C by ~{{c1::18%}} as monotherapy and by ~{{c2::25%}} when added to a statin.
extra: Table 5, Table 6 — Lipid-lowering drug classes and doses

text: The target trough for adalimumab in Crohn's disease is ≥{{c1::5 µg/mL}}.
HCC surveillance is indicated in all patients with cirrhosis {{c1::Child-Pugh A or B}}, and in Child-Pugh C patients only if {{c2::listed for liver transplant}}.
Status epilepticus is operationally defined as {{c1::≥5 minutes}} of continuous seizure activity or ≥2 discrete seizures without recovery of consciousness.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Definition section

text: In GCA, an isolated rise in ESR/CRP without clinical or imaging signs of activity should prompt {{c1::observation}}, not escalation of immunosuppression.

For Candida endocarditis with valve replacement, antifungal therapy should be continued for at least {{c1::6 weeks}} postoperatively; patients with prosthetic valves or non-operable disease require {{c2::lifelong fluconazole suppression}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section X, Recommendations 59–73

text: In adults with hypertension plus CKD (eGFR <60 or albuminuria ≥30 mg/g) or diabetes with albuminuria, preferred agents are {{c1::ACE inhibitors or ARBs (not both simultaneously)}}.

Life-threatening hypercortisolism (sepsis, PE, cardiovascular complications, acute psychosis) should be treated urgently within {{c1::24–72 hours}}, often with combination steroidogenesis inhibitors or etomidate.
Low-dose ICS for adults/adolescents: budesonide DPI {{c1::200–400 mcg/day}}; fluticasone propionate {{c2::100–250 mcg/day}} (metered daily dose).
Before cardioversion for AF of >{{c1::48 hours}} or unknown duration, ≥{{c2::3 weeks}} of therapeutic anticoagulation (or TEE-guided strategy) is required, followed by ≥4 weeks postcardioversion.
extra: Section 6.7 — periprocedural anticoagulation; cardioversion timing rule

text: An alternative OCS for children 6–11 with asthma exacerbation is dexamethasone {{c1::0.3–0.6 mg/kg (max 12 mg)}} for {{c2::1–2 days}}.
In elderly and postmenopausal women, a TSH <{{c1::0.1 mIU/L}} is associated with a ~3× increased risk of {{c2::atrial fibrillation}} and increased fracture risk.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 6a; Thresholds & Doses — Avoid TSH <0.1 mIU/L

text: For T2DM, {{c1::metformin}} is first-line pharmacotherapy; add an {{c2::SGLT-2 inhibitor or GLP-1 receptor agonist}} in patients with additional ASCVD risk factors for cardiovascular benefit.
extra: Section 4.2 Adults With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus; Figure 2

text: ICD implantation is indicated for {{c1::secondary prevention}} in survivors of SCA due to VF or hemodynamically unstable VT, or sustained VT with structural heart disease, when meaningful survival >1 year is expected.

Dexamethasone is strongly recommended in {{c1::critically ill}} COVID-19 and suggested in {{c2::severe (hypoxemic) non-critical}} COVID-19, but suggested against in {{c3::non-hypoxemic}} hospitalized patients.
4Ts score interpretation: ≤3 = {{c1::low}} probability; 4–5 = {{c2::intermediate}} probability; 6–8 = {{c3::high}} probability.
Warfarin (rather than a DOAC) is recommended for AF patients with {{c1::moderate-severe mitral stenosis}} or {{c2::mechanical heart valves}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.4.1 (Atrial fibrillation)

text: For cardiac surgery in acute HIT, the preferred strategy is to {{c1::delay surgery until subacute HIT B or remote HIT}}, then use heparin limited to the intraoperative period.
US H. pylori clarithromycin resistance rates are {{c1::20–30%}}, which is why PPI-clarithromycin triple therapy is no longer recommended empirically.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Antibiotic susceptibility testing; Thresholds & Doses

text: GINA 2026 recommends using a validated clinical score such as the {{c1::PRAM}} to assess exacerbation severity in children <{{c2::18}} years.
Confirmatory testing for PA may be skipped when the following triad coexists: spontaneous {{c1::hypokalemia}}, undetectable {{c2::renin}}, and PAC >{{c3::20 ng/dL}} (550 pmol/L).
Adults ≥65 years should receive {{c1::≥2 doses}} of 2024–2025 COVID-19 vaccine, whereas adults 19–64 years should receive {{c2::≥1 dose}}.
extra: Table 1; Addendum (updated July 2, 2025), 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: For antiphospholipid syndrome during the treatment phase, CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::adjusted-dose VKA (target INR 2.5)}} over DOACs.
For secondary prevention in CCD, aspirin {{c1::75-100 mg}} (81 mg in the US) daily is recommended indefinitely; higher doses (325 mg) offer no additional benefit (ADAPTABLE trial).
extra: Section 4.3.1 Antiplatelet Therapy — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: The standard vancomycin regimen for CDI is {{c1::125 mg PO four times daily}} for {{c2::10 days}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: The guideline recommends AGAINST {{c1::extracorporeal CO2 removal (ECCO2R)}} for ARDS outside of randomized trials due to lack of mortality benefit and increased {{c2::bleeding}} risk.
Infants born to HBsAg-positive mothers should receive {{c1::HBIG}} plus {{c1::hepatitis B vaccine}} within {{c2::12 hours}} of birth.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Section 1C & Guidance Statements on Pregnancy

text: In pregnancy, chronic hypertension should be treated to a goal of <{{c1::140/90 mm Hg}} using {{c2::labetalol}} or {{c2::extended-release nifedipine}}; ACEi, ARBs, and atenolol are contraindicated.
Obstructive CAD is defined as ≥{{c1::50%}} stenosis; high-risk CAD is defined as left main ≥{{c2::50%}} or 3-vessel disease ≥{{c2::70%}} stenosis.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Thresholds & Doses

text: In primary prevention adults at high ASCVD risk (≥10%), the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<70 mg/dL}} and the non–HDL-C goal is {{c2::<100 mg/dL}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — Primary prevention LDL-C goals

text: Refractory GERD is defined as persistent heartburn or regurgitation despite {{c1::8 weeks}} of {{c2::double-dose (twice-daily)}} PPI therapy.
In adults ≥40 at moderate or high fracture risk on GCs, first-line pharmacologic therapy is {{c1::an oral bisphosphonate}}.
Test for {{c1::C. difficile}} in all patients with new UC diagnosis or flare/hospitalization; treat positive cases with {{c2::vancomycin or fidaxomicin}}.
extra: Diagnosis, Assessment, Monitoring, and Prognosis of UC

text: In patients with high BP, CKD G1–G4, and moderately increased albuminuria (A2) without diabetes, KDIGO 2021 recommends starting {{c1::ACEi or ARB}} ({{c2::2C}}).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.2.2 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Triglycerides ≥{{c1::1000 mg/dL (11.3 mmol/L)}} carry pancreatitis risk and warrant referral to a registered dietitian nutritionist.
USPSTF 2018 assigns a {{c1::Grade C}} recommendation to PSA-based prostate cancer screening for men aged {{c2::55–69}} years, meaning it is an individualized decision via shared decision-making.
Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention should be avoided in adults older than {{c1::70}} years or in anyone with {{c2::increased bleeding risk}}.
ACR 2020 strongly prefers {{c1::allopurinol}} as first-line ULT for all patients, including those with CKD stage ≥3; {{c2::pegloticase}} is strongly recommended against as first-line.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2 — Initial ULT choice

text: AKI is diagnosed when serum creatinine reaches ≥{{c1::1.5×}} baseline within {{c2::7 days}}.
AGA 2022 prioritizes {{c1::semaglutide 2.4 mg SQ weekly}} over other anti-obesity medications for most patients, based on its mean weight loss of ~{{c2::10.8%}} TBWL vs placebo.
When ABI is {{c1::>1.40}} (noncompressible vessels), use {{c2::toe-brachial index (TBI)}} instead to diagnose PAD.
extra: Section 3.1 — resting ABI thresholds, TBI use in noncompressible vessels

text: In hospitalized ASUC, assess response by day {{c1::3–5}} and initiate {{c2::rescue therapy (infliximab or cyclosporine)}} or colectomy if no response.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: USPSTF 2021 assigns Grade {{c1::A}} to providing {{c2::behavioral counseling}} (not pharmacotherapy) for tobacco cessation in pregnant persons.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: Troponin >{{c1::99th percentile}} upper reference limit indicates myocardial injury; the assay coefficient of variation at that level should be ≤{{c2::10%}}.
Mineralocorticoid replacement in adults with PAI uses fludrocortisone at a typical starting dose of {{c1::50–100 μg/day}}, without dietary salt restriction.
In CLTI, wound healing is predicted by TcPO2 >{{c1::30}} mm Hg or SPP >{{c2::40}} mm Hg.
Primary VTE prophylaxis is recommended for ambulatory cancer patients on chemotherapy with a Khorana score ≥{{c1::2}}.
extra: Appendix 6 Q5 Conclusions — Ambulatory thromboprophylaxis; AVERT, CASSINI

text: For neonatal invasive candidiasis, first-line therapy is {{c1::amphotericin B deoxycholate 1 mg/kg/day}}; echinocandins are reserved for {{c2::salvage}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section VII, Recommendations 37–53

text: Adults with T2D and CKD should be treated with an SGLT2 inhibitor if eGFR is ≥{{c1::20}} ml/min/1.73 m² (1A).
Pretreatment AST/ALT/platelets are valid if drawn within {{c1::6 months}} prior to treatment initiation (may be deferred if transient elastography was done in the prior 6 months).
extra: Footnote * — timing window for pretreatment labs

text: The per-unit risk of TACO (transfusion-associated circulatory overload) from RBC transfusion in the US is approximately {{c1::1:125}} per AABB 2023.
extra: AABB 2023 — Table 1, per-unit RBC transfusion risks

text: The 2019 ACR/SAA/SPARTAN guideline strongly recommends {{c1::against}} mandated switching from an originator TNFi to its biosimilar in stable patients, and against switching to a biosimilar after TNFi failure.
Minimally invasive surgical (MIS) resection may extend resection candidacy to patients with {{c1::mild portal hypertension}} undergoing {{c2::minor}} hepatic resection.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Surgical Resection / Figure 10

text: A lenient resting heart rate target of <{{c1::110}} bpm is acceptable for many AF patients (RACE II); a stricter target of <{{c2::80}} bpm at rest is preferred for symptomatic AF or tachycardia-induced cardiomyopathy.
extra: Section 7.1 and Table 20 — rate control targets (RACE II)

text: Adults at risk for mpox exposure should receive {{c1::JYNNEOS}} vaccine as a {{c2::2-dose}} series.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Mpox, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: In the IMbrave150 trial, atezolizumab plus bevacizumab achieved a median overall survival of {{c1::19.2 months}} in advanced HCC.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Tables 7–8

text: In GCA, aspirin should be added only when there is {{c1::critical or flow-limiting vertebral or carotid artery involvement}}; it is not recommended routinely.

For ALI with viable or threatened limb (Rutherford I/IIa/IIb), initiate {{c1::IV unfractionated heparin}} and pursue urgent {{c2::revascularization (catheter-directed thrombolysis, mechanical thrombectomy, or surgery)}}.
For anterior-circulation LVO (ICA/M1), EVT within 0–6 hours requires NIHSS ≥{{c1::6}}, prestroke mRS {{c2::0–1}}, and ASPECTS {{c3::3–10}}.
Adults aged ≥65 should receive {{c1::≥2 doses}} of the 2024–2025 COVID-19 vaccine.
extra: Notes — COVID-19 vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: For primary prevention at 10-year risk ≥10%, high-intensity statin targets LDL-C <{{c1::70 mg/dL}} and non-HDL-C <{{c2::100 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.7 — Primary prevention algorithm by risk category with LDL-C and non-HDL-C goals

text: In CKD G3a–G3b, serum calcium and phosphate should be monitored every {{c1::6–12 months}}.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Thresholds & Doses — monitoring intervals

text: The treatment of choice for documented unilateral PA (APA or UAH) is {{c1::laparoscopic unilateral adrenalectomy}}.
For eligible AIS within 4.5 hours, either {{c1::tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg IV bolus (max 25 mg)}} or {{c2::alteplase 0.9 mg/kg IV (max 90 mg)}} is recommended.
extra: Section 4.6.2, COR 1 — Choice of Thrombolytic Agent

text: In patients with PAD and diabetic foot ulcers, an HbA1c target of <{{c1::8%}} is associated with lower amputation risk.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — HbA1c targets in PAD/diabetic foot

text: In a treat-to-target approach, disease activity should be reassessed at a minimum of every {{c1::3 months}} based on efficacy and tolerability.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treat-to-target and treatment modification)

text: After cardiac surgery, permanent pacemaker implantation is generally avoided for <{{c1::72 hours}} to allow conduction recovery; typically wait {{c2::5-7 days}} for CABG/MV and {{c2::3-5 days}} for AVR/TV.
IV fluid for CI-AKI prevention: {{c1::1.0–1.5 mL/kg/h}} for {{c2::3–12 h before}} and {{c2::6–12 h after}} contrast administration, targeting urine output ≥{{c3::150 mL/h}}.
Low tidal volume ventilation for ARDS targets {{c1::4–8 mL/kg}} predicted body weight and is recommended to reduce mortality, including in COVID-19 ARDS.
PrEP options include daily {{c1::TDF/FTC}}, daily {{c1::TAF/FTC}}, on-demand {{c1::2-1-1 TDF/FTC}} (for receptive anal sex in cisgender men), or long-acting injectable {{c1::cabotegravir}}.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 6 / Table 4 — biomedical HIV prevention options

text: Mineralocorticoid replacement with fludrocortisone is not required if the daily hydrocortisone dose exceeds {{c1::50 mg/24 h}}, as 40 mg hydrocortisone is equivalent to approximately 100 μg fludrocortisone.
CMR with late gadolinium enhancement should be used to evaluate suspected myopericarditis or MINOCA within {{c1::2 weeks}} of presentation.
After ≥2 unprovoked seizures, recurrence risk is {{c1::57%}} at 1 year and {{c2::73%}} at 4 years.
extra: Table 1 — Pooled recurrence rates from Class I/II studies

text: A meaningful preventive response is defined as approximately {{c1::50%}} reduction in monthly headache days, plus improvements in severity, function, disability, and HRQoL assessed with validated tools (e.g., MIDAS, HIT-6, MSQ).
extra: AHS 2021 — Preventive Treatment: Developing Treatment Plans

text: In liver dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5), heart failure, anasarca, or post-cardiac surgery, argatroban should be reduced to {{c1::0.5–1.2 µg/kg/min}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: The 2019 ACR/AF guideline {{c1::strongly recommends}} weight loss for overweight/obese patients with knee and/or hip OA, ideally combined with exercise.
extra: Table 1 / Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body approaches — weight loss recommendation

text: Dialysis catheters should be inserted under {{c1::ultrasound guidance}} (1A) and confirmed with a {{c2::post-placement chest radiograph}} (1B).
When treating acute ICH, SBP should not be lowered below {{c1::130 mm Hg}}, and large BP variability should be avoided.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.1 (Acute BP Lowering)

text: PPIs should be stopped {{c1::2-4 weeks}} before diagnostic endoscopy to maximize yield for erosive esophagitis (EE) and EoE.
High-intensity statin options for PAD include atorvastatin {{c1::40–80 mg}} or rosuvastatin {{c2::20–40 mg}} daily.

New endorsing societies added for the ADA Standards of Care 2026 edition are the {{c1::National Kidney Foundation (NKF)}} for the CKD section and {{c2::ISPAD}} for the Children and Adolescents section.
extra: Intended Audience and Endorsements — new NKF and ISPAD endorsements for 2026

text: HHS is diagnosed when glucose is ≥{{c1::600 mg/dL}} and effective osmolality is >{{c2::300 mOsm/kg}} (or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg).

In treatment-naive PsA, an OSM is acceptable instead of a TNFi when disease is not severe, the patient prefers an oral agent, or TNFi is contraindicated (e.g., {{c1::CHF}}, {{c2::recurrent/serious infections}}, or {{c3::demyelinating disease}}).
extra: Table 1 / Figure 3 — Recommendations for OSM- and treatment-naive active PsA

text: If hemoglobin does not improve within approximately {{c1::1 month}} of starting oral iron, reassess for nonadherence, malabsorption, ongoing blood loss, or missed celiac disease.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Section 'How should iron supplementation be managed?'; Thresholds & Doses

text:
2025 AHA/ACC BP categories:
Normal: <120/<80 mm Hg
Elevated: {{c1::120–129/<80}} mm Hg
Stage 1: {{c2::130–139/80–89}} mm Hg
Stage 2: {{c3::≥140/≥90}} mm Hg
extra: Top Take-Home Message #6; Table 1 (What Is New)

text: Weight loss targets in NAFLD: {{c1::3–5%}} improves steatosis; ≥{{c2::7–10%}} improves NASH; >{{c2::10%}} improves fibrosis.
The ADA guideline defines adults as ages {{c1::18–64 years}} and older adults as age {{c2::≥65 years}}.
extra: Scope of the Guidelines — age strata and diabetes types covered

text: In pregnant patients with Marfan syndrome, cesarean delivery is recommended when aortic diameter is >{{c1::4.5 cm}}.
extra: Section 8; Table 34 — 2022 ACC/AHA Aortic Disease Guideline

text: 'More symptoms' (Groups B/E) is defined as mMRC ≥{{c1::2}} or CAT ≥{{c1::10}}.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 2, Figure 2.13 — ABE Assessment Tool

text: Bezlotoxumab should be used cautiously in patients with a history of {{c1::congestive heart failure}} due to an FDA warning (RR for heart failure events 2.64 [1.00–7.03]).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Bezlotoxumab Implementation Considerations / FDA warning

text: In patients with cirrhosis, cure must be confirmed with {{c1::SVR12}} — defined as an undetectable HCV RNA at {{c1::12 weeks}} post-treatment.
The GDS-15 at a cutoff of ≥{{c1::5}} has a sensitivity of {{c2::0.94}} and specificity of {{c2::0.81}} for depression in older adults.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests

text: In thyroid storm, iodine (e.g., SSKI) must be administered at least {{c1::1 hour}} after the ATD to prevent iodine from fueling new thyroid hormone synthesis.
extra: Recommendations 34–35, Tables 6–7 — thyroid storm multimodality treatment

text: MR antagonists should be avoided in stage {{c1::IV}} CKD, and used with caution when GFR <{{c2::60 mL/min/1.73 m²}} (stage III).

ACR 2021 strongly recommends initiating a csDMARD without {{c1::longer-term (≥3 months)}} glucocorticoids; any glucocorticoid use should be at the lowest effective dose for the {{c2::shortest}} duration.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 2 (DMARD initiation); Table 1 (Guiding principles)

text: In neurologically stable patients with prior stroke/TIA, the blood pressure target is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}; antihypertensives should be initiated when BP exceeds {{c1::130/80 mm Hg}}.
DKA resolution requires venous pH >{{c1::7.3}} or bicarbonate >{{c1::18 mmol/L}} AND ketones <{{c2::0.6 mmol/L}}.
The 2026 ACC/AHA guideline recommends using the {{c1::Martin/Hopkins}} or {{c2::Sampson/NIH}} equation (not Friedewald) to estimate LDL-C on standard lipid profiles.
extra: Table 5, Table 6 — Lipid-lowering drug classes, doses, and statin intensity definitions

text: In GINA 2026 Track 1 (preferred) for adults/adolescents, the reliever at all steps is {{c1::as-needed low-dose ICS-formoterol}}.
A >5% BMI increase is associated with ~{{c1::60%}} higher recurrent flare odds, while a >5% BMI decrease is associated with ~{{c2::40%}} lower odds.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 7 — Lifestyle management

text: To rule out CTEPH in unexplained PH, {{c1::V/Q lung scan}} (not CTPA alone) is the recommended imaging modality.
extra: Recommendation Table 24 / Figures 14–15 — CTEPH multimodality management

text: The 2019 ACR/AF guideline strongly recommends AGAINST {{c1::TENS}}, {{c2::PRP}}, and stem cell injections for OA.
extra: Pharmacologic management — TNF inhibitors / IL-1 antagonists / PRP / stem cell sections; Figure 1 and Figure 2

text: Agents that should NOT be used as NASH-specific therapy due to lack of histological benefit include {{c1::metformin}}, {{c1::UDCA}}, {{c1::DPP-4 inhibitors}}, and {{c1::silymarin}}.
The 2020 NTCA/CDC LTBI regimens do NOT apply to contacts of {{c1::MDR-TB}}; these patients should be referred to the {{c2::2019 ATS/CDC/ERS/IDSA drug-resistant TB guideline}}.
A baseline ANC <{{c1::1000/mm³}} is a contraindication to starting ATD therapy.
The 2019 ACR/AF guideline {{c1::strongly recommends AGAINST}} intraarticular hyaluronic acid for {{c2::hip}} OA, and conditionally recommends against it for knee and first CMC OA.
extra: Pharmacologic management — Intraarticular hyaluronic acid section

text: Preoperative iodine preparation for thyroidectomy consists of Lugol's {{c1::5–7 drops}} or SSKI {{c2::1–2 drops}} three times daily for {{c3::10 days}} before surgery.

Withdrawal of pacemaker therapy in a pacemaker-dependent patient at the patient's request is {{c1::not considered physician-assisted suicide}}; shared decision-making including the right to refuse or withdraw therapy is endorsed.
extra: Section 11 and 14 — shared decision-making and end-of-life pacemaker management

text: USPSTF 2023 recommends screening all adults ≥{{c1::19}} years for major depressive disorder (Grade {{c2::B}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary table — Grade B for depression screening; Practice Considerations: Patient Population

text: Linezolid for MRSA HAP/VAP is dosed at {{c1::600 mg IV q12h}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3

text: For {{c1::TB meningitis}} or {{c1::cryptococcal meningitis}}, ART should be deferred {{c2::2–4 weeks}} after starting antimicrobial therapy.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 1 — timing of ART initiation including OI scenarios

text: In immunocompetent hospitalized COVID-19 patients, IDSA recommends {{c1::against}} convalescent plasma; high-titer plasma may be considered in high-risk {{c2::ambulatory}} patients with no other options within {{c3::8 days}} of symptom onset.
extra: Key Recommendations — convalescent plasma; Thresholds & Doses

text: The recommended adult dexamethasone dose for initial ITP therapy is {{c1::40 mg/day × 4 days}}; it may provide a faster {{c2::7-day}} response compared with prednisone.
extra: Recommendation 4 — prednisone vs dexamethasone dosing in adults

text: In acute pancreatitis, CT or MRI should be obtained if there is no clinical improvement after {{c1::48–72 hours}}.

Glucocorticoid-remediable aldosteronism (FH-I) is treated with the lowest effective dose of a long-acting glucocorticoid; options are dexamethasone {{c1::0.125–0.25 mg/day at bedtime}} or prednisone {{c2::2.5–5 mg/day}}.
The GINA 2026 bronchodilator responsiveness criterion (2005 ERS/ATS) is an increase in FEV₁ or FVC of ≥{{c1::12%}} AND ≥{{c2::200 mL}} from baseline.
For isolated subsegmental PE without proximal DVT and {{c1::low recurrence risk}}, CHEST 2021 recommends {{c2::clinical surveillance}} over anticoagulation.

For stroke prevention in AF, {{c1::DOACs}} are preferred over {{c1::warfarin}}, except in patients with {{c2::moderate-to-severe rheumatic mitral stenosis}} or {{c2::mechanical heart valves}}, where warfarin remains first-line.
extra: Section 6.3.1 and Figure 10 — antithrombotic therapy recommendations

text: Apixaban for cancer-associated thrombosis: {{c1::10 mg BID × 7 days}}, then {{c2::5 mg BID}} for ≥6 months.
For active severe GPA/MPA, {{c1::rituximab}} is preferred over {{c1::cyclophosphamide}} for remission induction (combined with glucocorticoids).
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendation 1 (GPA/MPA); Table 2

text: CGRP monoclonal antibody preventive response should be assessed after ≥{{c1::3 months}} for monthly dosing and ≥{{c2::6 months}} for quarterly dosing.
extra: AHS 2021 — Preventive Treatment: Developing Treatment Plans

text: Dexamethasone for severe/critical hospitalized COVID-19: {{c1::6 mg}} IV or PO daily × {{c2::10 days}} or until discharge.
CAC = 0 supports deferral of LLT except in the following settings: {{c1::FH, diabetes, active smoking, severe hypercholesterolemia, or strong premature ASCVD family history}}.

For perianal Crohn's disease, {{c1::surgical drainage}} of abscess must be performed before starting immunosuppression; complex perianal fistulas are treated with {{c2::seton placement plus infliximab}}.
extra: Fistulizing Crohn's Disease/Perianal — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: All pregnant women should be screened for {{c1::HIV, syphilis, HBsAg, and chlamydia}} at the first prenatal visit, with universal HCV screening each pregnancy.
extra: STI Detection Among Special Populations — Pregnant Women, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: In high-risk Crohn's patients after resection, {{c1::anti-TNF therapy}} is the preferred postoperative prophylaxis agent.
extra: Postoperative Crohn's Disease — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: Prasugrel is contraindicated in patients with a prior history of {{c1::TIA or stroke (CVA)}}.

For adults aged 40–75 years with diabetes, prescribe at minimum a {{c1::moderate}}-intensity statin regardless of risk score, escalating to {{c2::high}}-intensity statin if multiple ASCVD risk factors are present.
extra: Section 4.3 Adults With High Blood Cholesterol; Figure 3

text: HAP is defined as pneumonia occurring ≥{{c1::48 hours}} after hospital admission (non-ventilated); VAP is pneumonia occurring >{{c2::48 hours}} after endotracheal intubation.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Thresholds & Doses, HAP/VAP definitions

text: Prophylactic antibiotics are recommended for patients with ≥{{c1::3–4}} cellulitis episodes per year despite addressing predisposing factors.
ADA consensus reports, scientific reviews, and ADA statements are {{c1::distinct from the Standards of Care}} and do {{c2::not}} constitute official ADA clinical practice recommendations.
extra: Revision Process — public comment, peer review, and live updates

text: Refractory status epilepticus is treated with continuous IV infusion of {{c1::midazolam}}, {{c1::propofol}}, or {{c1::pentobarbital}}, titrated to seizure suppression or burst-suppression on EEG.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Refractory SE section

text: Patients with IPF at increased mortality risk should be referred for lung transplantation {{c1::at the time of diagnosis}}, with reassessment every {{c2::3–6 months}} for disease progression.
First-line treatment for torsades de pointes is {{c1::magnesium 1–2 g IV}}; target serum K+ ≥{{c2::4.5}} mEq/L.
extra: Section 5.1, Table 7 — torsades de pointes management

text: HLA DQ2/DQ8 testing in celiac disease is reserved for select scenarios such as {{c1::serology-histology discrepancy}}, evaluation of patients already on a GFD, or before gluten challenge; a negative result {{c2::rules out}} celiac disease.
4-factor PCC for warfarin reversal: INR 2–<4: {{c1::25 U/kg}}; INR 4–6: {{c2::35 U/kg}}; INR >6: {{c3::50 U/kg}}.
CAP clinical stability criteria include: temp {{c1::<37.8°C}}, HR {{c2::<100 bpm}}, RR {{c3::<24/min}}, SpO2 >90% or PaO2 >60 mm Hg on room air, SBP >90 mm Hg, and normal mental status.
Modality education and transplant/access planning should begin when the 2-year KFRE risk exceeds {{c1::40%}} or eGFR is {{c2::15–20}} ml/min/1.73 m².
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 5, Figure 48 and Practice Points 5.4.1–5.4.3

text: FDA-approved pharmacotherapies for tobacco cessation include {{c1::nicotine replacement therapy}}, {{c2::bupropion SR}}, and {{c3::varenicline}}; electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) are NOT recommended for cessation.
extra: Section 4.5 Treatment of Tobacco Use; Table 8

text: Liraglutide for obesity is dosed at {{c1::3.0 mg SQ daily}} (maintenance) and produces a mean weight loss of ~{{c2::4.8%}} TBWL vs placebo.
Initial MMI dose is {{c1::30–40 mg/day}} when free T4 is {{c1::2–3× ULN}}.
extra: ATA 2016 — Initial MMI dosing by free T4 level

text: Warfarin target INR is {{c1::2.0–3.0}} generally; for a mechanical mitral valve or aortic mechanical valve with prior stroke/TIA, target INR is {{c2::2.5–3.5}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Thresholds & Doses section

text: After negative bidirectional endoscopy without an identified etiology, the AGA recommends {{c1::noninvasive H. pylori testing}} (stool antigen, urea breath test, or serology) and treatment if positive (conditional, low quality).
A normal CCTA (no plaque or stenosis) carries a warranty period of {{c1::2 years}}, while a normal stress test with adequate stress carries a warranty period of {{c2::1 year}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 5.1.2 / Figure 11

text: In acute decompensated HF, IV loop diuretic dose should be at least {{c1::double}} the patient's home oral daily dose; furosemide maximum is {{c2::600 mg/day}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Key Recommendations / Acute HF

text: Refractory status epilepticus is defined as seizures continuing after treatment with {{c1::a benzodiazepine plus one urgent-control AED}}, regardless of time elapsed.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Refractory SE section

text: Adults with incomplete or unknown polio vaccination status should complete a {{c1::3-dose IPV}} series.
extra: Notes — IPV vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text:
Platelet monitoring frequency in heparin-exposed patients:
HIT risk <0.1% → {{c1::no monitoring}}
HIT risk 0.1–1% → {{c2::every 2–3 days}}
HIT risk >1% → {{c3::at least every other day}}.
Metformin is recommended as first-line glycemic therapy in T2D with CKD when eGFR is ≥{{c1::30}} ml/min/1.73 m² (1B), and should be discontinued when eGFR falls below {{c1::30}}.
The 2021 USPSTF colorectal cancer screening recommendation excludes patients with prior CRC, {{c1::adenomatous polyps}}, {{c1::IBD}}, or known hereditary syndromes ({{c1::Lynch syndrome or FAP}}).

In an older bacteriuric patient with delirium or a fall but NO localizing genitourinary symptoms or systemic signs, the recommended approach is to {{c1::assess for other causes and observe}} rather than treating with antimicrobials.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section V (Older patients with delirium/falls)

text: Men with trichomoniasis are treated with {{c1::metronidazole 2 g orally as a single dose}}.
extra: Trichomoniasis, Recommended Regimens, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Cyclophosphamide dose should be reduced by {{c1::25%}} for age >60, by {{c1::50%}} for age >70, and for GFR <30 ml/min/1.73 m².
IV pulse methylprednisolone is reserved for GCA with {{c1::threatened vision loss}} (cranial ischemia), rather than used routinely.
In NSTE-ACS, immediate angiography within {{c1::2 hours}} is indicated for patients with hemodynamic or electrical instability.
