extra: Section 6 / Figure 5 — NSTE-ACS timing of invasive strategy by GRACE

text: CKD is diagnosed when eGFR is {{c1::<60 ml/min/1.73 m²}} or urine ACR is {{c2::≥30 mg/g}} (≥3 mg/mmol) for ≥3 months.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (CKD definition)

text: For nicotine replacement patch dosing, use {{c1::21 mg}} if smoking ≥10 cigarettes/day and {{c2::14 mg}} if smoking <10 cigarettes/day.
extra: Section 4.5 Treatment of Tobacco Use; Table 8

text: Long-term oxygen therapy (LTOT) is indicated for resting hypoxemia with PaO2 ≤{{c1::55 mmHg}} or SaO2 ≤{{c2::88%}}.
OCS for children 6–11 years with asthma exacerbation: prednisone/prednisolone {{c1::1–2 mg/kg/day (max 40 mg)}} for {{c2::3–5 days}}.
For children 6–11 years, GINA 2026 Step 1–2 reliever options now include as-needed {{c1::ICS-formoterol}} or as-needed {{c2::ICS+SABA}} (based on CARE study), reducing exacerbations vs SABA alone.
Evidence-based short-course antibiotic durations: uncomplicated cellulitis {{c1::5 days}}; acute pyelonephritis {{c2::7 days}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XIII, Recommendation 14 and Table 2

text: The perioperative glucose target after CABG is {{c1::<180 mg/dL}}, achieved via {{c2::continuous insulin infusion}}.
extra: Section 13.1 / Table 13 — perioperative insulin and sternal wound infection prevention

text: In uncomplicated asymptomatic IDA with negative bidirectional endoscopy, the AGA recommends a trial of {{c1::oral iron supplementation}} rather than routine {{c2::video capsule endoscopy}} (conditional, very low quality).
The recommended dose of plecanatide for IBS-C is {{c1::3 mg}} PO daily (NNT ~10).
extra: Recommendations on IBS-C secretagogues — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Dabigatran is dosed at {{c1::150 mg BID}} for CrCl >30 mL/min, {{c2::75 mg BID}} for CrCl 15–30 mL/min, and is contraindicated for CrCl <{{c3::15}} mL/min.
Tenecteplase for AIS is given as a single IV bolus of {{c1::0.25 mg/kg}}, with a maximum dose of {{c2::25 mg}}.
The 2019 ACR/AF guideline strongly recommends {{c1::tai chi}} and {{c2::self-efficacy/self-management programs}} for knee and/or hip OA.
extra: Table 1 / Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body approaches — tai chi and self-management

text: FDA-approved rituximab maintenance dosing for ANCA vasculitis is {{c1::500 mg}} IV every {{c2::6 months}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 1 (drug dosing regimens)

text: Immunosuppression in ANCA vasculitis should {{c1::not}} be dosed based on ANCA titers alone; treatment decisions require {{c2::clinical, laboratory, or imaging evidence}} of disease activity.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2 (GPA/MPA recommendations)

text: EDACS defines low risk as a score <{{c1::16}} with negative 0- and {{c2::2-hour}} hs-cTn and no ischemic ECG changes.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 4.1 and Table 6

text: First-line antihypertensive and antiproteinuric therapy in glomerular disease is {{c1::ACEi or ARB}} titrated to maximally tolerated dose, indicated for hypertension and/or proteinuria >{{c2::0.5 g/d}}.
Among all osteoporosis pharmacotherapies, bisphosphonates have the most favorable balance of {{c1::benefits, harms, and cost}}, supporting their use as first-line therapy in nearly all candidates.
extra: ACP 2023 Living Guideline (M22-1034) — full guideline text

text: In all patients presenting with syncope, the initial evaluation must include detailed history, physical examination, and {{c1::resting 12-lead ECG}}.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Section 2.3 and Figure 1

text: The combination of {{c1::ACEi + ARB + direct renin inhibitor (DRI)}} should be avoided in CKD.
In patients with PAD and type 2 diabetes, {{c1::GLP-1 agonists (liraglutide, semaglutide)}} or {{c2::SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin, canagliflozin, dapagliflozin)}} are recommended to reduce MACE.
extra: Key Recommendations — cardioprotective diabetes agents in PAD

text: ACR 2020 recommends switching {{c1::febuxostat}} to an alternative ULT in patients with a history of CVD or a new cardiovascular event while on that agent.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 4 — Febuxostat CVD switching

text: PEG (polyethylene glycol) is recommended {{c1::AGAINST}} as monotherapy for global IBS-C symptoms because it improves constipation but not {{c2::abdominal pain}}.

Preemptive antibiotics for animal bites consist of {{c1::amoxicillin-clavulanate}} for {{c2::3–5 days}}, indicated for immunocompromised, asplenic, advanced liver disease, hand/face injuries, or deep penetrating wounds.
To reduce nephrotoxicity, KDIGO 2012 recommends preferring {{c1::lipid formulations of amphotericin B}} over conventional amphotericin B (2A), or using {{c2::azoles or echinocandins}} instead (1A).
For Crohn's disease, azathioprine is dosed up to {{c1::2.5 mg/kg/day}} and 6-mercaptopurine up to {{c2::1.5 mg/kg/day}}; check {{c1::TPMT}} before initiation.
In ALI, Rutherford Class {{c1::III}} (irreversible ischemia) is managed with primary {{c2::amputation}}.
extra: Section 11, Figure 8 — ALI diagnosis and management algorithm

text: Untreated LTBI carries an estimated lifetime risk of progression to active TB of approximately {{c1::5–10%}}.
AED adverse-event incidence after a first unprovoked seizure ranges {{c1::7%–31%}}, and adverse effects are predominantly {{c2::mild and reversible}} (Level B).
extra: Risks of AED treatment section — AE incidence 7%–31%

text: In patients aged >{{c1::65}} years, a FIB-4 cutoff of >{{c2::2.0}} (rather than 1.3) should be used to define elevated risk.
For hospitalized COVID-19 patients on supplemental oxygen but not on mechanical ventilation (SpO2 ≤94% on room air), a {{c1::5-day}} remdesivir course is preferred over a {{c1::10-day}} course.
extra: Remdesivir for Severe or Critical COVID-19

text: For initial corticosteroid therapy in adults with ITP, use a course of {{c1::≤6 weeks}} (including taper) rather than a prolonged course (strong recommendation).
extra: Recommendation 3 — short (≤6 weeks) vs prolonged corticosteroid course in adults

text: In compensated cirrhosis with CSPH, the preferred NSBB to prevent first decompensation is {{c1::carvedilol}}, regardless of variceal status.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH?; Section: Compensated cirrhosis with proven or likely CSPH (PREDESCI-based recommendation)

text: Supervised exercise therapy (SET) for claudication: ≥{{c1::3}} sessions/week, {{c2::30–45}} minutes per session, for a minimum of {{c3::12 weeks}}, walking to moderate-to-maximum claudication pain.
A post-vaccination anti-HBs titer of ≥{{c1::10 mIU/mL}} is considered protective.
Lymphogranuloma venereum (LGV) is treated with {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg orally twice daily for 21 days}}.
Antimicrobials should be administered within {{c1::1 hour}} for septic shock or high-likelihood sepsis, and within {{c2::3 hours}} for possible sepsis without shock.
extra: Infection / Time to Antibiotics — Figure 1; SSC 2021

text: After taking lasmiditan, patients must not drive for {{c1::8 hours}} post-dose; it is classified as a {{c2::Schedule V}} controlled substance.
extra: AHS 2021 — Recently Approved Acute Treatments: lasmiditan

text: In nonneutropenic candidemia where the CVC is the presumed source, central venous catheters should be removed {{c1::early}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section II, Recommendation 13

text: Per AABB 2023, the {{c1::storage age}} of RBCs {{c2::need not be considered}} when making transfusion decisions.
extra: AABB 2023 — Good Practice Statement / Key Recommendations

text: In minor noncardioembolic AIS (NIHSS ≤3) or high-risk TIA (ABCD2 ≥4), DAPT with clopidogrel + aspirin is given for {{c1::21 days}} followed by single antiplatelet therapy, starting within {{c2::24 hours}}.
extra: Section 4.8 and Table 9 — CHANCE, POINT, THALES, INSPIRES trials

text: In septic shock, vasopressin should be added when the norepinephrine dose reaches {{c1::0.25–0.5 µg/kg/min}}.
extra: Hemodynamic Management / Vasoactive Agents — Figure 2; SSC 2021

text: Per ATA 2015 sonographic pattern-based FNA framework, the size threshold for FNA of a high-suspicion nodule is {{c1::≥1 cm}}.
Test-and-treat for uninvestigated dyspepsia is appropriate in patients aged {{c1::<60 yr}} without alarm features (consider {{c2::<50 yr}} in higher-risk populations).
Nirmatrelvir/ritonavir for eGFR <30 mL/min (including hemodialysis): {{c1::300 mg/100 mg on day 1}}, then {{c1::150 mg/100 mg once daily on days 2–5}}.
The 2021 AHA/ACC guideline recommends abandoning the descriptor '{{c1::atypical}}' chest pain; symptoms should instead be classified as {{c2::cardiac, possible cardiac, or noncardiac}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: Cellulitis prophylaxis regimens include oral {{c1::penicillin}} or {{c1::erythromycin}} BID, or IM {{c2::benzathine penicillin}} every 2–4 weeks.
For IBS symptoms, {{c1::soluble, viscous, poorly fermentable fiber (psyllium/ispaghula)}} is preferred over {{c2::insoluble bran}}.
extra: Recommendation on fiber — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: An HV interval ≥{{c1::70 ms}} predicts high-grade AV block in patients with myotonic dystrophy and bundle branch block.
extra: Section 2.2/Table 3 — definitions of SND, AV block grades, and conduction disorders

text: Plasma exchange should be added in AAV when SCr exceeds {{c1::5.7 mg/dl (500 µmol/l)}}, dialysis is required, creatinine is rapidly rising, or there is diffuse alveolar hemorrhage with hypoxemia.
The 2021 USPSTF requires a smoking history of ≥{{c1::20}} pack-years as part of the LDCT lung cancer screening eligibility criteria.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — eligibility criteria; Clinician Summary 'What's new?'

text: The USPSTF upgraded PSA screening for ages 55–69 from Grade {{c1::D}} (2012) to Grade {{c2::C}} (2018), based on longer ERSPC follow-up showing reduced prostate cancer mortality and increased use of active surveillance.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation — USPSTF 2018

text: In adult hematology/oncology inpatients, AABB 2023 recommends RBC transfusion at Hgb <{{c1::7 g/dL}} ({{c2::conditional}} recommendation).
In patients on carvedilol who develop systolic BP <{{c1::90 mm Hg}}, down-titrate or switch to {{c2::propranolol or nadolol}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH?

text: Benralizumab (≥12 years) is given 30 mg SC q4 weeks for the first {{c1::3 doses}}, then every {{c2::8 weeks}}.
When dolutegravir is co-administered with rifampin-based TB regimens, the dose is {{c1::50 mg twice daily}}; with 3HP preventive therapy, the dose is {{c2::50 mg once daily}}.
In Brugada syndrome, options for VF storm or recurrent ICD shocks include {{c1::quinidine}} and {{c1::epicardial RVOT ablation}}.
Epinephrine injection (1:10,000 dilution) should {{c1::never be used as monotherapy}}; it must always be combined with {{c2::a second hemostatic modality}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Choice of endoscopic hemostatic therapy / Table 7

text: For device-detected atrial high-rate episodes, oral anticoagulation is reasonable if episode duration is ≥{{c1::24 hours}} and CHA2DS2-VASc is ≥{{c2::2}}.
extra: Section 6.4.1 and Figure 12 — device-detected AHRE management

text: SGLT2 inhibitors should be {{c1::stopped on admission}} and not re-initiated during hospitalization; they are contraindicated in {{c2::type 1 diabetes}}.
Adults aged 19–64 should receive {{c1::≥1 dose}} of the 2024–2025 COVID-19 vaccine.
extra: Notes — COVID-19 vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Chronic colchicine after MI is dosed at {{c1::0.5–0.6 mg once or twice daily}} and is contraindicated if CrCl <{{c2::15 mL/min}}.
The TSH target for treated congenital hypothyroidism is {{c1::0.5–2.0 mIU/L}}; TSH and T4 should be monitored every {{c2::1–2 months}} in the first year of life.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 6c; Thresholds & Doses — Pediatric monitoring

text: Hypogonadism diagnosis requires BOTH {{c1::symptoms/signs of testosterone deficiency}} AND {{c2::unequivocally and consistently low serum testosterone}} on repeat morning fasting measurements.
A baseline hematocrit >{{c1::48%}} (>{{c2::50%}} at altitude) is a relative contraindication to starting testosterone therapy.
After an unprovoked first seizure, recurrence risk is approximately {{c1::32%}} at 1 year and {{c2::46%}} at 5 years.
extra: Table 1 — Pooled recurrence rates from Class I/II studies

text: Febrile neutropenia with SSTI: hospitalize and start {{c1::vancomycin}} PLUS an {{c2::antipseudomonal beta-lactam}} (cefepime, carbapenem, or piperacillin-tazobactam).
After ICD implantation for syncope with LVEF <35% and presumed arrhythmic etiology, a {{c1::3-month}} symptom-free driving restriction applies.

Transition from echinocandin to fluconazole in candidemia is appropriate after {{c1::5–7 days}} if the patient is clinically stable, the isolate is fluconazole-susceptible, and repeat blood cultures are negative.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section I, Recommendations 1–12; Thresholds & Doses

text: Prophylactic antibiotics {{c1::should NOT}} be used in sterile necrosis or predicted severe acute pancreatitis; antibiotics are reserved for {{c2::proven or strongly suspected infected necrosis}}.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Role of Antibiotics

text: KDIGO 2021 recommends avoiding {{c1::combination ACEi + ARB + direct renin inhibitor}} (in any combination) in CKD with or without diabetes (1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.3.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD (ONTARGET, VA-NEPHRON-D, ALTITUDE)

text: In IgAN, a 6-month glucocorticoid course is considered only if proteinuria remains >{{c1::0.75–1 g/d}} despite ≥{{c2::90 days}} of optimized supportive care.
For active non-severe GPA, the preferred induction regimen is {{c1::methotrexate}} plus glucocorticoids (over cyclophosphamide, rituximab, azathioprine, MMF, or TMP-SMX).
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2 (GPA/MPA recommendations)

text: In stable patients with suspected acute aortic syndrome, the diagnostic test of choice is {{c1::CT angiography}}; chest x-ray {{c2::cannot rule it out}}.
Esophageal candidiasis always requires systemic therapy: oral fluconazole {{c1::200–400 mg}} daily for {{c2::14–21 days}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section XV–XVII, Recommendations 115–140

text: Biologics (dupilumab or mepolizumab) are considered when exacerbations persist on triple therapy with blood eosinophils ≥{{c1::300}} cells/µL.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.11 — Biologic therapy evidence (BOREAS/NOTUS; MATINEE)

text: Ustekinumab for UC: induction ~{{c1::6 mg/kg IV}}, then {{c2::90 mg SC every 8 weeks}} maintenance.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: First-episode genital herpes antiviral therapy (acyclovir, valacyclovir, or famciclovir) is given for {{c1::7–10 days}}.
extra: Diseases Characterized by Genital Ulcers — Genital Herpes, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Peppermint oil has an NNT of {{c1::3}} for global IBS symptoms and {{c2::4}} for abdominal pain specifically.
HPV vaccination is recommended starting at age {{c1::11–12 years}} (may start as early as age 9), with catch-up vaccination through age {{c2::26}}.
extra: Human Papillomavirus Infections, Prevention — ACIP HPV vaccine recommendations, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: High-dose insulin for beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker overdose: {{c1::1 unit/kg IV bolus}}, then {{c2::0.5 units/kg/h}} infusion.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: Children with PAI are treated with hydrocortisone ~{{c1::8 mg/m²/day}} BSA in {{c2::3–4 divided doses}}; long-acting synthetic glucocorticoids should be avoided.
Preferred initial ART regimens for most people with HIV are {{c1::BIC/TAF/FTC}} or {{c2::dolutegravir + TXF/XTC}}.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 2 — recommended initial ART regimens

text: Preferred first-line systemic therapy for advanced HCC (Child-Pugh A, ECOG 0–1) is {{c1::atezolizumab plus bevacizumab}} or {{c2::durvalumab plus tremelimumab}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Figure 16 / Tables 7–8

text: The recommended validated depression screening tool for older adults (≥65) is the {{c1::GDS (Geriatric Depression Scale)}}.
KDIGO 2021 recommends {{c1::standardized office BP measurement}} (rather than routine office BP) for managing high BP in adults with CKD (1B).
extra: Chapter 1, Recommendation 1.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::against}} extending pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis (LMWH or DOAC) beyond {{c2::hospital discharge}} in acutely or critically ill medical patients (strong recommendation).

AAN 2018 recommends counseling women to {{c1::stop DMT before conception}} for planned pregnancies; DMT should not be started or continued during pregnancy unless MS disease activity risk outweighs fetal risk (Level B).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Recommendation 10

text: The BP treatment target in adults who meet criteria for antihypertensive therapy per the 2019 ACC/AHA primary prevention guideline is {{c1::<130/80}} mm Hg.
extra: Section 4.4 Adults With High Blood Pressure; Figure 4; Table 7

text: After RAI therapy, women should delay pregnancy for at least {{c1::6 months}}; men should wait {{c2::3–4 months}}.
extra: Recommendations 8–10 (Section D2) — radiation safety after RAI

text: In AF with HFrEF, catheter ablation is {{c1::Class 1}} to improve symptoms, LVEF, and reduce {{c2::mortality and HF hospitalization}}.
Non-heparin anticoagulants acceptable for acute HIT include {{c1::argatroban}}, {{c1::bivalirudin}}, {{c1::danaparoid}}, {{c1::fondaparinux}}, and DOACs (rivaroxaban preferred among DOACs).
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: Ticagrelor dosing in ACS: loading dose {{c1::180 mg}}, then {{c2::90 mg twice daily}} maintenance.
HFNO can deliver flow rates up to {{c1::60 L/min}} and generates approximately {{c2::3–5 cmH2O}} of PEEP.
Long-acting injectable {{c1::cabotegravir + rilpivirine}} is offered to patients with difficulty maintaining {{c2::daily oral adherence}} or a strong preference for injectable therapy.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 3 — switching ART in virologic suppression

text: For refractory GERD with objective evidence, baclofen can be used at {{c1::5-20 mg}} {{c2::three times daily (TID)}}.
In children aged 4–17, an oral bisphosphonate is recommended if an osteoporotic fracture occurs and the GC dose is ≥{{c1::0.1 mg/kg/day}} for ≥3 months.

For women aged 30–65, three USPSTF-approved screening strategies (all Grade A) are:
1. Cytology alone every {{c1::3}} years
2. Primary hrHPV testing alone every {{c2::5}} years
3. hrHPV/cytology cotesting every {{c2::5}} years.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: The minimum 10-year CVD risk threshold to consider aspirin for primary prevention (ages 40–59) is {{c1::≥10%}}, with greater benefit at risk {{c2::>15–20%}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table; Table. Summary of USPSTF Rationale — USPSTF 2022

text: Routine periodic {{c1::anatomic or ischemic testing}} is NOT recommended in stable CCD patients without a change in clinical or functional status.
extra: Section 7.1 Follow-Up Plan — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Per US FDA labeling, {{c1::tofacitinib}} and {{c2::upadacitinib}} should be positioned after {{c3::anti-TNF failure}} in UC.
Entecavir is dosed at {{c1::0.5 mg}} PO daily in treatment-naïve patients, and {{c2::1 mg}} PO daily in lamivudine-experienced patients or those with decompensated cirrhosis.
In adults with high BP and non-dialysis CKD, KDIGO 2021 targets SBP {{c1::<120 mm Hg}} using standardized office BP measurement, when tolerated (2B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.1.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD (based on SPRINT)

text: Additional MDR VAP risk factors (beyond prior IV antibiotics) include: ≥{{c1::5 days}} hospitalization before VAP onset, {{c2::septic shock}} at VAP onset, ARDS preceding VAP, and acute renal replacement therapy before VAP.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Table 2, MDR VAP risk factors

text: EOLIA criteria for VV-ECMO referral include: PaO2/FiO2 <{{c1::50 mmHg}} for >3 h, OR PaO2/FiO2 <{{c1::80 mmHg}} for >6 h, OR pH <{{c1::7.25}} with PaCO2 ≥60 mmHg for >6 h (with RR up to 35 and Pplat ≤32 cmH2O).
In PMR, {{c1::single}} daily oral glucocorticoid dosing is preferred over divided doses, except for prominent night pain at doses {{c2::<5 mg/day}}.
Immunoglobulin supplementation ({{c1::400–800 mg/kg/month}} IV) should be considered in rituximab-maintained ANCA vasculitis patients with IgG {{c2::<3 g/L}} and recurrent severe infections.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 1 & Table 2 (GPA/MPA recommendations)

text: Voclosporin is dosed at {{c1::23.7 mg orally twice daily}} when added to MMF and glucocorticoids for lupus nephritis.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Treatment of Class III/IV LN; Thresholds & Doses

text: High-intensity statin therapy (targeting LDL-C reduction ≥{{c1::50%}}) is indicated for primary severe hypercholesterolemia defined as LDL-C ≥{{c2::190 mg/dL}}, without requiring formal risk calculation.
extra: Section 4.3 Adults With High Blood Cholesterol; Figure 3

text: In the saline infusion test (2 L 0.9% NaCl over 4 h), a post-infusion PAC >{{c1::10 ng/dL}} confirms PA, while <{{c2::5 ng/dL}} excludes it.
In stage A HF patients with CVD risk ≥10%, the blood pressure target is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Thresholds & Doses / Stage A

text: In acutely or critically ill medical patients, {{c1::pharmacologic}} prophylaxis is preferred over {{c1::mechanical}} prophylaxis when bleeding risk is acceptable.
UNOS downstaging (UNOS-DS) criteria include: 1 lesion {{c1::5.1–8 cm}}; OR 2–3 lesions each <5 cm with total ≤{{c2::8 cm}}; OR 4–5 lesions each <3 cm with total ≤{{c2::8 cm}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Liver Transplantation / Table 3 / Figure 12

text: Target INR for mechanical AVR with no risk factors is {{c1::2.5}} (range {{c2::2.0–3.0}}).
HBV antiviral prophylaxis should continue for ≥{{c1::6 months}} after completion of immunosuppression, extended to ≥{{c2::12 months}} after anti-CD20 therapy.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Section 6D & Guidance Statements on Immunosuppression

text: Corticosteroid benefit in severe CAP is strongest when administered within {{c1::24 hours}} of meeting severe CAP criteria; benefit is weaker if started {{c2::>72 hours}} from severe CAP onset.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 4

text: Colorectal cancer surveillance colonoscopy in Crohn's colitis should begin after {{c1::8 years}} of disease with ≥{{c2::30%}} colonic involvement, or at {{c1::diagnosis}} if PSC is present.
extra: Colorectal cancer surveillance — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: The USPSTF 2021 endorses the {{c1::Lung-RADS}} classification system for nodule reporting to reduce false-positive rates.
When initiating ULT, concomitant anti-inflammatory prophylaxis (colchicine, NSAID, or low-dose glucocorticoid) is strongly recommended for at least {{c1::3–6 months}}, extended if flares continue.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2 — Anti-inflammatory prophylaxis duration

text: A fasting lipid panel should be obtained {{c1::4–8 weeks}} after starting or adjusting lipid-lowering therapy in ACS patients.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Top Take-Home Messages

text: In Brugada syndrome, ICD implantation is indicated (Class I) for spontaneous type 1 ECG pattern plus {{c1::prior SCA or syncope}}.
Genetic testing is indicated in TAD patients with syndromic features, family history of TAD, or onset before age {{c1::60}}; a {{c2::multigene HTAD panel}} should be used.
Hyponatremia is classified as acute vs. chronic based on a duration threshold of {{c1::48 hours}}.
DTG/3TC should be avoided if HIV RNA is ≥{{c1::500,000}} copies/mL or CD4 is <{{c2::200}}/μL.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 2 — recommended initial ART regimens

text: Traditional cardiovascular risk factors should be reassessed every {{c1::4–6}} years in adults aged ≥{{c2::20}} years.
extra: Section 2.2 Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk; Table 3

text: Voclosporin for lupus nephritis requires a baseline eGFR of ≥{{c1::45 ml/min/1.73 m²}}.
Track 1 for adults/adolescents uses AIR-only (as-needed ICS-formoterol) at Steps {{c1::1–2}} and ICS-formoterol MART at Steps {{c2::3–5}}.
After IVT for AIS, blood pressure should be maintained below {{c1::<180/105 mm Hg}} for the first {{c2::24 hours}}.
extra: Section 4.3 — Blood Pressure Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: Caspofungin dosing for invasive candidiasis: {{c1::70 mg}} loading dose, then {{c2::50 mg}} IV daily.
Tight glycemic control with plasma glucose <{{c1::110 mg/dL}} should be avoided in critically ill patients due to risk of {{c2::hypoglycemia}} (2C).
In stable patients with infected necrotizing pancreatitis, necrosectomy should be delayed {{c1::≥4 weeks (≥30 days)}} from admission until the collection is walled off.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Surgery; Thresholds & Doses

text: In asymptomatic patients with SCD, routine {{c1::echocardiography}} to screen for pulmonary hypertension is {{c2::not recommended}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Hypertension: Screening

text: Nonsevere CDI is defined as WBC ≤{{c1::15,000 cells/µL}} AND serum creatinine <{{c2::1.5 mg/dL}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: For RRT anticoagulation in patients without bleeding risk: use {{c1::heparin (UFH or LMWH)}} for IHD (1C) and {{c2::regional citrate}} for CRRT (2B).
Testosterone therapy is contraindicated in men who have had an MI or stroke within the past {{c1::6 months}}.
Cirrhotic hyponatremia is graded as: mild {{c1::126–135}}, moderate {{c2::120–125}}, and severe {{c3::<120}} mEq/L.
extra: Hyponatremia section — graded management thresholds; AASLD 2021

text: For standardized office BP, the cuff bladder must encircle ≥{{c1::80%}} of the arm circumference, and the result is the average of ≥{{c2::2}} readings on ≥{{c2::2}} occasions.
extra: Chapter 1, Recommendation 1.1 and Figure 2 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Immediate AED treatment after a first unprovoked seizure {{c1::does not improve}} long-term (>3 years) prognosis for sustained seizure remission (Level B).
extra: Table 3 — 2-year seizure remission rates with immediate vs deferred AED treatment over >3 years

text: Baricitinib for severe/critical COVID-19: {{c1::4 mg PO daily}} (renally adjusted) for up to {{c2::14 days}} or until hospital discharge.
If FIB-4 is ≥1.3, secondary risk assessment with {{c1::VCTE, MRE, or ELF}} is indicated.
CHEST 2021 defines the treatment phase of anticoagulation for acute VTE as {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}}, after which all patients should be reassessed for extended-phase therapy.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #21–27; Thresholds & Doses

text: The risk of a second primary malignancy significantly increases with cumulative 131I activity >{{c1::500–600 mCi}}.
IDSA defines severe COVID-19 as SpO2 {{c1::≤94%}} on room air, and critical COVID-19 as requiring {{c2::mechanical ventilation, ECMO, high-flow nasal cannula, or non-invasive ventilation}}.
For severe nonpurulent cellulitis or suspected necrotizing infection, give empiric {{c1::vancomycin}} PLUS {{c2::piperacillin-tazobactam}} (alternatives: carbapenem, or ceftriaxone + metronidazole).
Women who have received HPV vaccination should {{c1::continue cervical cancer screening}} per standard age-based recommendations.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Patient Population Under Consideration — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: CNI trough targets in FSGS: cyclosporine {{c1::100–175 ng/ml}}; tacrolimus {{c2::5–10 ng/ml}}; treatment continued ≥12 months if responsive.
ApoB should be measured in patients on LLT with ASCVD, CKM syndrome, T2DM, or elevated TG once LDL-C/non-HDL-C goals are achieved, to detect {{c1::residual atherogenic particle burden}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.7 — Primary prevention algorithm; apoB use in LLT monitoring

text: In patients with embolic stroke of undetermined source (ESUS), empiric {{c1::anticoagulation}} is NOT recommended, based on the negative {{c2::NAVIGATE ESUS and RESPECT ESUS}} trials.
In IgA-deficient patients with suspected celiac disease, use {{c1::IgG-based testing (DGP IgG and/or TTG IgG)}} for screening.
Cryptococcal antigen (CrAg) screening is recommended when CD4 is ≤{{c1::100}} cells/μL.
Pregnant women with syphilis who have a penicillin allergy must undergo {{c1::penicillin desensitization}}; there is no acceptable penicillin substitute in pregnancy.
extra: Management of Persons Who Have a History of Penicillin Allergy; Syphilis in Pregnancy, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Primary, secondary, or early latent syphilis is treated with {{c1::benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM as a single dose}}.
extra: Syphilis — Primary/Secondary/Latent sections, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: For the treatment phase of acute VTE (DVT or PE), CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::DOACs (apixaban, dabigatran, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban)}} over {{c2::VKA}} (strong, moderate-certainty).
After starting or escalating an ACEi or ARB in SCD, GFR and serum {{c1::potassium}} should be checked within {{c2::1 week}}.
Bicarbonate is indicated only if pH is {{c1::<7.0}}; give {{c2::100 mmol NaHCO₃ in 400 mL sterile water}} every 2 h until pH >7.0.
extra: Section 4 (Recommended Treatment); Thresholds & Doses — Bicarbonate indication

text: Adults with newly diagnosed ITP and platelet count <{{c1::20}} ×10⁹/L should be admitted; established adult ITP with the same platelet count can be managed as {{c2::outpatient}}.
extra: Recommendations 2a–2b — inpatient vs outpatient management thresholds in adults

text: For primary hypothyroidism, target serum TSH within {{c1::0.4–4.0 mIU/L}}; do not adjust dose based on symptoms alone when TSH is in this range.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 1b; Thresholds & Doses — Adult TSH target

text: Acute pancreatitis is diagnosed when {{c1::2 of 3}} criteria are present: characteristic abdominal pain, lipase >3× ULN, or characteristic imaging findings.
After successful EVT recanalization (mTICI 2b/2c/3), targeting SBP {{c1::<140 mm Hg}} is HARMFUL and should be avoided; maintain SBP {{c1::140–180 mm Hg}} for the first 72 hours.
extra: Section 4.3, COR 3 Harm — intensive BP post-EVT

text: Atropine should be avoided in post-heart transplant patients because it can cause {{c1::paradoxical AV block}}.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: In PsA, {{c1::biologic monotherapy}} is generally preferred over biologic + MTX combination; however, continuing MTX with a monoclonal antibody TNFi is recommended to reduce {{c2::immunogenicity}}.
In adults, a restrictive versus liberal RBC transfusion strategy reduced transfusion exposure by {{c1::32.4%}} absolute (RR {{c2::0.60}}, 95% CI 0.54–0.66) per AABB 2023 meta-analysis.
extra: AABB 2023 — Table 2, adult summary of findings

text: In AIS with atrial fibrillation, early initiation of oral anticoagulation within {{c1::4 days}} for minor to moderate stroke is reasonable and safe.
extra: Key Recommendations — anticoagulation in AF-related AIS

text: Preferred first-line therapies for immune-active chronic hepatitis B are {{c1::peg-IFN}}, {{c2::entecavir}}, {{c3::TDF}}, and {{c4::TAF}}; lamivudine, adefovir, and telbivudine should be avoided as initial therapy.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Figure 1 / Treatment Algorithms

text: Risk factors that favor adding bezlotoxumab to SOC for CDI include age ≥{{c1::65}} years, {{c2::immunocompromise}}, and severe CDI on presentation.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Recommendation III; Bezlotoxumab Implementation Considerations

text: PMBC is first-line for symptomatic severe rheumatic MS (MVA ≤{{c1::1.5 cm²}}) with favorable valve morphology and no {{c2::LA thrombus}} or >mild MR.
Physician advice increases tobacco cessation at ≥6 months to {{c1::8.0%}} vs 4.8% for control (RR {{c2::1.76}}).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Benefits of Tobacco Cessation Interventions — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: A significant brain-imaging abnormality increases recurrence risk after a first unprovoked seizure with an HR of {{c1::2.44}} (95% CI 1.09–5.44) at 1–4 years (Level B).
extra: Analysis of Evidence: Risk of seizure recurrence — imaging abnormality

text: In all patients with acute pancreatitis, {{c1::transabdominal ultrasound}} should be obtained to assess for a {{c2::biliary}} etiology; repeat in 1 week if initially inconclusive.
The 2021 USPSTF recommends annual LDCT lung cancer screening for adults aged {{c1::50–80}} years.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — eligibility criteria; Clinician Summary 'What's new?'

text: At the time of EGPA diagnosis, an {{c1::echocardiogram}} should be obtained, and the {{c2::Five Factor Score}} should be used to guide therapy.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 13–14 (EGPA); Table 3

text: Acceptable primary screening modalities per the 2024 USPSTF include {{c1::digital mammography}} and {{c1::digital breast tomosynthesis (DBT)}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening Tests — digital mammography and DBT both acceptable

text: To counter underreporting of tobacco use in pregnancy, clinicians should use {{c1::multiple-choice questions}} rather than simple yes/no questions to assess smoking status.
extra: Practice Considerations: Pregnant Persons — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: Intermittent high-dose PPI post-hemostasis: {{c1::40 mg PO/IV 2–4 times daily}} for 3 days (total ≥80 mg/day), with an optional {{c2::80 mg initial bolus}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Antisecretory therapy after endoscopic hemostatic therapy

text: Initiate antihypertensive therapy at BP ≥{{c1::130/80}} mm Hg in adults with clinical ASCVD, diabetes, CKD, or 10-year ASCVD risk ≥{{c2::10%}}.
extra: Section 4.4 Adults With High Blood Pressure; Figure 4; Table 7

text: The USPSTF 2021 recommends screening with LDCT every {{c1::1 year (annually)}} rather than biennially, based on CISNET modeling showing greater mortality benefit.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening Intervals — annual LDCT preferred over biennial per CISNET modeling

text: RAS inhibitor therapy should be continued unless serum creatinine rises by more than {{c1::30%}} within {{c2::4 weeks}} of initiation or dose increase.
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendations 3.2.1–3.2.3 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Decompensated cirrhosis — an exclusion criterion for simplified HCV treatment — is defined by the presence of {{c1::ascites}}, {{c1::encephalopathy}}, {{c1::jaundice}}, or {{c1::varices}}.
extra: Initial Visit algorithm — exclusion criteria triggering specialist referral

text: Piperacillin-tazobactam for HAP/VAP is dosed at {{c1::4.5 g IV q6h}}; extended infusion is appropriate.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3

text: KDIGO 2017 recommends {{c1::against}} the routine use of calcitriol or vitamin D analogs in CKD G3a–G5 patients not on dialysis, reserving them for severe, progressive hyperparathyroidism in CKD G4–G5.
When baricitinib and tocilizumab are unavailable, suggested alternative immunomodulators (added to corticosteroids) for severe/critical COVID-19 are {{c1::abatacept}} or {{c2::infliximab}}.
Patients <{{c1::35}} years with spontaneous hypokalemia, marked aldosterone excess, and a unilateral CT adenoma may proceed to adrenalectomy {{c2::without AVS}}.
Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) is recommended {{c1::against}} for IBS {{c2::outside of clinical trials}}.
For remission maintenance in severe GPA/MPA after induction, {{c1::rituximab}} is preferred over methotrexate or azathioprine, using {{c2::scheduled re-dosing}} rather than ANCA/CD19-guided dosing.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 9–14 (GPA/MPA); Table 2

text: USPSTF recommends against screening women older than 65 (Grade D) if they have {{c1::adequate prior screening}} and are {{c2::not otherwise high risk}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table; Clinical Considerations: Women Older Than 65 Years — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: Transplant candidacy is suggested by peak VO₂ {{c1::≤14 mL/kg/min}} (or {{c1::≤12 mL/kg/min}} on beta blocker), or 6-minute walk distance {{c2::<300 m}}.
The ACG 2023 duodenal biopsy protocol for celiac disease requires {{c1::1-2}} specimens from the duodenal bulb (at {{c2::9 or 12 o'clock}}) and ≥4 from the distal duodenum.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diagnosis: Evidence and rationale

text: Tocilizumab for severe/critical COVID-19: {{c1::8 mg/kg IV (max 800 mg)}} as a single infusion; may repeat once after ≥{{c2::8 hours}}.
extra: Recommendation: Baricitinib vs. Tocilizumab (10/14/2025); RECOVERY trial CRP threshold ≥75 mg/L

text: Low-dose colchicine {{c1::0.5 mg daily}} may be considered for MACE reduction in select CCD patients; it should be avoided if eGFR is {{c2::<30 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
extra: Section 4.3.4 Colchicine (LoDoCo2, COLCOT) — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Spironolactone for ascites is started at {{c1::100 mg/day}} and titrated to a maximum of {{c2::400 mg/day}}.
Apixaban dose is reduced from 5 mg BID to {{c1::2.5 mg BID}} if ≥{{c2::2}} of the following are present: age ≥80, weight ≤60 kg, SCr ≥1.5 mg/dL.
For NIV delivery in AHRF, a {{c1::helmet}} interface may be preferable to a face mask, though it requires expertise to manage patient–ventilator synchrony (expert opinion).
HPV vaccination is routinely recommended through age {{c1::26}}; for ages {{c2::27–45}}, shared clinical decision-making is used.
extra: ACIP Shared Clinical Decision-Making Recommendations; Table 1, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: After a large cardioembolic stroke (NIHSS >{{c1::15}} or large infarct territory), anticoagulation should be delayed approximately {{c2::14 days}}; TIA patients may start earlier.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Thresholds & Doses section

text: Steady-state TSH is reached approximately {{c1::6 weeks}} after a LT4 dose change; TSH should be rechecked {{c2::4–6 weeks}} after any adjustment.
MRI of the spine/pelvis should be obtained when disease activity is {{c1::unclear}} in a patient on a biologic, but should {{c2::not}} be obtained to confirm inactivity in clinically stable patients.
Dense breasts occur in approximately {{c1::40-50%}} of screened women; the FDA mandated breast density notification starting {{c2::September 2024}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — dense breasts prevalence and FDA notification

text: Low-risk acute chest pain is defined as <{{c1::1%}} 30-day risk of death or MACE; these patients should be {{c2::discharged with outpatient follow-up}} rather than undergo urgent stress testing or cardiac imaging.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 4.1.1 and Table 8

text: Adults without evidence of immunity to varicella should receive {{c1::2 doses}} of varicella vaccine, especially those born in {{c2::1980 or later}}.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — Varicella, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: In AVH, IV {{c1::ceftriaxone 1 g}} every 24 hours (up to 5 days) is the recommended antibiotic prophylaxis.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Table 5

text: RSV vaccination is recommended for COPD patients aged ≥{{c1::50}} years with risk factors, or universally at age ≥{{c2::75}} years.
In primary prevention adults at borderline or intermediate ASCVD risk, the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<100 mg/dL}} and the non–HDL-C goal is {{c2::<130 mg/dL}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — Primary prevention LDL-C goals

text: POTS is diagnosed by a heart rate increase of ≥{{c1::30 bpm}} (≥{{c2::40 bpm}} in ages 12–19 years) within 10 minutes of standing, without orthostatic hypotension.
The gold-standard test for primary adrenal insufficiency uses {{c1::250 μg}} IV corticotropin (standard-dose), measuring cortisol at {{c2::30 or 60}} minutes.
PAD screening is recommended for: age ≥{{c1::65}}; age {{c2::50–64}} with cardiovascular risk factors; or age <50 with {{c3::diabetes plus ≥1 additional risk factor}}.
Severe MR is defined by vena contracta ≥{{c1::0.7 cm}}, regurgitant volume ≥{{c2::60 mL}}, regurgitant fraction ≥50%, or ERO ≥{{c3::0.40 cm²}}.
The 2026 ACC/AHA guideline recommends measuring Lp(a) {{c1::at least once}} in every adult for ASCVD risk assessment.
extra: Section 3.4 / Table 4 — Lp(a) measurement and risk stratification

text: In children with newly diagnosed ITP and no or mild (skin-only) bleeding, the recommended management is {{c1::observation}} as outpatient, regardless of platelet count (strong recommendation against IVIG or anti-D).
extra: Recommendations 10–13 — outpatient management and observation in children

text: By transient elastography, an LSM of {{c1::≥15 kPa}} rules IN cACLD (compensated advanced chronic liver disease).
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Stages of cirrhosis; LSM cutoffs

text: Etomidate for urgent hypercortisolism is given as a loading bolus of {{c1::3–5 mg}}, then a continuous infusion of {{c2::0.03–0.10 mg/kg/h}}, titrating serum cortisol to 10–20 µg/dL checked every 4–6 h.
When endoscopic therapy fails to achieve hemostasis, the preferred salvage intervention is {{c1::transcatheter arterial embolization (TAE)}} rather than surgery.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Failure of endoscopic therapy section (Tarasconi meta-analysis; Sverden cohort)

text: In AF, oral anticoagulation is {{c1::reasonable}} (intermediate risk, ~1–2%/year) when CHA2DS2-VASc is {{c2::1}} in men or {{c2::2}} in women.
extra: Section 6.1 and Table 8 — CHA2DS2-VASc risk thresholds

text: For STEMI presenting to a PCI-capable hospital, the goal FMC-to-device time is ≤{{c1::90 minutes}}.
In the WHO pulmonary hypertension classification, SCD-associated PH falls under Group {{c1::5}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — PAH Treatment / Figure 2

text: In patients with HIT requiring RRT, anticoagulation should use {{c1::direct thrombin inhibitors or Factor Xa inhibitors}} instead of heparin (1A).
LTOT is also indicated when PaO2 is {{c1::55–60 mmHg}} with evidence of cor pulmonale, pulmonary hypertension, or hematocrit >{{c2::55%}}.
The 2022 guideline issues {{c1::no treatment recommendation}} for pirfenidone in PPF, citing insufficient evidence and recommending {{c2::further research}} before routine use.
extra: Part II Evidence-based Recommendations — pirfenidone research recommendation for PPF

text: Systemic corticosteroid treatment for a COPD exacerbation is {{c1::prednisone 40 mg/day × 5 days}}.

In nonneutropenic candidemic patients, a dilated ophthalmologic exam should be performed {{c1::within the first week}} after diagnosis; in neutropenic patients, delay until {{c2::neutrophil recovery}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section I, Recommendations 1–12; Section III, Recommendations 14–23

text: First-line antihypertensive drug classes are {{c1::thiazide-type diuretics}}, {{c1::long-acting dihydropyridine CCBs}}, {{c1::ACE inhibitors}}, and {{c1::ARBs}}; beta-blockers are NOT first-line unless a compelling indication (CHD, HF) exists.
HEART score risk stratification: ≤{{c1::3}} = low risk; {{c2::4–6}} = intermediate risk; {{c3::7–10}} = high risk.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 4.1 and Table 6

text: CHEST 2021 recommends offering extended-phase anticoagulation with a DOAC for {{c1::unprovoked VTE}} or VTE associated with a {{c2::persistent risk factor}} (strong, moderate-certainty).
A minor transient risk factor (e.g., surgery <30 min, estrogen use, pregnancy) must occur within {{c1::2 months}} before VTE to qualify as a provoking factor.
Prednisone induction for Crohn's disease: {{c1::40–60 mg/day}} for 1–2 weeks, then taper {{c2::5 mg/week}} to 20 mg, then 2.5–5 mg/week; do not exceed {{c1::60 mg/day}} or 3 months total.
Mirikizumab induction: {{c1::300 mg IV every 4 weeks × 3 doses}}; maintenance: {{c2::200 mg SC every 4 weeks}}.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: For second-line SIAD therapy, the US guideline permits {{c1::vaptans, demeclocycline, or urea}}, whereas the European guideline recommends {{c2::urea or loop diuretics plus oral NaCl}} and recommends against vaptans and demeclocycline.
extra: Table 2; Vaptans section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017

text: Urgent ERCP (within 24 hours) in gallstone pancreatitis is reserved for patients with {{c1::cholangitis}} or {{c1::progressive cholestasis (bilirubin >3–5 mg/dL)}}, not for predicted severe AP alone.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — ERCP in AP; Schepers/APEC trial

text: RAI remnant ablation should {{c1::not}} be routinely given for ATA {{c2::low-risk}} DTC, including unifocal or multifocal papillary microcarcinoma.
Fluconazole prophylaxis in ICU patients is considered when the unit's invasive candidiasis rate exceeds {{c1::5%}}; preferred empiric agent in critically ill patients is {{c2::an echinocandin}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section V, Recommendations 28–33; Thresholds & Doses

text: Simultaneous liver-kidney transplantation (SLK) is indicated for AKI on dialysis for ≥{{c1::6 weeks}} or eGFR ≤{{c2::25 mL/min}}, or CKD ≥90 days with eGFR ≤60 and current eGFR ≤{{c3::30 mL/min}}.

When pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis is contraindicated due to unacceptable bleeding risk, use {{c1::mechanical prophylaxis (pneumatic compression devices or graduated compression stockings)}} rather than no prophylaxis.
TBLC diagnostic yield is approximately {{c1::79%}}, rising to approximately {{c2::85%}} with ≥3 samples; SLB yield is approximately {{c2::90%}}.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF — TBLC vs SLB yield

text: Per AHA 2023, routine antibiotic prophylaxis before nondental invasive procedures (NDIPs) is {{c1::not recommended}}; AP remains indicated only before {{c2::invasive dental procedures}} in high-risk IE patients.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Current Position; AHA 2021 (Wilson et al, Ref 8)

text: Before tapering any DMARD, patients must have been at target (low disease activity or remission) for a minimum of {{c1::6 months}}.
The MAINRITSAN maintenance rituximab regimen for AAV is {{c1::500 mg IV}} at months {{c2::6, 12, and 18}} after induction.
For initial volume expansion in patients at risk for or with AKI, KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::isotonic crystalloids}} rather than {{c2::colloids (albumin or starches)}} (2B).
Ketoconazole carries a risk of idiosyncratic hepatic injury of approximately {{c1::1 in 15,000}}, with transaminase elevation in {{c2::10–15%}} of patients (FDA black box warning 2013).

In pregnant women treated for hyperthyroidism, the target maternal free T4 is {{c1::at or slightly above the upper limit of the pregnancy-specific reference range}}, using the lowest effective ATD dose.
Symptomatic Candida cystitis is treated with {{c1::fluconazole 200 mg daily}} for {{c2::2 weeks}}; echinocandins and lipid AmB should NOT be used for urinary infections.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section XIV, Recommendations 97–114

text: A CAC score of ≥{{c1::100}} Agatston units or ≥{{c1::75th percentile}} favors initiating statin therapy.
extra: Section 4.3 Adults With High Blood Cholesterol; Figure 3

text: In pediatric adrenal crisis, the hydrocortisone bolus dose is {{c1::50 mg/m²}} IV/IM, followed by 50–100 mg/m²/day divided q6h.
The target INR for warfarin in AF is {{c1::2.0–3.0}}; bleeding risk rises notably with INR >{{c2::4}}.
Semaglutide titration for obesity: start at {{c1::0.25 mg weekly × 4 weeks}}, then escalate through 0.5 → 1.0 → 1.7 → {{c2::2.4 mg weekly}}, reaching maintenance at {{c3::16 weeks}}.
CHEST 2021 defines three phases of anticoagulation: initiation ({{c1::5–21}} days), treatment ({{c2::3 months}}), and extended (no preplanned stop date).
Fixed-dose hydralazine/isosorbide dinitrate (target {{c1::75/40 mg TID}}) is recommended for {{c2::self-identified Black patients}} with NYHA III–IV HFrEF on optimal GDMT.
All sexually active women aged {{c1::<25 years}} should be screened annually for chlamydia and gonorrhea.
extra: STI Detection Among Special Populations, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: After bilateral adrenalectomy for Cushing's disease, approximately {{c1::21%}} of patients develop Nelson's syndrome, with corticotroph progression on MRI in ~{{c2::50%}}.
In NSTE-ACS, an early invasive strategy within {{c1::<24 hours}} is indicated for high-risk patients with GRACE score >{{c2::140}}.
extra: Section 6 / Figure 5 — NSTE-ACS timing of invasive strategy by GRACE

text: Galcanezumab is initiated with a {{c1::240 mg SC loading dose}}, followed by {{c2::120 mg SC monthly}}.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 6 / Table 7: CGRP monoclonal antibody dosing

text: In most opportunistic infections, ART should be initiated within {{c1::2 weeks}} of starting OI treatment.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 1 — timing of ART initiation including OI scenarios

text: Tezepelumab (anti-TSLP) is dosed at {{c1::210 mg SC every 4 weeks}} for patients ≥12 years.
In adult ITP, either {{c1::eltrombopag}} (oral daily) or {{c1::romiplostim}} (weekly subcutaneous) is acceptable as the TPO-RA; choice depends on patient preference.
extra: Recommendations 6–9 and Figure 1 — second-line therapy algorithm

text: Eplerenone is started at {{c1::25 mg twice daily}} as an alternative MR antagonist in PA.
A VKA should not be started in acute HIT until platelet count recovers to ≥{{c1::150 × 10⁹/L}}; if the patient is already on a VKA at HIT onset, stop it and give {{c2::IV vitamin K}}.
Hyperkalemia requiring immediate hospital assessment is defined as K+ ≥{{c1::6.5}} mmol/l; management is triggered at K+ >{{c2::5.5}} mmol/l.
In preexcited AF (WPW), AV nodal blockers ({{c1::digoxin, verapamil, diltiazem, beta-blockers, IV amiodarone}}) are {{c2::contraindicated}} due to risk of accelerating accessory pathway conduction.
An Lp(a) ≥{{c1::125 nmol/L (50 mg/dL)}} is a risk-enhancer (~1.4× ASCVD risk), and ≥{{c2::250 nmol/L (100 mg/dL)}} approximately doubles ASCVD risk.
extra: Section 3.4 / Table 4 — Lp(a) risk stratification by concentration

text: The preferred ketone criterion for DKA diagnosis is {{c1::β-hydroxybutyrate ≥3.0 mmol/L}}; urine ketones {{c2::≥2+}} are an acceptable alternative.
Device-detected atrial high-rate episodes of <{{c1::5 minutes}} do not require anticoagulation (likely artifact or non-AF).
extra: Section 6.4.1 and Figure 12 — device-detected AHRE management

text: Combined behavioral + pharmacotherapy interventions typically include ≥{{c1::4}} counseling sessions with {{c2::90–300}} minutes total contact time; the largest effect is seen with ≥{{c3::8}} sessions.
Foot inspection frequency by ulcer risk: low-risk {{c1::annually}}; moderate-risk every {{c2::3–6 months}}; high-risk every {{c3::1–3 months}}.
extra: Section 5.8, Tables 12–13 — preventive foot care and risk stratification

text: Gepants, ditans, or neuromodulatory devices should be reserved for adults who have contraindications/intolerance to triptans or {{c1::inadequate response to ≥2 oral triptans}}.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 3: Criteria for initiating acute treatment with gepants, ditans, or neuromodulatory devices

text: IDSA/SHEA recommends pharmacokinetic monitoring/adjustment programs for {{c1::aminoglycosides}} (strong, moderate) and for {{c2::vancomycin}} (weak, low).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section IX–X, Recommendations 9–11

text: When switching from ACEi to ARNi (sacubitril-valsartan), a washout period of at least {{c1::36 hours}} is required to reduce risk of angioedema.
Adult inpatients with CAP (both {{c1::nonsevere}} and {{c1::severe}}) and a positive respiratory virus test: empiric antibiotics {{c2::SHOULD}} be prescribed (conditional, very low-quality evidence).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 2

text: PJP prophylaxis is indicated in ANCA vasculitis patients receiving {{c1::cyclophosphamide}}, {{c1::rituximab}} (for ≥6 months after last dose), or {{c1::prednisone >20 mg/day plus MTX/AZA/MMF}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2 (GPA/MPA recommendations)

text: ACR 2020 conditionally recommends against ULT after a first gout flare UNLESS the patient has CKD stage ≥3, serum urate >{{c1::9 mg/dl}}, or {{c2::urolithiasis}}.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 1 — Indications for pharmacologic ULT

text: Vaptans are {{c1::not indicated}} for acute or severely symptomatic hyponatremia; {{c2::hypertonic saline}} is the treatment of choice in this setting.

For mild–moderate migraine attacks, first-line acute agents include {{c1::NSAIDs, nonopioid analgesics, and caffeinated combinations}}; for moderate–severe attacks or poor responders, migraine-specific agents ({{c2::triptans, DHE, gepants, lasmiditan}}) are preferred.
extra: AHS 2021 — Acute Treatment: Indications and Principles

text: Before primary prevention ICD implantation, wait ≥{{c1::40 days}} after MI and ≥{{c2::90 days}} after revascularization.
extra: Section 7.1.2 — timing thresholds for primary prevention ICD post-MI

text: The ACR 2017 GIOP guideline applies to adults on prednisone ≥{{c1::2.5 mg/day}} for ≥{{c2::3 months}}.
RSV vaccine should be given {{c1::seasonally during pregnancy}} to protect newborns.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — RSV; Addendum (updated July 2, 2025), 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Adult outpatients {{c1::with comorbidities}} and a positive respiratory virus test: empiric antibiotics {{c2::SHOULD}} be prescribed for possible bacterial-viral coinfection (conditional recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 2

text: Penicillin allergy is reported in {{c1::10–15%}} of admitted patients and in {{c2::15–24%}} of those specifically requiring antibiotics.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XII, Recommendation 13

text: Rituximab dosing for membranous nephropathy is either {{c1::1 g IV × 2 doses, 2 weeks apart}}, or {{c1::375 mg/m² weekly × 4 doses}}.
extra: KDIGO 2021 Glomerular Diseases, Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.3.1 and Figures 30–31

text: Long-term or in-flight oxygen is indicated in PAH when PaO2 <{{c1::8 kPa (60 mmHg)}} or SaO2 <{{c2::92%}}.
extra: Table 19 — PAH drug dosing; Section 2.1 'What is new'

text: ACR 2020 strongly recommends switching to pegloticase when XOI plus uricosurics fail to achieve the SU target AND the patient has {{c1::≥2 flares/year}} or {{c2::nonresolving tophi}}.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 5 — Pegloticase indications

text: In ascites with a high SAAG, an ascitic protein of {{c1::>2.5 g/dL}} suggests cardiac (rather than cirrhotic) ascites.
extra: Figure 2 and Table 4 — SAAG-based diagnostic algorithm; AASLD 2021

text: After TSS or adrenal adenoma resection, a postoperative serum cortisol <{{c1::1.8 µg/dL (50 nmol/L)}} indicates likely remission/hypocortisolism.
In CKD G5D, the recommended dialysate calcium concentration is {{c1::1.25–1.50 mmol/L (2.5–3.0 mEq/L)}}.
Tofacitinib induction: {{c1::10 mg PO BID}} for {{c2::8 weeks}} (extendable to 16 weeks if no response).
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: In adults with ITP ≥3 months who are corticosteroid-dependent or unresponsive, the preferred second-line agent is {{c1::TPO-RA}} over rituximab, and either is preferred over {{c2::splenectomy}}.
extra: Recommendations 6–9 and Figure 1 — second-line therapy algorithm

text: In premenopausal women with IDA, the pooled lower GI malignancy yield is {{c1::0.9%}} and upper GI malignancy yield is {{c2::0.2%}}, reflecting the low overall risk in this group.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Recommendation 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: Post-liver-transplant HCC recurrence within Milan criteria occurs in {{c1::10–15%}} of patients; the RETREAT score stratifies 5-year recurrence from <3% (score {{c2::0}}) to 75% (score ≥{{c3::5}}).
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Liver Transplantation / Figure 12

text: NDIPs with the strongest temporal association with subsequent IE in high-risk patients include {{c1::CIED implantation}}, upper and lower GI endoscopy, bronchoscopy, bone marrow biopsy, and blood transfusion/exchange.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Key Recommendations; Table 3

text: The preferred anticoagulant and dose for superficial vein thrombosis at increased risk of progression is {{c1::fondaparinux 2.5 mg}} daily.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #18–20; Thresholds & Doses

text: Amiodarone for VF/pulseless VT: initial bolus {{c1::300 mg IV}}, followed by 1 mg/min × 6 h, then 0.5 mg/min × 18 h.
The guideline recommends against routine {{c1::tapering or discontinuation}} of biologics in stable axSpA; TNFi discontinuation in remission leads to relapse in {{c2::60–74%}} of patients.
To prevent post-ERCP pancreatitis, give {{c1::rectal indomethacin 100 mg}} plus periprocedural {{c2::lactated Ringer's}} to all patients undergoing ERCP unless contraindicated.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Preventing Post-ERCP Pancreatitis

text: In CKD G4, serum calcium and phosphate should be monitored every {{c1::3–6 months}}, and PTH every {{c2::6–12 months}}.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Thresholds & Doses — monitoring intervals

text: Edoxaban for cancer-associated thrombosis requires at least {{c1::5 days}} of parenteral anticoagulation before initiation, at a dose of {{c2::60 mg daily}}.
Intensive insulin targeting glucose {{c1::80–130 mg/dL}} is NOT recommended in AIS (SHINE trial); instead, target glucose {{c2::140–180 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.5, COR 3 No Benefit — Blood Glucose Management

text: In obese GERD patients with BMI >{{c1::35}}, {{c2::Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (RYGB)}} is the preferred antireflux surgical procedure.
Lifelong recurrence surveillance is recommended for all Cushing's syndrome patients except those who underwent resection of a low-density adrenal adenoma with CT density <{{c1::10 Hounsfield units}}.
Acute limb ischemia (ALI) is defined as symptoms ≤{{c1::2 weeks}}; chronic limb-threatening ischemia (CLTI) is defined as symptoms >{{c1::2 weeks}}.
extra: Section 2.1, Table 4 — definitions of ALI and CLTI

text: If uncertainty persists, repeat troponin beyond {{c1::3 hours}} for hs-cTn or beyond {{c2::6 hours}} for conventional cTn.
In males with primary osteoporosis, first-line pharmacologic therapy to reduce fracture risk is {{c1::a bisphosphonate}}.

In critically ill or at-risk, hemodynamically stable children without hemoglobinopathy, cyanotic heart disease, or severe hypoxemia, AABB 2023 recommends RBC transfusion at Hgb <{{c1::7 g/dL}} (strong recommendation).
LV thrombus after ischemic stroke should be treated with {{c1::warfarin}} (INR 2.0–3.0) for approximately {{c2::3 months}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Thresholds & Doses section

text: Radial artery access for PCI reduces {{c1::bleeding and vascular complications}} compared with femoral access and reduces {{c2::mortality}} specifically in ACS.
extra: Key Recommendations — radial artery access for PCI

text: The 2022 guideline maintains {{c1::four}} HRCT categories for IPF: UIP, probable UIP, indeterminate, and alternative diagnosis.
In HIT with a low-probability 4Ts score (≤3), the recommended action is to {{c1::not order HIT laboratory testing and not empirically discontinue heparin or start a non-heparin anticoagulant}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendations 2.1–2.10 and Figure 1

text: Lesion-specific ischemia is indicated by FFR ≤{{c1::0.80}} or iFR ≤{{c2::0.89}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Thresholds & Doses

text: Clinicians should maintain a low threshold to evaluate for IE in high-risk patients presenting within {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}} of an NDIP.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Future Considerations; Janszky 2018 (Ref 3) and Thornhill 2022 (Ref 4)

text: Metronidazole (500 mg PO TID × 10–14 days) is only an alternative for {{c1::nonsevere}} CDI when {{c2::fidaxomicin and vancomycin are unavailable}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Key Recommendations; Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: Bupropion SR achieves a cessation rate of {{c1::19.0%}} vs 11.0% for control at ≥6 months (RR {{c2::1.64}}).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Benefits of Tobacco Cessation Interventions — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: First-line treatment for benign adrenal Cushing's syndrome is {{c1::unilateral adrenalectomy}}.
extra: Recommendation 3.1a–3.1c — First-line surgical approach by etiology

text: In patients with cirrhosis after HCV treatment, HCC surveillance consists of {{c1::ultrasound + AFP}} every {{c2::6 months}}.
extra: Cirrhosis monitoring section — US/AFP every 6 months and endoscopic variceal surveillance

text: FMT for CDI should be reserved until appropriate antibiotic therapy has failed for at least {{c1::2 recurrences}} (i.e., ≥{{c1::3 CDI episodes}}).
In at-risk patients, HCC should be diagnosed noninvasively using {{c1::LI-RADS}} on multiphase CT or contrast-enhanced MRI; biopsy is required for {{c2::LR-M, LR-TIV, and noncharacteristic}} lesions.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Diagnosis section / Figure 6 / LI-RADS algorithm

text: FIB-4 should be repeated every {{c1::1–2 years}} in patients with pre-diabetes/T2DM or ≥2 metabolic risk factors.
To qualify for USPSTF 2021 lung cancer screening, a former smoker must have quit within the past {{c1::15 years}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — quit interval cutoff

text: UFH for PCI is dosed as a {{c1::70–100 U/kg}} bolus, targeting an ACT of {{c2::250–300 seconds}}.
Immunosuppression should be withheld in anti-GBM disease if the patient has {{c1::100% crescents}}, is {{c1::dialysis-dependent}}, and has {{c1::no pulmonary hemorrhage}}.

Phentermine-topiramate ER should be discontinued if a patient achieves <{{c1::5%}} TBWL after {{c2::12 weeks}} at maximum maintenance dose; taper every-other-day for ≥{{c3::1 week}} to reduce seizure risk.
Dupilumab for COPD is dosed at {{c1::300 mg every 2 weeks}} subcutaneously (indicated particularly in chronic bronchitis phenotype).
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.11 — Biologic therapy; Appendix 3

text: {{c1::Evinacumab}} {{c2::15 mg/kg IV q4wk}} is indicated for homozygous FH (HoFH), achieving ~49% LDL-C reduction.
extra: Table 5, Table 6 — Lipid-lowering drug classes and doses

text: In patients with {{c1::NYHA class III or IV}} heart failure, a non-TNFi bDMARD or tsDMARD is preferred over a TNF inhibitor.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: In CYP2C19 rapid/ultrarapid metabolizers, manage subtherapeutic PPI exposure by {{c1::increasing PPI dose 50–100%}} or switching to {{c2::esomeprazole, rabeprazole, or a PCAB (vonoprazan)}}.
extra: ACG 2024 – Thresholds & Doses; Section: Antibiotic susceptibility testing

text: Thyroid nodule growth is defined as a {{c1::≥20%}} increase in two dimensions (minimum {{c2::2 mm}}) or a >50% volume change.
Serial imaging for isolated distal DVT should be performed {{c1::weekly}} for {{c2::2 weeks}} to monitor for extension.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #1–2; Thresholds & Doses

text: For pregnant women with asthma, ICS-containing therapy should {{c1::NOT be stopped}}, because risks of {{c2::uncontrolled asthma}} exceed the risks of the medications.
First-line pharmacologic treatment for HRS-AKI is {{c1::terlipressin (or norepinephrine)}} plus {{c2::albumin 1 g/kg on day 1 then 40–50 g/day}}.
For stage 2 hypertension (≥140/90 mm Hg), the guideline recommends initiating {{c1::combination therapy with two first-line agents as a single-pill combination}} to improve adherence.
ASB should be screened for and treated before endoscopic urologic procedures that {{c1::breach the mucosal lining}} (e.g., TURP, TURBT, ureteroscopy, percutaneous stone surgery).
Severe AS is defined by aortic Vmax ≥{{c1::4 m/s}} or mean gradient ≥{{c2::40 mm Hg}}, with AVA typically ≤{{c3::1.0 cm²}}.
Rome IV defines IBS as abdominal pain occurring ≥{{c1::1 time per week}} on average, associated with defecation, change in stool frequency, or change in stool form.
extra: Rome IV diagnostic criteria (Table 1) — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: Sporadic aortic root/ascending aneurysm surgical threshold is {{c1::≥5.5 cm}} at standard centers, or {{c2::≥5.0 cm}} at experienced centers with a Multidisciplinary Aortic Team.
extra: Top 10 Take-Home Messages; Section 6.5 — 2022 ACC/AHA Aortic Disease Guideline

text: In myxedema coma, adjunctive IV LT3 is given as a loading dose of {{c1::5–20 μg}}, then {{c2::2.5–10 μg q8h}}.
A nocturnal seizure increases recurrence risk after a first unprovoked seizure with an OR of {{c1::2.1}} (95% CI 1.0–4.3) at 1–4 years (Level B).
extra: Analysis of Evidence: Risk of seizure recurrence — nocturnal seizure

text: In confirmed antiphospholipid syndrome, CHEST 2021 recommends adjusted-dose {{c1::VKA}} (target INR {{c2::2.5}}) over DOACs (weak, low-certainty).
3HR (isoniazid + rifampin daily × 3 months) carries a {{c1::conditional}} recommendation for adults and children of all ages, including HIV-positive persons as drug interactions allow.
Surgical LAA occlusion is recommended in AF patients with CHA2DS2-VASc ≥{{c1::2}} undergoing {{c2::cardiac surgery}}, in addition to continued oral anticoagulation.
Extended-window IVT ({{c1::4.5–9 hours}} from onset, or wake-up stroke <9 h from sleep midpoint) requires perfusion mismatch: ratio >{{c2::1.2}}, mismatch >10 mL, and core <70 mL.
extra: Section 4.6.3 and Table 3 — WAKE-UP, EXTEND, TRACE-III, TIMELESS trials

text: For adults with elevated BP or stage 1 hypertension (130–139/80–89 mm Hg) and a 10-year ASCVD risk {{c1::<10%}}, use {{c2::nonpharmacologic therapy alone}} (pharmacotherapy is not indicated).
extra: Section 4.4 Adults With High Blood Pressure; Figure 4; Table 7

text: After TAVR, new persistent LBBB or high-grade AV block beyond {{c1::24-48 hours}} warrants careful surveillance and consideration of permanent pacing.
extra: Section 8.1.2.4 — transcatheter aortic valve replacement conduction abnormalities

text: ATTR cardiomyopathy (NYHA I–III) is treated with {{c1::tafamidis}} {{c2::61 mg daily}} (or 80 mg meglumine formulation).
DoxyPEP consists of {{c1::doxycycline 200 mg}} taken within {{c2::72 hours}} of condomless sex, maximum once daily.
First-line endoscopic modalities with strongest evidence for ulcer bleeding are {{c1::bipolar electrocoagulation}}, {{c1::heater probe}}, or {{c1::absolute ethanol injection}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Choice of endoscopic hemostatic therapy / Table 7

text: Exercise PH is defined as an mPAP/CO slope of {{c1::>3 mmHg/L/min}} between rest and exercise.
extra: Section 3.1 Definitions, Table 5 — updated hemodynamic definitions of PH

text: The 2019 ACC/AHA guideline recommends estimating 10-year ASCVD risk using the Pooled Cohort Equations in adults aged {{c1::40–75}} years before initiating statin, antihypertensive, or aspirin therapy.
extra: Section 2.2 Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk; Table 3

text: In elderly patients >70–80 years, a higher TSH target of up to {{c1::4–6 mIU/L}} is acceptable to avoid overtreatment.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 6a; Thresholds & Doses — Elderly TSH target

text: In patients with high BP, CKD G1–G4, and moderately-to-severely increased albuminuria (A2 or A3) with diabetes, KDIGO 2021 recommends starting {{c1::ACEi or ARB}} (1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.2.3 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: In ACS patients with anemia who are not actively bleeding, the target hemoglobin for red blood cell transfusion is ~{{c1::10 g/dL}} (MINT trial threshold).
A high-volume thyroid surgeon for Graves' disease thyroidectomy is defined as performing >{{c1::25}} thyroidectomies per year.
extra: Recommendations 27–28 (Section F2) — near-total/total thyroidectomy by high-volume surgeon

text: In cancer patients with VTE, anticoagulation dose modification or platelet transfusion support is warranted when platelets fall below {{c1::50×10⁹/L}}.
extra: Appendix 6 Q8 Conclusions — Special populations: thrombocytopenia

text: If HBsAg returns positive during DAA therapy, the correct action is to {{c1::continue HCV treatment}} and {{c2::initiate or refer for HBV therapy}} to prevent flare/reactivation.
extra: Footnote ** — HBV coinfection risk discussion and shared decision-making

text: USPSTF 2023 gives an {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement for routine {{c2::suicide risk}} screening in asymptomatic adults.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — I statement for suicide risk screening; Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendations

text: NIV is first-line ventilatory support for hypercapnic respiratory failure defined as PaCO2 ≥{{c1::45 mmHg}} with pH ≤{{c2::7.35}}.
The DETECT algorithm for PAH screening applies to SSc patients with disease duration >{{c1::3 years}}, FVC ≥{{c2::40%}}, and DLCO <{{c2::60%}}.
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10 — PAH treatment algorithm; Section 2.1 'What is new'

text: DMT may be discontinued in SPMS patients who have no relapses or gadolinium-enhancing lesions and have been nonambulatory (EDSS ≥{{c1::7}}) for at least {{c2::2 years}} (Level C).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Stopping Recommendations 1–3

text: Procalcitonin-guided algorithms reduce antibiotic duration by approximately {{c1::2–4 days}} in ICU sepsis without increased mortality.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XVIII, Recommendation 19

text: The recommended validated depression screening tool for perinatal (pregnant/postpartum) persons is the {{c1::EPDS (Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale)}}.
The recommended RRT dose for intermittent or extended RRT is a Kt/V of {{c1::3.9}} per week (1A).

Catheter ablation is recommended for recurrent sustained monomorphic VT in ischemic cardiomyopathy {{c1::refractory to or intolerant of amiodarone}}, and is superior to escalating drug therapy (per the {{c2::VANISH}} trial).
extra: Section 6 — catheter ablation for VT in ischemic cardiomyopathy

text: ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::against}} routinely combining mechanical and pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis — use {{c2::either alone}}.
LQTS diagnostic thresholds include QTc ≥{{c1::500 ms}}, or QTc {{c1::480–499 ms}} with syncope, or an LQTS risk score ≥{{c2::3.5}}.
NSBBs should NOT be started in compensated cirrhosis without CSPH (HVPG {{c1::6–9 mm Hg}}) because there is no benefit on variceal development or decompensation.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH?

text: FIB-4 risk categories: <{{c1::1.3}} = low risk; {{c1::1.3–2.67}} = intermediate risk; >{{c1::2.67}} = high risk.
An excellent response to therapy is defined as suppressed Tg {{c1::<0.2 ng/mL}} or stimulated Tg {{c2::<1 ng/mL}}.
For ASB in pregnancy, the recommended treatment duration is {{c1::4–7 days}}; {{c2::single-dose}} therapy is NOT preferred.
Screening for cardiac amyloidosis includes {{c1::serum/urine immunofixation electrophoresis}}, {{c1::serum free light chains}}, and {{c1::99mTc-PYP scintigraphy}}.
A catheter with related thrombosis should {{c1::not}} be removed if it is functional, well-positioned, and non-infected.

IDSA/SHEA recommends targeted interventions to reduce use of antibiotics with high CDI risk: {{c1::clindamycin}}, {{c1::broad-spectrum cephalosporins}}, and {{c1::fluoroquinolones}} (strong, moderate).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section V, Recommendation 5

text: HPV vaccination is recommended routinely through age {{c1::26}} years; shared clinical decision-making applies for ages {{c2::27–45}} years.
extra: Notes — HPV vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Nirmatrelvir/ritonavir for eGFR 30–<60 mL/min: {{c1::150 mg/100 mg}} PO every 12 hours × 5 days.
USPSTF 2021 assigns an {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement to e-cigarettes as a cessation aid, meaning clinicians should {{c2::not recommend}} e-cigarettes for tobacco cessation.
extra: Suggestions for Practice Regarding I Statements — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: {{c1::Statins}} are recommended for CVD risk reduction across the NAFLD disease spectrum, including {{c2::compensated cirrhosis}}, but should be used cautiously in decompensated cirrhosis.
The recommended duration of antibiotic therapy for both HAP and VAP is {{c1::7 days}}, individualized based on clinical response.
An Lp(a) ≥{{c1::430 nmol/L (180 mg/dL)}} is associated with approximately {{c2::4×}} the baseline ASCVD risk.
extra: Section 3.4 / Table 4 — Lp(a) risk stratification by concentration

text: In atherosclerotic aortic disease, target LDL <{{c1::70 mg/dL}} with {{c2::high-intensity statin}} therapy.
Substituting {{c1::doxycycline}} for tetracycline in BQT should be avoided when possible because eradication rates are {{c2::substantially lower}}.
extra: ACG 2024 – Summary of recommendations; Key Recommendations

text: Quitting smoking {{c1::early in pregnancy}} provides the greatest fetal benefit, though quitting at any point benefits both mother and infant.
extra: Practice Considerations: Pregnant Persons — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: HPV vaccine requires a {{c1::2-dose}} schedule (0, 6–12 months) if initiated before age 15, and a {{c2::3-dose}} schedule if initiated at age ≥15 or in immunocompromised persons.
extra: Human Papillomavirus Infections, Prevention, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Endobronchial valve placement is indicated when post-BD FEV1 is {{c1::15–45%}} predicted with marked hyperinflation and intact fissures (no collateral ventilation).
After initiating or up-titrating a RAS inhibitor in CKD, BP, serum creatinine, and potassium should be checked within {{c1::2–4 weeks}}.
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendations 3.2.1–3.2.3 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: In BAV patients undergoing concomitant AVR, aortic replacement is indicated when aortic diameter ≥{{c1::4.5 cm}}.
SIRS criteria that trigger antibiotics in purulent SSTI include: T >{{c1::38°C}} or <{{c1::36°C}}, RR >{{c2::24}}, HR >{{c2::90}}, WBC >12,000 or <{{c2::400}} cells/µL.
For extended-phase VTE anticoagulation, reduced-dose rivaroxaban is dosed at {{c1::10 mg once daily}}.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #21–27; Thresholds & Doses

text: A subcentimeter hepatic lesion on surveillance ultrasound (US LI-RADS category 2) should be followed with repeat ultrasound + AFP in {{c1::3–6 months}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Recall and Management of Surveillance Results / Figure 5

text: All adults ≥19 years should receive {{c1::1 dose}} of influenza vaccine {{c2::annually}}.
extra: Table 1; Adult Schedule Notes — Influenza, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: DTG/3TC is contraindicated in {{c1::HBV coinfection}}, {{c2::pregnancy}}, and documented lamivudine or integrase resistance.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 2 — recommended initial ART regimens

text: Initial treatment of adult MCD is oral prednisone {{c1::1 mg/kg/d}} (max {{c2::80 mg}}) for up to {{c2::16 weeks}}, then tapered over ≥24 weeks after remission.
Significant stenosis is defined as ≥{{c1::70%}} diameter for non-left main lesions and ≥{{c2::50%}} for left main lesions.
Every patient with PAI should be equipped with a {{c1::steroid emergency card}}, a {{c1::medical alert ID}}, and a {{c1::parenteral hydrocortisone injection kit}} for crisis prevention.
For first CMC joint OA, the 2019 ACR/AF guideline {{c1::strongly recommends}} hand orthoses (neoprene or rigid), while orthoses for other hand joints receive a {{c2::conditional}} recommendation.
extra: Table 1 / Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body approaches — hand orthoses

text: The ADA guideline defines children as ages {{c1::birth–11 years}} and adolescents as ages {{c2::12–17 years}}.
extra: Scope of the Guidelines — age strata and diabetes types covered

text: In patients with high BP, CKD G1–G4, and severely increased albuminuria (A3) without diabetes, KDIGO 2021 recommends starting {{c1::ACEi or ARB}} (1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.2.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Uncomplicated acute type B dissection is managed medically with anti-impulse therapy targeting HR {{c1::60–80 bpm}} and SBP {{c2::<120 mm Hg}}.
Women of childbearing potential on phentermine-topiramate ER must use effective contraception because {{c1::topiramate}} is {{c2::teratogenic}}.
For factor Xa inhibitor–associated ICH (apixaban, rivaroxaban, edoxaban), give {{c1::andexanet alfa}} or {{c2::4F-PCC}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: In patients with chronic inflammation or CKD, ferritin alone is insufficient to diagnose iron deficiency; add {{c1::CRP, transferrin saturation, or soluble transferrin receptor}} to aid diagnosis.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Recommendation 1 / Key Recommendations

text:
Trimester-specific TSH targets in pregnancy:
1st trimester: {{c1::0.1–2.5}} mIU/L
2nd trimester: {{c2::0.2–3.0}} mIU/L
3rd trimester: {{c3::0.3–3.0}} mIU/L
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 6b; Thresholds & Doses — Pregnancy TSH targets

text: Treatment failure on oral bisphosphonate is defined as a fracture after ≥{{c1::18 months}} of therapy or BMD loss ≥{{c2::10%/year}}; the recommended switch is to teriparatide, denosumab, or IV bisphosphonate.
For intrathyroidal cN0 DTC {{c1::<1 cm}}, {{c2::lobectomy alone}} is sufficient.
{{c1::Smoking}} is the only modifiable risk factor for postoperative recurrence in Crohn's disease; {{c2::NSAIDs}} should also be avoided as they may trigger flares.
extra: Postoperative Crohn's Disease — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: CDAI thresholds: <{{c1::150}} = remission; 150–220 = mild; 220–450 = moderate-severe; >{{c2::450}} = severe/fulminant.
extra: Working definitions of disease activity — ACG 2018 Crohn's Guideline

text: Platelet transfusion in acute HIT is indicated only for {{c1::active bleeding or high bleeding risk}}; routine transfusion is not recommended.

AGA 2022 recommends adding pharmacotherapy to lifestyle intervention in adults with BMI ≥{{c1::30}} kg/m², or ≥{{c2::27}} kg/m² with weight-related complications, who have inadequate response to lifestyle alone.
Febuxostat should be started at <{{c1::40 mg/day}} with subsequent upward titration to achieve the serum urate target.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2 — Low starting doses

text: The Endocrine Society recommends {{c1::against routine screening}} of the general male population for hypogonadism; case detection should be reserved for high-prevalence conditions.
Flexible sigmoidoscopy alone is performed every {{c1::5 years}}, OR every {{c2::10 years}} combined with annual FIT, for colorectal cancer screening.
Prone positioning is indicated for moderate-to-severe ARDS when PaO2/FiO2 is <{{c1::150 mmHg}} with PEEP ≥{{c2::5 cmH2O}} despite optimization.
In T-treated men, prostate cancer screening generally begins at age {{c1::55–69}} years, but at age {{c2::40}} if at increased risk (African American or first-degree relative with prostate cancer).
Catheter ablation is Class 1 as {{c1::first-line}} rhythm control in selected {{c2::younger, symptomatic patients with paroxysmal AF}}.
extra: Section 8.1 and Section 8.4 — EARLY-AF, STOP-AF First

text: When escalating UC therapy to a biologic or JAK inhibitor, {{c1::stop 5-ASA}} — there is no efficacy benefit and it is not cost-effective to continue.
extra: Positioning Considerations section — stopping 5-ASA after escalation

text: When starting a biologic in PsA, {{c1::killed}} vaccines may be given concurrently with the biologic; {{c2::live attenuated}} vaccines should be administered before biologic initiation.
extra: Table 7 — Vaccination recommendations around biologic initiation
