text: On the IMPROVE VTE score, pharmacologic prophylaxis is indicated at a score of ≥{{c1::2}} (VTE incidence ~1.5% at 2–3; ~5.7% at ≥4).
The hydrocortisone regimen used in the Dequin trial for severe CAP: {{c1::200 mg/day}} continuous IV infusion for {{c2::4 or 7 days}}, then tapered to a total of 8 or 14 days.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 4 (Dequin trial)

text: Bivalirudin for acute HIT is given at {{c1::0.15 mg/kg/h}} IV (no bolus), titrated to an aPTT of {{c2::1.5–2.5×}} baseline.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: Patients with HIT should carry or wear an emergency identifier if HIT was diagnosed within the last {{c1::3 months}}; this is not recommended beyond {{c1::3 months}}.
For PCI in acute HIT or subacute HIT A, the preferred anticoagulant is {{c1::bivalirudin}} (argatroban if unavailable).
In the Janszky 2018 Swedish case-crossover study, the inpatient OR for IE following upper GI endoscopy was {{c1::3.97}}.
extra: Janszky et al 2018 (Ref 3) / Table 3 — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: When switching from natalizumab to fingolimod, initiate fingolimod {{c1::8–12 weeks}} after natalizumab discontinuation (not 16 weeks) to reduce relapse and MRI activity.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Recommendation 9

text: During the first 12 months of T therapy, urologic referral is indicated for: PSA increase >{{c1::1.4 ng/mL}} above baseline, confirmed PSA >{{c2::4.0 ng/mL}}, or a prostatic abnormality on DRE.
Standardized office BP measurement requires ≥{{c1::5}} minutes of seated rest and avoidance of caffeine, exercise, and smoking for ≥{{c2::30}} minutes prior.
extra: Chapter 1, Recommendation 1.1 and Figure 2 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: Finerenone is dosed at {{c1::10 mg}} daily when eGFR is 25–59 ml/min/1.73 m², and {{c2::20 mg}} daily when eGFR is ≥60 ml/min/1.73 m².
Patients with severe ARDS meeting EOLIA criteria should be referred to {{c1::an experienced ECMO center}} for {{c2::VV-ECMO}}; this recommendation extends to COVID-19 severe ARDS.

Compared with LMWH for cancer-associated thrombosis, DOACs are non-inferior or superior for recurrent VTE but increase {{c1::clinically relevant non-major bleeding}}, especially with {{c2::GI or genitourinary}} cancers.
extra: Appendix 6 Q2 Conclusions — Early maintenance and long-term anticoagulation

text: The 2024 USPSTF recommends {{c1::biennial (every 2 years)}} mammography screening, because it has a more favorable benefit-to-harm balance than {{c2::annual}} screening.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening Interval — biennial favored over annual

text: The two foundational ASP interventions recommended by IDSA/SHEA are {{c1::preauthorization}} and/or {{c2::prospective audit and feedback (PAF)}} (strong, moderate).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section I, Recommendation 1

text: CLSI fluconazole breakpoints for C. albicans: susceptible at ≤{{c1::2 μg/mL}}, resistant at ≥{{c2::8 μg/mL}}.
Behavioral counseling for tobacco cessation in pregnancy increases mean birth weight by approximately {{c1::55.6 g}} and lowers the low-birth-weight rate (RR {{c2::0.83}}).
extra: Practice Considerations: Pregnant Persons — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: In adults with glomerular disease, the target SBP is {{c1::<120 mm Hg}} (standardized office measurement).
The ATD of choice in virtually all hyperthyroid patients is {{c1::methimazole (MMI)}}, except in {{c2::first-trimester pregnancy}}, thyroid storm, or MMI intolerance.
extra: Recommendation 13 (Section E1) — MMI as preferred ATD

text: For temporal artery biopsy in GCA, a long-segment specimen of {{c1::>1 cm}} is preferred over <1 cm.
After an unprovoked first seizure in adults, the 2-year recurrence risk is {{c1::21%–45%}}, with risk greatest within the {{c2::first 2 years}}.
extra: Table 1 — Pooled recurrence rates; Recommendations section (Level A)

text: The 2021 USPSTF assigns a Grade {{c1::A}} recommendation to colorectal cancer screening for average-risk adults aged {{c2::50-75}} years.

A major transient risk factor (e.g., surgery with GA >30 min, ≥3 days inpatient bedrest, major trauma, cesarean) must have occurred within {{c1::3 months}} before the VTE to qualify as the provoking event.
The preferred treatment for chlamydia in pregnancy is {{c1::azithromycin 1 g orally as a single dose}}; test of cure should occur at {{c2::4 weeks}}, with rescreening at 3 months.
extra: Chlamydial Infections, Recommended Regimens; STI Detection Among Special Populations — Pregnant Women, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: {{c1::Aminoglycoside monotherapy}} should be avoided for P. aeruginosa HAP/VAP; aminoglycosides and colistin should also be avoided {{c2::empirically}} when alternatives exist.
Nocturnal bradycardia should prompt screening for {{c1::sleep apnea}} and is not by itself an indication for permanent pacing.
extra: Section 4.2.7 — sleep apnea evaluation in nocturnal bradycardia

text: For suspected LT4 nonadherence with persistently elevated TSH despite high doses, consider supervised {{c1::weekly oral LT4 (7× the daily dose)}} after confirming adequate absorption.
Patients with or at high risk for {{c1::cardiovascular disease}} should be switched off {{c2::abacavir}}-containing regimens when an active alternative is available.
After successful fibrinolysis for STEMI, patients should be transferred for routine angiography {{c1::2–24 hours}} later.
In the captopril challenge (25–50 mg PO), PAC normally suppresses by >{{c1::30%}}; failure to suppress confirms PA.
In long-distance travelers (>4 hours) {{c1::without}} VTE risk factors, ASH 2018 recommends against using graduated compression stockings, LMWH, or aspirin.
TEER for chronic severe secondary MR requires LVEF {{c1::20%–50%}}, LVESD ≤{{c2::70 mm}}, PASP ≤{{c3::70 mm Hg}}, and persistent NYHA II–IV symptoms despite optimal GDMT (COAPT criteria).
Contraindications to exercise stress testing include severe symptomatic {{c1::aortic stenosis}} and SBP ≥{{c2::200/110 mm Hg}}.
Adjunctive methotrexate in PMR is dosed at {{c1::7.5–10 mg/week}} orally, considered in patients at high relapse risk or with refractory disease.
During T therapy, monitor symptoms, serum T, hematocrit, and prostate at {{c1::3–12 months}}, at {{c1::1 year}}, then {{c1::annually}}.
The SSC 2021 recommends {{c1::prolonged (extended/continuous) infusion}} of beta-lactams after an initial loading dose, guided by {{c2::PK/PD}} principles.
AKI Stage 3 urine output criteria: UO <{{c1::0.3 mL/kg/h}} for ≥{{c2::24 h}} or anuria ≥{{c3::12 h}}.
In acute variceal hemorrhage (AVH), use a restrictive transfusion strategy targeting hemoglobin ~{{c1::7 g/dL}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Table 5

text: For sinus node dysfunction, the guideline uses sinus rate <{{c1::50 bpm}} and/or sinus pause >{{c2::3 seconds}} as diagnostic thresholds.
extra: Section 2.2/Table 3 — definitions of SND, AV block grades, and conduction disorders

text: For patients ≥150 kg with uncomplicated gonorrhea, the ceftriaxone dose is increased to {{c1::1 g IM}} (single dose).
extra: Treatment of Gonococcal Infections — ceftriaxone dose-by-weight recommendation, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: In PMR, {{c1::glucocorticoids}} are preferred over {{c2::NSAIDs}} as first-line therapy.
Splenectomy in ITP should be delayed at least {{c1::12 months (1 year)}} after diagnosis when possible, to allow for potential spontaneous remission.
extra: Recommendations 6–9 and Figure 1 — second-line therapy algorithm; Table 3

text: The initial MAP target in septic shock requiring vasopressors is ≥ {{c1::65 mm Hg}}.
extra: Initial Resuscitation — MAP ≥ 65 mm Hg target; SSC 2021

text: KDIGO 2024 recommends a reduced-dose glucocorticoid induction starting at ~{{c1::0.5 mg/kg/day}} (rather than 1 mg/kg/day) with rapid taper to minimize steroid toxicity.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Practice Points on glucocorticoid dosing

text: For extended-phase VTE anticoagulation, reduced-dose apixaban is dosed at {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}}.
extra: CHEST 2021 VTE — Summary of Recommendations #21–27; Thresholds & Doses

text: The European guideline limits sodium correction to {{c1::10 mmol/L per day}} with no specified minimum correction rate.
extra: Table 2 — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017; Spasovski et al. 2014

text: In sepsis, RBC transfusion should be initiated using a restrictive threshold of hemoglobin < {{c1::70 g/L}}.
extra: Additional Therapies / RBC Transfusion Targets (TRISS, TRICC); SSC 2021

text: USPSTF recommends against cervical cancer screening (Grade D) after hysterectomy with cervix removal, provided there is no history of {{c1::CIN 2/3 or cervical cancer}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table; Clinical Considerations: Patient Population Under Consideration — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: A CAC score of {{c1::0}} generally supports deferring statin therapy, EXCEPT in patients with {{c2::smoking, diabetes, or family history of premature ASCVD}}.
extra: Section 4.3 Adults With High Blood Cholesterol; Figure 3

text: In the semaglutide phase 2b trial, NASH resolution occurred in {{c1::59%}} of patients on 0.4 mg daily vs {{c2::17%}} on placebo at 72 weeks.
4R (rifampin daily × 4 months) is strongly recommended for {{c1::HIV-negative}} adults and children of all ages; there is no efficacy evidence in {{c1::HIV-positive}} persons.
Patients with acute chest pain should be transported by {{c1::EMS}} rather than private vehicle; approximately 1 in {{c2::300}} self-transported patients suffers cardiac arrest en route.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: The two SGLT2 inhibitors recommended for HFrEF GDMT are {{c1::dapagliflozin}} and {{c1::empagliflozin}}, each dosed at {{c2::10 mg daily}}, regardless of diabetes status.
The long-term blood pressure target for secondary prevention after ICH is ≤{{c1::130/80 mm Hg}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 9.1.2; PROGRESS, SPS3

text: DAPT with aspirin + clopidogrel is recommended for minor stroke (NIHSS {{c1::≤3}}) or high-risk TIA (ABCD2 {{c2::≥4}}) presenting within {{c1::24 hours}} of onset.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.19 / Figure 6; CHANCE and POINT trials

text: In DMARD-naive patients with low disease activity, {{c1::hydroxychloroquine}} is the preferred csDMARD over other options.
Sulfasalazine {{c1::3–6 g/day}} has modest efficacy only for mildly active {{c2::colonic or ileocolonic}} Crohn's disease (not isolated small bowel).
Any positive non-colonoscopy colorectal cancer screening test must be followed by {{c1::colonoscopy}} to achieve the benefits of screening.
To reduce AF burden and recurrence, alcohol intake should be limited to ≤{{c1::3 standard drinks/week}} or {{c2::abstinence}}; AF risk increases linearly with intake.
extra: Section 5.2 — lifestyle and risk factor modification

text: In DMARD-naive patients with low disease activity, the preferred order of csDMARDs is hydroxychloroquine, then {{c1::sulfasalazine}}, then {{c2::methotrexate}}, then leflunomide.
Apixaban, edoxaban, and rivaroxaban should be avoided if CrCl <{{c1::15}} mL/min; dabigatran should be avoided if CrCl <{{c2::30}} mL/min.
Percutaneous PFO closure is reasonable in patients aged {{c1::18–60}} years with {{c2::nonlacunar cryptogenic stroke}} and high-risk PFO features after multidisciplinary evaluation.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.4.5 (PFO); CLOSE, RESPECT, REDUCE, DEFENSE-PFO trials

text: CHEST 2021 recommends anticoagulating cerebral vein/venous sinus thrombosis for at least {{c1::3 months}}, even in the presence of {{c2::intracranial hemorrhage}} (strong, low-certainty).
All stroke/TIA patients should be screened for {{c1::obstructive sleep apnea (OSA)}}; if diagnosed, treat with {{c2::CPAP}} to improve sleepiness and blood pressure control.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 4.6 (OSA); SAVE trial

text: Before planned pregnancy in LN, {{c1::MMF}} and {{c2::cyclophosphamide}} should be switched to safer alternatives (azathioprine, tacrolimus, or CNI-based regimens); hydroxychloroquine is continued throughout.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Special Populations

text: For IV resuscitation in acute pancreatitis, {{c1::lactated Ringer's solution}} is preferred over {{c2::normal saline}}.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Initial Management: early moderately aggressive LR hydration

text: AVR for chronic severe AR is triggered by symptoms, LVEF ≤{{c1::55%}}, or LVESD >{{c2::50 mm}} (indexed >25 mm/m²).
Bempedoic acid {{c1::180 mg}} daily reduces LDL-C by approximately {{c2::17–24%}}.
extra: Table 5, Table 6 — Lipid-lowering drug classes and doses

text: Decompressive hemicraniectomy for malignant MCA infarction should be performed within {{c1::48 hours}}, with the strongest benefit in patients ≤{{c2::60 years}}.
MMI should be held {{c1::2–3 days}} before RAI administration and resumed {{c2::3–7 days}} after RAI in high-risk patients.
For adults ≥65, preferred influenza vaccine formulations are {{c1::HD-IIV3}}, {{c2::RIV3}}, or {{c2::aIIV3}}.
extra: Notes — Influenza vaccination (preferred products ≥65), 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: In the HIMALAYA trial, durvalumab plus tremelimumab achieved a median overall survival of {{c1::16.4 months}} in advanced HCC.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Tables 7–8

text: Over-the-scope clips are reserved for {{c1::recurrent bleeding after prior successful endoscopic hemostasis}}, rather than as primary therapy.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Choice of endoscopic hemostatic therapy / Table 7

text: In hospitalized ASUC, VTE prophylaxis should be provided with {{c1::low molecular weight heparin}}.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: Inclisiran 284 mg SC is given at baseline, again at {{c1::3 months}}, then every {{c2::6 months}}, achieving ~48–52% LDL-C reduction.
extra: Table 5, Table 6 — Lipid-lowering drug classes and doses

text: RSV vaccination is routinely recommended for all adults ≥{{c1::75}} years; adults aged {{c2::50–74}} years should be offered RSV vaccine if they have risk factors.
extra: Table 1; Adult Schedule Notes — RSV, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: MMI is continued for {{c1::12–18 months}} for Graves' disease; TRAb should be checked before stopping because persistently elevated TRAb predicts {{c2::relapse}}.
extra: Recommendations 22–23 (Section E8) — 12–18 month MMI course and TRAb-guided discontinuation

text: In acute chest pain, a 12-lead ECG should be obtained and interpreted within {{c1::10 minutes}} of ED arrival.
All GC-treated patients should target calcium intake of {{c1::1,000–1,200 mg/day}} and vitamin D intake of {{c2::600–800 IU/day}}, maintaining serum 25(OH)D ≥20 ng/mL.
extra: ACR 2017 GIOP — Recommendations section; Thresholds & Doses

text: In NSTE-ACS, immediate angiography (<2 hours) is indicated for {{c1::hemodynamic or electrical instability}}, refractory angina, or acute heart failure.
For HAP/VAP, imipenem is dosed at {{c1::500 mg IV q6h}} (lower dose if <70 kg to prevent seizures) and meropenem at {{c2::1 g IV q8h}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3

text: To prevent medication-overuse headache, acute migraine medications should be limited to an average of ≤{{c1::2}} headache treatment days per week.
extra: AHS 2021 — Acute Treatment: Indications and Principles

text: The loading dose of valproate sodium for status epilepticus is {{c1::20–40 mg/kg}} IV, with an additional {{c2::20 mg/kg}} allowed if needed.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Urgent control therapy section; Thresholds & Doses

text: Severe-range hypertension in pregnancy (≥{{c1::160/110 mm Hg}}) requires treatment within {{c2::30–60 minutes}} to a target of <160/<110 mm Hg.
In critically ill medical patients, VTE prophylaxis should use {{c1::UFH or LMWH}} (strong), with {{c2::LMWH}} preferred over the other (conditional).
AKI in cirrhosis is defined as a rise in creatinine of ≥{{c1::0.3 mg/dL}} within 48 hours OR ≥{{c2::50%}} above baseline within 7 days.
After treatment for chlamydia, gonorrhea, or trichomoniasis, patients should be retested at {{c1::3 months}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — general retest recommendation

text: Seasonal influenza vaccine is recommended as {{c1::1 dose annually}} for all adults ≥19 years.
extra: Notes — Influenza vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: When using erythropoiesis-stimulating agents in SCD, the hemoglobin ceiling is {{c1::10 g/dL}} (Hct {{c1::30%}}); the dose should be held or decreased above this threshold to reduce vaso-occlusion, stroke, and VTE risk.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Hydroxyurea and Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents

text: Mepolizumab for COPD is dosed at {{c1::100 mg every 4 weeks}} subcutaneously.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.11 — Biologic therapy (MATINEE trial); Appendix 3

text: Uric acid–lowering therapy (preferably a {{c1::xanthine oxidase inhibitor}}) is recommended for symptomatic hyperuricemia or gout in CKD (1C), but should NOT be used to {{c2::slow CKD progression}} in asymptomatic hyperuricemia (2D).
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Key Recommendations

text: EBRT should be avoided in HCC patients with Child-Pugh score ≥{{c1::8}}, uncontrolled {{c2::ascites}}, or uncontrolled {{c3::encephalopathy}}.
ACEi or ARB should be discontinued if serum creatinine rises >{{c1::30%}} within {{c2::4 weeks}} of initiation.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Practice Points 3.6.1–3.6.7

text: Pregnant women with eclamptic seizures should be treated with {{c1::IV magnesium sulfate}} rather than standard AEDs, at a load of {{c2::4–6 g IV}} followed by 1–2 g/h.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Special populations section; Thresholds & Doses

text: For hemodynamically stable preexcited AF (WPW), use {{c1::ibutilide or procainamide}}; if unstable, perform {{c2::DCCV}}; definitive treatment is {{c3::accessory pathway ablation}}.
T therapy should {{c1::NOT be routinely prescribed}} to all men ≥65 with low testosterone; it should be offered only to those with consistent symptoms and unequivocally low morning testosterone.
In postmenopausal females with primary osteoporosis, first-line pharmacologic therapy to reduce fracture risk is {{c1::a bisphosphonate (alendronate, risedronate, or zoledronate)}}.
Prasugrel dosing in ACS: loading dose {{c1::60 mg}}, then {{c2::10 mg daily}}; reduce maintenance to 5 mg daily if <60 kg or ≥75 years.
In PLHIV aged 40–75 on stable ART, the preferred statin for primary prevention is {{c1::pitavastatin}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.7 — Primary prevention algorithm by risk category

text: In children with newly diagnosed ITP and non–life-threatening mucosal bleeding, corticosteroid course should be limited to {{c1::≤7 days}} (strong recommendation).
Treat-to-target in UC emphasizes {{c1::symptomatic remission}} plus {{c2::endoscopic improvement (MES ≤1)}}, assessed every {{c3::3 months}} during active disease.
extra: Goals for Managing Patients with UC / STRIDE-II

text: In children with FH, statins may be initiated at age ≥{{c1::8}} years if LDL-C remains ≥{{c2::160 mg/dL}} after lifestyle modification, targeting ≥50% reduction and LDL-C ≤130 mg/dL.
extra: Pediatric screening thresholds — Statin initiation in FH children

text: Inclisiran is dosed at {{c1::284 mg SC}} at day 0, day 90, then every {{c2::6 months}}, achieving approximately {{c3::48–52%}} LDL-C reduction.
The recommended delivered effluent volume for CRRT is {{c1::20–25 mL/kg/h}} (1A); the prescribed dose should be {{c2::25–30 mL/kg/h}} to account for downtime.
ATD-induced agranulocytosis occurs in {{c1::0.1–0.3%}} of patients, most commonly within the first {{c2::90 days}} of therapy.
For acute isolated distal DVT without severe symptoms or risk factors for extension, CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::serial ultrasound imaging for 2 weeks}} over anticoagulation.
In adult kidney transplant recipients with hypertension, KDIGO 2021 recommends {{c1::a dihydropyridine CCB or ARB}} as first-line antihypertensive therapy (1C).
extra: Chapter 4, Practice Point 4.1 and Recommendation 4.1 — KDIGO 2021 BP in CKD

text: For children ≤5 years with an acute asthma exacerbation, the target SpO₂ on supplemental oxygen is {{c1::≥92%}}.
Varenicline achieves a cessation rate of {{c1::25.6%}} vs 11.1% for control at ≥6 months (RR {{c2::2.24}}).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Benefits of Tobacco Cessation Interventions — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: Before H. pylori test of cure, hold {{c1::PPIs for 2 weeks}} and hold {{c2::bismuth/antibiotics for 4 weeks}} to avoid false-negative results.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Post-treatment testing for cure; Thresholds & Doses

text: As soon as pregnancy is confirmed, LT4 dose should be increased by {{c1::~2 extra tablets per week}} (approximately 29% increase); TSH should be monitored every {{c2::4–6 weeks}} in the first half of pregnancy.
extra: ATA 2014 Recommendation 6b; Thresholds & Doses — Pregnancy LT4 adjustment

text: For LDL-C estimation, the 2026 guideline recommends the {{c1::Martin/Hopkins or Sampson/NIH}} equation in preference to {{c2::Friedewald}} in all adults and children.
Natalizumab PML risk at 25–36 months (no prior immunosuppression) with JCV antibody index 0.9–1.5 is approximately {{c1::0.3/1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 16

text: The ACG 2024 guideline targets a local first-line H. pylori eradication rate of >{{c1::85%}}; susceptibility testing should be considered if rates fall below this threshold.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Antibiotic susceptibility testing; Thresholds & Doses

text: Severe CAP is defined by 1 major criterion: {{c1::septic shock requiring vasopressors}} OR {{c1::respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation}}; OR by ≥3 minor criteria.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Table 2 (2007/2019 IDSA/ATS severe CAP definition)

text: For SIHD with 3-vessel disease and normal EF, CABG to improve survival is classified as {{c1::Class 2b}} (downgraded from Class I) based on ISCHEMIA and contemporary trials.
extra: Section 7 / Figure 6 — SIHD revascularization; Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: ICS use in COPD: blood eosinophils <{{c1::100}} cells/µL argues against ICS; ≥{{c2::100}} to <300 cells/µL favors ICS; ≥{{c3::300}} cells/µL strongly favors ICS.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.10 — Factors favoring/against ICS use

text: Initial therapy for candidemia in both nonneutropenic and neutropenic adults is {{c1::an echinocandin (caspofungin, micafungin, or anidulafungin)}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section I, Recommendations 1–12

text: Pneumococcal vaccination (PCV15, PCV20, PCV21, or PPSV23) is recommended for all adults ≥{{c1::50}} years.
extra: Notes — Pneumococcal vaccination, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: AAMI/ISO standards for dialysate quality require bacteria <{{c1::100 CFU/mL}}, endotoxin <{{c2::0.5 EU/mL}}, and substitution fluid must be {{c3::sterile}} (1B).

The 2021 USPSTF recommends {{c1::selective/individualized}} colorectal cancer screening for adults aged 76-85 years (Grade {{c2::C}}), based on overall health, prior screening history, and patient preferences.

Bezlotoxumab is suggested as an adjunct to SOC antibiotics for patients who have had a recurrent CDI episode within the past {{c1::6 months}}, particularly those with additional recurrence risk factors.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Recommendation III (Bezlotoxumab); Table 4 (PICO 3): recurrence RR 0.62 (0.51–0.75)

text: In centers with appropriate expertise, {{c1::transbronchial lung cryobiopsy (TBLC)}} is an acceptable alternative to {{c2::surgical lung biopsy (SLB)}} for histopathologic diagnosis of ILD of undetermined type (conditional, very low quality).
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF — TBLC as acceptable alternative to SLB

text: In the fludrocortisone suppression test, fludrocortisone {{c1::0.1 mg PO q6h × 4 days}} is given; a day-4 upright PAC >{{c2::6 ng/dL}} with PRA <1 ng/mL/h confirms PA.
The USPSTF 2018 does not recommend using {{c1::digital rectal exam (DRE)}} as a prostate cancer screening modality due to insufficient evidence of benefit.
After treatment of a high-grade cervical precancerous lesion, screening surveillance should continue for at least {{c1::20 years}}, even if this extends {{c2::beyond age 65}}.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Women Older Than 65 Years; Thresholds & Doses — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: Fondaparinux (2.5 mg SC daily) is contraindicated if CrCl <{{c1::30 mL/min}} and should {{c2::not}} be used as the sole anticoagulant during PCI.
In vasomotor shock with AKI or at risk for AKI, {{c1::vasopressors}} should be used together with fluids (1C).
First-line treatment for vasovagal syncope is {{c1::patient education, reassurance, salt/fluid liberalization, and physical counterpressure maneuvers}}.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Section 5.1 and Figure 4

text: USPSTF 2022 recommends considering stopping aspirin around age {{c1::75}}, due to declining net benefit with advancing age.
For pure Class V (membranous) LN with nephrotic-range proteinuria, first-line immunosuppression is {{c1::MMF}} combined with antiproteinuric/supportive therapy.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Class V LN

text: Recommended protein intake in CKD with diabetes is {{c1::0.8}} g/kg/day for non-dialysis patients, and {{c2::1.0–1.2}} g/kg/day for patients on hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis (2C).
All HBsAg-positive liver transplant recipients require {{c1::lifelong NA prophylaxis}} (entecavir, TDF, or TAF) with or without HBIG; {{c2::HBIG monotherapy}} is inadequate.
Oral methotrexate is preferred initially over subcutaneous, with titration to a target dose of {{c1::≥15 mg weekly}} within {{c2::4–6 weeks}} of initiation.
Standard infliximab dosing for UC: {{c1::5 mg/kg}} IV at weeks {{c2::0, 2, 6}}, then every {{c3::8 weeks}}.
extra: Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC — induction/maintenance

text: Initial prednisone doses should never exceed {{c1::30 mg/day}} in PMR (strong recommendation).
In HFpEF (LVEF ≥50%), SGLT2 inhibitors carry a {{c1::Class 2a}} recommendation, while MRA and ARNi carry a {{c2::Class 2b}} recommendation.
The cost-effectiveness threshold for HCC surveillance in cirrhosis is an annual HCC incidence of ≥{{c1::1.0%}} (lowered from the prior threshold of 1.5%).
Complete renal response in lupus nephritis is defined as proteinuria {{c1::<0.5 g/g creatinine}} with {{c2::stable or improved eGFR}}.
Empiric MRSA coverage is added when local S. aureus methicillin resistance exceeds {{c1::10–20%}} for VAP or >{{c2::20%}} for HAP, or when prevalence is unknown.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Thresholds & Doses, MRSA empiric coverage threshold

text: The blood pressure target in PAD is <{{c1::130/80 mm Hg}}, using {{c2::ACE inhibitors or ARBs}} as first-line agents.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — blood pressure target in PAD

text: For acute isolated distal DVT without severe symptoms or extension risk factors, CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::serial ultrasound for 2 weeks}} rather than immediate anticoagulation.
The three evidence-based beta blockers approved for HFrEF GDMT are {{c1::bisoprolol}}, {{c1::carvedilol}}, and {{c1::sustained-release metoprolol succinate}}.
The recommended pediatric dexamethasone dose for ITP is {{c1::0.6 mg/kg/day}} (maximum {{c2::40 mg/day}}) × 4 days.
To confirm hypogonadism, measure fasting morning total testosterone on {{c1::two separate days}} using a {{c2::CDC-certified accuracy-based assay}}.
When prednisone >7.5 mg/day, FRAX-derived major OP fracture risk is multiplied by {{c1::1.15}} and hip fracture risk by {{c2::1.2}}.
Shared decision-making regarding ICD {{c1::deactivation of antitachycardia therapy}} should be initiated at implantation, generator change, and in {{c2::terminally ill}} patients.
extra: Sections 14–15 — terminal care and ICD deactivation

text: Psyllium has an NNT of approximately {{c1::7}} for overall IBS symptom improvement.
extra: Recommendation on fiber — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: PTU should be discontinued if transaminases rise above {{c1::3× ULN}} in adults (or {{c2::2–3× ULN}} in children).
In suspected NAFLD, {{c1::FIB-4}} is recommended as the first-line primary risk stratification tool.
In the ADA Standards of Care evidence-grading system, a Grade {{c1::E}} recommendation reflects {{c2::expert consensus}} in the absence of adequate clinical evidence.
extra: Table 1, ADA evidence-grading system — definitions of A, B, C, E levels

text: At follow-up, intermediate-low risk PAH patients should have {{c1::selexipag added}} or the PDE5i switched to {{c1::riociguat}}.
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10, Figure 9 — PAH treatment algorithm

text: The NLST trial demonstrated a ~{{c1::20%}} relative reduction in lung cancer mortality with LDCT screening (approximately {{c2::16%}} with extended follow-up).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Benefits — NLST mortality data

text: IM midazolam dose for status epilepticus: {{c1::0.2 mg/kg}} (max {{c2::10 mg}}).
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Emergent initial therapy section; Thresholds & Doses

text: The 2024 USPSTF cervical cancer screening recommendation does NOT apply to individuals with {{c1::HIV, immunocompromise, in utero DES exposure, or prior high-grade lesion/cervical cancer}}, who require individualized follow-up.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Patient Population Under Consideration — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: Bempedoic acid is dosed at {{c1::180 mg PO daily}}, reducing LDL-C by approximately {{c2::17–24%}}.
Test of cure for gonococcal pharyngitis should be performed {{c1::7–14 days}} after treatment.
extra: Treatment of Gonococcal Infections, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: A single-sample hs-cTn rule-out strategy is valid only if symptoms began ≥{{c1::3 hours}} before ED arrival.
The US guideline limits sodium correction to {{c1::10–12 mmol/L per day}}, reduced to {{c2::8 mmol/L per day}} in patients at high risk for osmotic demyelination syndrome.
extra: Table 2 — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017; Verbalis et al. 2013

text: Before implementing depression screening, USPSTF requires that adequate systems for {{c1::diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up}} are in place.
Injectable testosterone undecanoate (US) is given at {{c1::750 mg IM}} at weeks 0 and 4, then every {{c2::10 weeks}}.
Skeletal muscle tolerates ischemia for approximately {{c1::4–6 hours}} before irreversible damage occurs.
extra: Section 11, Figure 8 — ALI diagnosis and management algorithm

text: An IMPROVE bleeding score of ≥{{c1::7}} indicates high bleeding risk, favoring {{c2::mechanical}} over pharmacologic prophylaxis.
Compared to the 2013 USPSTF statement, the 2021 update expanded the eligible age range from {{c1::55–80}} to {{c2::50–80}} years.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation (Box) — A-55-80-30-15 replaced by A-50-80-20-15

text: Asymptomatic candiduria does NOT require treatment except in {{c1::neutropenic patients}}, {{c1::very low birth weight infants}}, or {{c1::patients undergoing urologic procedures}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline — Section XIV, Recommendations 97–114

text: After adequate time on DMT with good adherence, consider switching if the patient has ≥{{c1::1}} relapse, ≥{{c2::2}} unequivocally new MRI lesions, or increased disability over {{c2::1 year}} (Level B).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Recommendation 1c

text: For steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome in children and steroid-resistant primary FSGS in adults, preferred therapy is {{c1::cyclosporine or tacrolimus}} for ≥{{c2::6 months}}.
extra: KDIGO 2021 Glomerular Diseases, Chapter 4, Recommendation 4.4.1; Chapter 6, Recommendation 6.3.1.1

text: Pure/uncontaminated oats {{c1::can}} be included in a gluten-free diet, but patients should be monitored for {{c2::clinical or serologic relapse}} in the minority who are intolerant.
Teriflunomide carries potential teratogenic risk from male sperm for up to {{c1::2 years}} after cessation without accelerated elimination; men should be counseled before starting (Level B).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendations; Switching Recommendation 10

text: FeNO supporting Type 2 asthma: >{{c1::50}} ppb in adults/adolescents and >{{c2::35}} ppb in children.
For suspected GCA in the US, the first-line diagnostic test is {{c1::unilateral temporal artery biopsy}}, preferred over temporal artery ultrasound or cranial MRI.
extra: Table 2 — GCA diagnostic testing recommendations (PICO 1–9); Discussion — divergence from EULAR

text: Continuous EEG monitoring should be initiated in any patient who does not return to baseline within {{c1::10 minutes}} of clinical seizure cessation, or who is in coma or receiving IV anesthetic infusion.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Diagnostic evaluation and EEG monitoring section

text: The clinical trial definition of severe psoriasis requires PASI ≥{{c1::12}} AND body surface area ≥{{c2::10%}}.
For PsA patients with frequent serious infections, the guideline STRONGLY recommends starting an {{c1::OSM}} over a {{c2::TNFi biologic}}; IL-12/23i or IL-17i are preferred over TNFi in this setting.
Basilar artery occlusion EVT is recommended within {{c1::24 hours}} when NIHSS ≥{{c2::10}} and PC-ASPECTS ≥{{c3::6}} with baseline mRS 0–1.

Class I sodium channel blockers ({{c1::flecainide, propafenone, encainide}}) are contraindicated in patients with prior MI or structural heart disease due to increased mortality demonstrated in the {{c2::CAST}} trial.
GLP-1 RAs with proven cardiovascular benefit preferred in CKD include {{c1::liraglutide}}, {{c1::semaglutide}}, and {{c1::dulaglutide}}.
For cancer-associated thrombosis, CHEST 2021 recommends an oral {{c1::Xa inhibitor}} (apixaban, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban) over {{c2::LMWH}} (strong, moderate-certainty).

In non-intubated patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure (not from cardiogenic edema or COPD), the 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM guideline recommends {{c1::HFNO}} rather than {{c1::conventional oxygen therapy}} to reduce intubation risk.
Long-term beta blockers are NOT recommended in CCD patients without {{c1::MI in the past year}}, {{c2::LVEF ≤50%}}, or another primary indication (angina, HTN, arrhythmia).
extra: Section 4.3.2 Beta Blockers — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: The overall natalizumab PML risk across all patients is approximately {{c1::4/1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 16

text: First-line treatment for ectopic ACTH-dependent Cushing's syndrome is {{c1::tumor resection with nodal dissection}}.
extra: Recommendation 3.1a–3.1c — First-line surgical approach by etiology

text: IV iron (rather than oral iron) is reserved for IDA patients with {{c1::malabsorption, prior gastric surgery, IBD, CKD, or blood loss exceeding oral repletion capacity}}.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Section 'How should iron supplementation be managed?'

text: AAA screening with {{c1::ultrasound}} is recommended for men ≥{{c2::65}} years with a smoking history; AAA is defined as aortic diameter >3.0 cm.
Women with trichomoniasis are treated with {{c1::metronidazole 500 mg orally twice daily for 7 days}} (not the single 2 g dose); women should be retested at {{c2::3 months}}.
extra: Trichomoniasis, Recommended Regimens — 7-day metronidazole based on Kissinger RCT, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: In euvolemic patients with acute pancreatitis, the recommended initial IV hydration rate is {{c1::1.5 mL/kg/hr}}; if hypovolemic, give a {{c2::10 mL/kg}} bolus first.
extra: ACG 2024 AP Guideline — Initial Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: Fixed-rate IV insulin infusion for moderate/severe DKA is {{c1::0.1 units/kg/h}}; for HHS without significant ketosis, start at {{c2::0.05 units/kg/h}}.
In nondiabetic, insulin-resistant patients with prior stroke, {{c1::pioglitazone}} reduces recurrent stroke and MI risk, as shown in the {{c2::IRIS}} trial.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 4.4 (Glucose); IRIS trial

text: For stage B (pre-HF) patients, ACEi/ARB and beta blocker should be initiated if LVEF is {{c1::≤40%}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Key Recommendations / Stage B

text: VT/VF storm is defined as ≥{{c1::3}} episodes of sustained VT/VF or appropriate ICD shocks within {{c2::24 hours}}.

Cardiogenic shock is defined by SBP {{c1::<90 mm Hg}} for >30 minutes (or vasopressor-dependent) plus evidence of hypoperfusion (lactate {{c2::>2 mmol/L}}, urine output {{c3::<30 mL/h}}, altered mentation, or cool extremities).
For familial chylomicronemia syndrome with TG ≥{{c1::1000 mg/dL}}, {{c2::olezarsen}} (an apoC3 inhibitor) {{c3::80 mg SC monthly}} is recommended alongside a very-low-fat diet.
extra: Section 4.2.9 / Figures 14–17 — FCS management with olezarsen

text: Tdap should be administered {{c1::once per pregnancy}}, regardless of prior Tdap history.
extra: Notes — Tdap/Td (pregnancy), 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Rivaroxaban for cancer-associated thrombosis: {{c1::15 mg BID × 21 days}}, then {{c2::20 mg daily}} for ≥6 months.
Per 2021 USPSTF, average-risk adults should begin colorectal cancer screening at age {{c1::45}} years.
extra: Practice Considerations: Starting and Stopping Ages; Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation (lowered from 50 to 45 in 2021)

text: High fracture risk (≥40 years) includes FRAX 10-yr major OP fracture ≥{{c1::20%}} or hip fracture ≥{{c2::3%}}, prior OP fracture, or T-score ≤-2.5.
A multidisciplinary {{c1::Heart Team}} approach is recommended when optimal revascularization strategy is unclear, especially for {{c2::left main}}, complex multivessel, or diabetic multivessel CAD.
extra: Section 3 / Table 4 — Heart Team factors and STS risk score

text: Papillary microcarcinoma is defined as ≤{{c1::1 cm}}; its disease-specific mortality is {{c2::<1%}} with recurrence rates of 2–6%.
KDIGO 2017 recommends initiating phosphate-lowering therapy only for {{c1::elevated (overt or progressive) hyperphosphatemia}}, not preventively in normophosphatemic CKD patients.
ACR 2020 recommends switching {{c1::hydrochlorothiazide}} to an alternate antihypertensive in gout patients and preferring {{c2::losartan}} when feasible due to its uricosuric effect.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 8 — Concurrent medications

text: In treatment-naive non-cirrhotic patients, SVR is assessed by HCV RNA {{c1::4 weeks}} after completing therapy.
extra: Follow-Up Visit algorithm — HCV RNA 4 weeks post-therapy for SVR determination

text: M. genitalium is treated with a two-stage approach: doxycycline 100 mg BID × 7 days, then — if macrolide-sensitive — {{c1::azithromycin}} or — if macrolide-resistant — {{c2::moxifloxacin 400 mg daily × 7 days}}.
extra: Mycoplasma genitalium, Treatment — resistance-guided two-stage therapy, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: Ramucirumab is indicated as second-line systemic therapy for HCC only if AFP ≥{{c1::400 ng/mL}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Tables 7–8

text: Rituximab induction dosing options (adults): {{c1::375 mg/m²}} IV weekly × 4 OR {{c2::1000 mg}} IV on days 1 and 15.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 1 (drug dosing regimens)

text: Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention is a {{c1::Class IIb}} recommendation and may be considered only in select adults aged {{c2::40–70}} years at higher ASCVD risk who are not at increased bleeding risk.
ACG 2023 does {{c1::not}} recommend {{c2::probiotics or gluten detection devices (food, urine, stool)}} as routine adjuncts to the gluten-free diet due to insufficient evidence.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Medical device use / Figure 3

text: {{c1::Evinacumab}} {{c2::15 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks}} is indicated for {{c3::HoFH}}, achieving approximately 49% LDL-C reduction.
The 2026 ACC/AHA guideline uses PREVENT-ASCVD equations to classify 10-year ASCVD risk as: low {{c1::<3%}}, borderline {{c1::3–<5%}}, intermediate {{c1::5–<10%}}, or high {{c1::≥10%}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.2 / Tables 11–12 — PREVENT-ASCVD equations and risk categories

text: For VKA-associated ICH with INR ≥2, give {{c1::4-factor PCC}} (preferred over FFP) plus {{c2::IV vitamin K 5–10 mg}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: After SVR in HCV-related cirrhosis, HCC surveillance should be continued {{c1::indefinitely}}; surveillance is NOT routinely indicated in post-SVR HCV patients {{c2::without cirrhosis}}.

Lipid formulation amphotericin B is reserved for candidiasis patients with {{c1::intolerance, resistance, or suspected azole- and echinocandin-resistant Candida}}; standard dosing is {{c2::3–5 mg/kg/day}} (5 mg/kg for CNS disease).
For a PMR relapse, prednisone should be increased to the {{c1::pre-relapse dose}}, then re-tapered to the relapse dose over {{c2::4–8 weeks}}.
Barrett's esophagus is considered diagnostic of GERD when the segment is {{c1::>3 cm}} with {{c2::intestinal metaplasia}} confirmed on biopsy.
extra: Diagnosis of GERD / Endoscopy section — LA grading and criteria

text: In hemodynamically stable hospitalized patients with UGIB, the restrictive RBC transfusion threshold is hemoglobin {{c1::<7 g/dL}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Red blood cell transfusion section / Table 3 (Villanueva RCT)

text: {{c1::Mobile stroke units (MSUs)}} are preferred over conventional EMS where available to accelerate thrombolytic treatment and improve functional outcomes (B_PROUD and BEST-MSU trials).
extra: Section 2.5, COR 1 — Role of Mobile Stroke Units

text: Supplemental oxygen in AIS should be used only if SpO2 falls below {{c1::94%}}; routine oxygen for nonhypoxic patients is not recommended.
extra: Key Recommendations — Oxygen Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: Excessive diurnal PEF variability is defined as >{{c1::10%}} in adults and >{{c2::13%}} in children.
After 5-ASA failure, do {{c1::not switch 5-ASA formulations}}; instead escalate to {{c2::budesonide MMX or systemic steroids}}, then advanced therapy.
extra: Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC

text: The blood pressure target in adults with CKD is SBP <{{c1::120}} mm Hg, measured using {{c2::standardized office}} technique when tolerated.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Thresholds & Doses (BP target)

text: Personalized dosimetry for TARE (transarterial radioembolization) targets a dose of >{{c1::205 Gy}} to the tumor.
The physiologic criterion for PPF includes an absolute decline in FVC of ≥{{c1::5%}} predicted within 1 year.
extra: Table 4 — Definition of Progressive Pulmonary Fibrosis; Thresholds & Doses

text: Lipid screening should begin in adults at age {{c1::19 years}} (every {{c2::5 years}}) and in children once at age {{c3::9–11 years}}.
AAA elective repair is indicated at ≥{{c1::5.5 cm}} in men and ≥{{c2::5.0 cm}} in women.
The European protocol for acute severely symptomatic hyponatremia is {{c1::3% NaCl 150 mL over 20 min}}, repeated {{c2::2–3 times}} as needed.
extra: Treatment of Acute Hyponatremia section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017; Spasovski et al. 2014

text: In adults, a TTG IgA ≥10× ULN has a positive predictive value of ~{{c1::95%}} for celiac disease, supporting after-the-fact diagnosis when biopsy is not feasible.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diagnosis: Evidence and rationale

text: The AGA 2022 lifestyle intervention target paired with AOMs is a {{c1::500–600 kcal/day}} deficit plus ≥{{c2::150 min/week}} of physical activity.
Rectal 5-ASA is dosed at {{c1::1 g/day}} as an enema or suppository.
extra: Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC — 5-ASA dosing

text: The 2026 guideline recommends against adding {{c1::niacin or fibrates}} to a statin for {{c2::ASCVD prevention}}.
MELD exception for HCC is granted {{c1::6 months}} after listing at {{c2::MMaT-3}} for Milan or successfully downstaged candidates.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Liver Transplantation / Figure 12

text: {{c1::Nintedanib}} is conditionally recommended for PPF in patients who have failed standard management for their underlying fibrotic ILD (conditional, low quality).
extra: Part II Evidence-based Recommendations — nintedanib conditional recommendation for PPF

text: Initial prednisone doses of {{c1::≤7.5 mg/day}} should be avoided in PMR (conditional recommendation).
In patients at risk for or with renal dysfunction or bone disease, {{c1::TAF}} or {{c1::entecavir}} is preferred over {{c2::TDF}}.
Eluxadoline for IBS-D is contraindicated in patients {{c1::without a gallbladder}}, with a history of {{c2::pancreatitis}}, or consuming {{c2::>3 alcoholic drinks per day}}.
extra: Recommendation on eluxadoline — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: In adult adrenal crisis, fluid resuscitation begins with {{c1::1000 mL isotonic saline}} in the {{c2::first hour}}.
The 2024 USPSTF assigns an {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement to screening mammography in women aged {{c2::≥75}} years.
extra: I statements — women ≥75 and supplemental screening for dense breasts

text: Pulmonary rehabilitation requires a minimum of {{c1::6–8 weeks}} with supervised exercise at least {{c2::2×}} weekly.
In ASUC with albumin <{{c1::2.5 g/dL}}, consider intensified infliximab dosing at {{c2::10 mg/kg}}.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: For recurrent CDI, {{c1::fidaxomicin}} (standard or extended-pulsed regimen) is preferred over a standard vancomycin course (conditional, low certainty).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Recommendation II (Recurrent CDI); Table 3 (PICO 2): sustained response RR 1.27 (1.05–1.54)

text: For active severe TAK, initiate {{c1::high-dose oral glucocorticoids}} plus a non-glucocorticoid immunosuppressive agent, with {{c2::methotrexate, azathioprine, or a TNF inhibitor}} preferred over tocilizumab as initial therapy.
extra: Table 4 — TAK medical management recommendations; Figure 2

text: In AF, oral anticoagulation is recommended when CHA2DS2-VASc is ≥{{c1::2}} in men or ≥{{c2::3}} in women (annual thromboembolic risk ≥2%).
extra: Section 6.1 and Table 8 — CHA2DS2-VASc risk thresholds

text: Lubiprostone for IBS-C is dosed at {{c1::8 μg PO BID with meals}} and is indicated for {{c2::women}} (NNT 12.5).
extra: Recommendations on IBS-C secretagogues — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: ADA PPC members must disclose conflicts of interest from {{c1::1 year prior to PPC appointment}} through publication; PPC chairs must remain unconflicted for {{c2::1 year post-publication}}.
extra: Methodology and Procedure — conflict-of-interest disclosure policy

text: OnabotulinumtoxinA for chronic migraine prevention is given at {{c1::155 units}} per the PREEMPT protocol, administered {{c2::quarterly}}.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 6: Medications with evidence of efficacy in migraine prevention

text: HBsAg-positive pregnant women with HBV DNA >{{c1::200,000 IU/mL}} should start {{c2::TDF}} at {{c3::28–32 weeks}} gestation to prevent perinatal transmission.
extra: AASLD 2018 HBV Guidance — Section 1C & Guidance Statements on Pregnancy

text: In HIT with an intermediate/high-probability 4Ts score (≥4), the immediate steps are to {{c1::discontinue heparin, start a non-heparin anticoagulant, and order an immunoassay}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendations 2.1–2.10 and Figure 1

text: {{c1::TNF-α inhibitors}} are strongly recommended against in the treatment of PMR.

Edoxaban is dosed at {{c1::60 mg daily}} for CrCl >50 to ≤95 mL/min and {{c2::30 mg daily}} for CrCl 15–50 mL/min; it is contraindicated if CrCl >{{c3::95}} mL/min (increased stroke risk) or <15 mL/min.
When IV access is unavailable in status epilepticus, the preferred benzodiazepine route is {{c1::IM midazolam}}.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Emergent initial therapy section; Silbergleit RAMPART trial (ref 84)

text: When RAI is given in patients with mild active Graves' orbitopathy and risk factors, prophylactic prednisone is started at {{c1::0.4–0.5 mg/kg/day}} beginning {{c2::1–3 days after RAI}}, tapered over 3 months.
extra: Recommendations 99–108, Tables 12–14 — GO prophylactic glucocorticoids with RAI

text: Garcia-Albeniz 2016 found {{c1::7.3}} excess IE cases per 10,000 colonoscopies (with biopsy/polypectomy) compared with no colonoscopy in high-risk Medicare patients.
extra: Garcia-Albeniz et al 2016 (Ref 20) — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Rifabutin triple therapy (Talicia) for H. pylori consists of omeprazole {{c1::40 mg}} + rifabutin {{c2::50 mg}} + amoxicillin {{c3::1 g}}, each given every 8 hours for 14 days.
In selected STEMI patients with cardiogenic shock, a microaxial flow pump was associated with a {{c1::26%}} relative reduction in 180-day mortality in the DanGer-SHOCK trial (NNT = {{c2::8}}).
CKD is defined as abnormalities of kidney structure or function persisting for ≥{{c1::3 months}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (CKD definition)

text: CD4 monitoring can be discontinued once CD4 has remained >{{c1::250}}/μL for {{c2::1 year}}.
Primary prevention ICD is indicated in ischemic cardiomyopathy with LVEF {{c1::≤30%}} and NYHA class {{c2::I}} HF on GDMT ≥3 months and ≥40 days post-MI.
extra: Section 7.1.2, Figure 3–4 — primary prevention ICD in ischemic cardiomyopathy

text: In GOLD 2026, Group E is defined as ≥{{c1::1}} moderate OR severe exacerbation in the prior year — lowered from the previous threshold of ≥2.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 2, Figure 2.13 — ABE Assessment Tool (new 2026 threshold)

text: In pregnant women with hyperthyroidism, {{c1::PTU}} is preferred in the first trimester; switching to {{c2::MMI}} may be considered in the second trimester.

In SCD, diagnostic echocardiography or PH expert consultation is indicated for unexplained {{c1::dyspnea}}, {{c1::hypoxemia}}, {{c1::chest pain}}, {{c1::syncope}}, exercise limitation, signs of heart failure, or history of pulmonary embolism.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Hypertension: Screening

text: In postmenopausal women and men with IDA, the pooled diagnostic yield for lower GI malignancy is {{c1::8.9%}} and for upper GI malignancy is {{c2::2.0%}}.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Recommendation 2; Thresholds & Doses

text: ART should be initiated within {{c1::7 days}} of HIV diagnosis, ideally on the {{c2::same day}}, unless a concomitant opportunistic infection is suspected.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 1 — timing of ART initiation

text: The target mid-interval serum testosterone for injectable enanthate/cypionate is {{c1::350–600 ng/dL}} (14.1–24.5 nmol/L).
Amiodarone for hemodynamically stable VT: initial bolus {{c1::150 mg IV}}, followed by 1 mg/min × 6 h, then 0.5 mg/min × 18 h.
Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention increases major GI bleeding by approximately {{c1::58%}} (Peto OR {{c2::1.58}}, 95% CI 1.38–1.80).
For ambulatory cancer patients with Khorana score ≥2, primary prophylaxis options include {{c1::apixaban 2.5 mg BID}} (AVERT) or {{c2::rivaroxaban 10 mg daily}} (CASSINI) for 6 months.
extra: Appendix 6 Q5 Conclusions — AVERT and CASSINI trials

text: In the European diagnostic algorithm, a urine osmolality {{c1::<100 mOsm/kg}} suggests primary polydipsia, low solute intake, or beer potomania, while ≥100 mOsm/kg points to vasopressin-driven causes.
Adults with celiac disease and functional hyposplenism should receive either {{c1::1 dose PCV15 followed by PPSV23 ≥1 year later}}, OR {{c1::1 dose PCV20 alone}}.
The major change from the 2012 USPSTF cervical cancer screening guideline is the addition of {{c1::primary hrHPV testing every 5 years}} as a stand-alone option for women ≥30.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: The 2016 IDSA/ATS guideline recommends diagnosing VAP using {{c1::noninvasive sampling (endotracheal aspirate)}} with {{c2::semiquantitative}} cultures rather than bronchoscopic quantitative cultures.
For superficial vein thrombosis at increased risk of progression, CHEST 2021 recommends anticoagulation for {{c1::45 days}}.
USPSTF 2021 assigns Grade {{c1::A}} to providing both behavioral counseling and FDA-approved pharmacotherapy for tobacco cessation in {{c2::nonpregnant}} adults.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: For patients with eGFR <{{c1::30}} ml/min/1.73 m², only ACR group {{c2::II or III}} gadolinium-based contrast agents should be used.
Evidence-based short-course antibiotic durations: CAP {{c1::3–5 days}}; VAP {{c2::7–8 days}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XIII, Recommendation 14 and Table 2

text: According to IDSA 2019, ASB should be {{c1::screened for and treated}} in {{c2::pregnant women}} at an early prenatal visit.
LT4 absorption is reduced by {{c1::calcium}}, {{c2::iron}}, PPIs, bile acid sequestrants, and phosphate binders; these should be separated from the LT4 dose.
For treatment-phase anticoagulation in DVT/PE, CHEST 2021 makes a strong recommendation to use {{c1::apixaban, dabigatran, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban}} over {{c2::VKA}}.
For stable chest pain with no known CAD, noninvasive testing provides benefit when pretest probability of obstructive CAD is >{{c1::15%}}; testing should be deferred at ≤{{c1::15%}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline, Section 5.1.2 / Figure 11

text: A 2-dose varicella vaccine series is indicated for adults born in {{c1::1980 or later}} who lack evidence of immunity.
extra: Notes — Varicella vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Long-term NIV is considered for COPD patients with persistent PaCO2 >{{c1::53 mmHg}} following a hospitalization for exacerbation.
MenB vaccine for adults aged {{c1::19–23}} years is offered via {{c2::shared clinical decision-making}}.
extra: Notes — Meningococcal vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The US protocol for acute severely symptomatic hyponatremia is {{c1::3% NaCl 100 mL over 10 min}}, repeated up to {{c2::3 times}} as needed.
extra: Treatment of Acute Hyponatremia section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017; Verbalis et al. 2013

text: In resistant hypertension, spironolactone {{c1::25–50 mg/day}} is recommended as fourth-line therapy when eGFR is ≥{{c2::45 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
Furosemide for ascites is started at {{c1::40 mg/day}} and titrated to a maximum of {{c2::160 mg/day}}.
Bariatric surgery should be avoided in patients with NASH and {{c1::decompensated cirrhosis}}.
Obtaining ≥4 total duodenal biopsy specimens nearly triples the diagnostic yield for celiac disease ({{c1::1.8%}} vs {{c1::0.7%}}).
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diagnosis: Evidence and rationale

text: The appropriate initial test in patients with LBBB is {{c1::echocardiography}}, because of the high likelihood of underlying structural heart disease and LV systolic dysfunction.
LT4 oral bioavailability under fasting conditions is {{c1::70–80%}}; its serum half-life is approximately {{c2::7 days}}.
Postoperative glucocorticoid replacement in hypocortisolemic patients is hydrocortisone {{c1::10–12 mg/m²/day}} given in {{c2::2–3 divided doses}}, with the first dose on waking.
In patients with T2D and ASCVD after ischemic stroke, a {{c1::GLP-1 receptor agonist}} or {{c1::SGLT2 inhibitor}} should be added to metformin for cardiovascular risk reduction.
For active non-severe EGPA, the preferred induction regimen is {{c1::mepolizumab}} plus glucocorticoids (over MTX, azathioprine, or MMF).
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Recommendations 1–4 (EGPA); Table 3

text: Established catheter-related thrombosis should be treated with at least {{c1::3 months}} of anticoagulation, with {{c2::LMWH}} preferred.
extra: Appendix 6 Q7 Conclusions — CVC thrombosis management

text: For initial therapy of newly diagnosed adult ITP, use {{c1::corticosteroids alone}} rather than rituximab plus corticosteroids.
extra: Recommendation 5 — corticosteroids alone vs rituximab + corticosteroids initial therapy

text: In AIS, fever >{{c1::37.5°C}} should be treated to maintain normothermia; therapeutic {{c2::hypothermia}} should NOT be induced.
extra: Key Recommendations — Temperature Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: CHEST 2021 recommends treating incidentally found asymptomatic PE {{c1::the same as symptomatic PE}}.
Prasugrel dosing: load {{c1::60 mg}}, maintenance {{c2::10 mg}} daily; reduce maintenance to 5 mg if weight <60 kg or age ≥75 years.

The decision to initiate antibiotics for suspected HAP/VAP should be based on {{c1::clinical criteria alone}}; biomarkers such as PCT, sTREM-1, CRP, and CPIS should {{c2::not}} be used to guide this decision.
Infliximab for Crohn's disease is dosed at {{c1::5 mg/kg IV}} at weeks 0, 2, 6, then every {{c2::8 weeks}}; target trough is ≥{{c1::7.5 µg/mL}}.
Two antipseudomonal agents from different classes should be used empirically when >{{c1::10%}} of local gram-negatives are resistant to the candidate monotherapy agent.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Thresholds & Doses, dual antipseudomonal coverage threshold

text: USPSTF 2022 states aspirin should {{c1::not}} be used for primary prevention with the goal of reducing {{c2::colorectal cancer}} incidence or mortality, due to inadequate evidence.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation — USPSTF 2022

text: Per ILAE, a recurrence risk of ≥{{c1::60%}} over 10 years after a single unprovoked seizure justifies diagnosing and treating as epilepsy.
extra: Clinical Context — ILAE practical clinical definition of epilepsy

text: After a benzodiazepine in status epilepticus, an urgent-control IV AED (fosphenytoin/phenytoin, valproate, levetiracetam, or phenobarbital) should be given {{c1::immediately, regardless of whether seizures have stopped}}.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Urgent control therapy section

text: The exercise prescription to reduce AF burden is {{c1::210 minutes/week}} of moderate-to-vigorous {{c2::aerobic exercise}}.
extra: Section 5.2 — lifestyle and risk factor modification

text: For PH associated with ILD, the target dose of inhaled treprostinil (INCREASE trial) is {{c1::72 µg four times daily}}.
extra: Table 19 — PAH drug dosing in adults; Recommendation Tables 22–23 — group 3 management

text: Initial MMI dose is {{c1::5–10 mg/day}} when free T4 is {{c1::1–1.5× ULN}}.
extra: ATA 2016 — Initial MMI dosing by free T4 level

text: In secondary prevention not at very high risk, the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<70 mg/dL}} and the non–HDL-C goal is {{c2::<100 mg/dL}}.
In AF with ACS/PCI, {{c1::dual therapy (DOAC + P2Y12 inhibitor)}} is preferred over triple therapy after a short period of ≤{{c2::1 week}} of triple therapy to reduce bleeding risk.
Compared to the 2013 USPSTF statement, the 2021 update lowered the pack-year threshold from {{c1::30}} to {{c2::20}} pack-years.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation (Box) — A-55-80-30-15 replaced by A-50-80-20-15

text: After the pregnancy Tdap dose, a Td or Tdap booster is recommended every {{c1::10 years}}.
extra: Notes — Tdap/Td (decennial booster), 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: HPV vaccine requires {{c1::2 doses}} if initiated before age 15, or {{c2::3 doses}} if initiated at age ≥15 or in immunocompromised individuals.
extra: Notes — HPV vaccination, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Finerenone (nonsteroidal MRA) is indicated in T2D with eGFR ≥{{c1::25}} ml/min/1.73 m², normal serum potassium, and persistent ACR ≥{{c2::30 mg/g}} despite maximally tolerated RAS inhibitor (2A).
IV pulse methylprednisolone for GCA is dosed at {{c1::500–1,000 mg/day}} in adults for {{c2::3–5 days}}.
extra: Table 1 — glucocorticoid dose categories; Table 3 — GCA treatment

text: Combination NRT — a {{c1::long-acting patch}} plus a short-acting form (gum, lozenge, inhaler, or nasal spray) — is more effective than {{c2::single-form NRT}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Tobacco Cessation — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: For GCA induction, use prednisone {{c1::40–60 mg/day}}, taper to ≤{{c2::5 mg/day}} by 1 year; add {{c3::tocilizumab}} for refractory or relapsing disease.
Phentermine and phentermine-containing drugs should be held ≥{{c1::4 days}} before procedures requiring {{c2::anesthesia}}.

After PCI in CCD, standard DAPT duration is {{c1::6 months}}; shorter durations of {{c2::1-3 months}} followed by P2Y12 monotherapy are reasonable when bleeding risk is high and ischemic risk is low-moderate.
extra: Section 4.3.1 Antiplatelet Therapy; Figure 9 — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Anabolic agents for osteoporosis (romosozumab or teriparatide) are reserved for females at {{c1::very high}} fracture risk.
In CKD G5/G5D, serum calcium and phosphate should be monitored every {{c1::1–3 months}}, and PTH every {{c2::3–6 months}}.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Thresholds & Doses — monitoring intervals

text: Magnetic sphincter augmentation (MSA) is contraindicated in patients requiring MRI scanners of {{c1::>1.5 Tesla}}, and carries a device erosion risk of approximately {{c2::0.3%}} at 4 years.
For newly diagnosed GCA without cranial ischemia, induction therapy is {{c1::high-dose oral glucocorticoids}} combined with {{c2::tocilizumab}}.
T therapy should be stopped and the patient evaluated for hypoxia/OSA if hematocrit rises above {{c1::54%}}; therapy may be restarted at a reduced dose.
USPSTF 2018 recommends a PSA screening interval of every {{c1::2–4 years}} as a reasonable trade-off between mortality benefit and overdiagnosis (rather than annual screening).
{{c1::Intracoronary imaging (IVUS or OCT)}} is recommended to guide PCI in {{c2::complex coronary lesions}}.
extra: 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline — Top Take-Home Messages

text: In ACS patients requiring long-term anticoagulation after PCI, aspirin should be discontinued {{c1::1–4 weeks}} after PCI, continuing a P2Y12 inhibitor (preferably {{c2::clopidogrel}}) plus a DOAC.
In treatment-naive H. pylori patients with unknown susceptibility, the preferred first-line empiric regimen is {{c1::optimized bismuth quadruple therapy (BQT)}} for {{c2::14}} days.
extra: ACG 2024 – Figure 1, Table 5; Summary of recommendations for treatment-naive patients

text: Recommended imaging surveillance intervals for asymptomatic AS: severe AS every {{c1::6–12 months}}; moderate AS every {{c2::1–2 years}}; mild AS every {{c3::3–5 years}}.
When IV vasopressors are unavailable for HRS-AKI, the alternative is {{c1::midodrine 5–15 mg PO q8h}} plus {{c2::octreotide 100–200 µg q8h}}.
PA prevalence is ~{{c1::6%}} among all hypertensives and rises to {{c2::17–23%}} in resistant hypertension.
The 2025 guideline uses the {{c1::PREVENT}} equation (applicable ages {{c2::30–79}}) to estimate 10-year CVD risk, with a threshold of ≥7.5% to guide pharmacotherapy initiation.
extra: Section 5.2.2; Table 1 (What Is New) — replaces Pooled Cohort Equations

text: In patients with LVEF {{c1::36-50%}} and AV block expected to require >{{c2::40%}} ventricular pacing, CRT or His bundle pacing is preferred over RV pacing to prevent heart failure.
extra: Section 6.4.4.1 — pacing mode selection and CRT/His bundle pacing for LVEF 36-50%

text: Acceptable first-line options for PTH lowering in CKD G5D include {{c1::calcimimetics}}, {{c1::calcitriol}}, or {{c1::vitamin D analogs}} (alone or in combination).
Post-treatment test of cure should be performed ≥{{c1::4 weeks}} after completing H. pylori therapy using {{c2::urea breath test or fecal antigen}}; serology should not be used.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Post-treatment testing for cure

text: In the ACR/NPF 2018 PsA guideline, {{c1::94%}} of recommendations are conditional and {{c2::6%}} are strong, reflecting predominantly low/very-low-quality evidence.
Refer PAH patients for lung transplant evaluation when REVEAL score is >{{c1::7}}; list for transplant when REVEAL score is ≥{{c2::10}}.
extra: Recommendation Table 13 — lung transplantation referral and listing criteria

text: Oral 5-ASA induction dose is ≥{{c1::2 g/day}} (up to {{c2::4.8 g/day}} for moderate disease); maintenance is ≥2 g/day.
extra: Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC — 5-ASA dosing

text: ELF score ≥{{c1::9.8}} identifies progression risk; ≥{{c2::11.3}} predicts future liver-related events.
Romosozumab is classified as a {{c1::sclerostin inhibitor}} used as an anabolic agent in osteoporosis.
SGLT2i agents with proven kidney/CV benefit include: dapagliflozin {{c1::10 mg daily}} (eGFR ≥25), empagliflozin {{c1::10 mg daily}}, and canagliflozin {{c1::100 mg daily}} (eGFR ≥30).
PCI of an intermediate lesion is indicated when FFR is ≤{{c1::0.80}} or iFR is ≤{{c2::0.89}}; revascularization should be deferred if values are above these thresholds.
extra: Section 4.3 — FFR/iFR thresholds for PCI guidance

text: Low-dose aspirin reduces nonfatal ischemic stroke in primary prevention with a Peto OR of {{c1::0.88}} (95% CI 0.78–1.00).
Pasireotide-induced hyperglycemia occurs in approximately {{c1::73%}} of treated Cushing's patients.
Conditions that alter SHBG and prompt free testosterone measurement include obesity, T2DM, aging, HIV, hepatitis, hyperthyroidism, and {{c1::anticonvulsant use}}.
The ACG 2022 guideline recommends AGAINST using {{c1::barium radiography, laryngoscopy, salivary pepsin, or oropharyngeal pH testing}} to diagnose GERD or laryngopharyngeal reflux (LPR).

For NDIPs with routine surgical-site infection prophylaxis (e.g., CIED implantation, ERCP, transurethral/transrectal prostate procedures), perioperative antibiotic regimens in high-risk IE patients should be selected to also cover {{c1::likely IE pathogens}}.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Future Considerations

text: The clopidogrel loading dose is {{c1::600 mg}} for primary PCI, but reduced to {{c2::300 mg}} when given after fibrinolysis.

Neutropenia in SSTI guidelines is defined as ANC <{{c1::500}} cells/µL or expected to fall below {{c1::500}} within {{c2::48 hours}}; high risk includes prolonged neutropenia >7 days with ANC <{{c2::100}} or MASCC score <21.
Fluconazole dosing for candidemia step-down therapy: {{c1::800 mg (12 mg/kg)}} loading dose, then {{c2::400 mg (6 mg/kg)}} daily.
After failure of a first TNFi, a second TNFi results in meaningful response (e.g., 50% BASDAI improvement) in approximately {{c1::25–40%}} of patients.
Anticoagulation for cancer-associated VTE should be continued beyond 6 months in patients with {{c1::active cancer}}, {{c1::metastatic disease}}, or {{c1::ongoing chemotherapy}}.
extra: Appendix 6 Q2 Conclusions — Long-term anticoagulation duration

text: LTOT must be used for >{{c1::15}} hours/day; 24-hour use has not been shown superior to 15-hour use per 2024 evidence.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.19 — Oxygen therapy evidence

text: Percutaneous LAA occlusion is reasonable (Class {{c1::2a}}) in AF patients with a {{c2::long-term contraindication to anticoagulation due to a nonreversible cause}}.
extra: Section 6.5.1 and Table 14 — percutaneous LAAO indications

text: KDIGO 2017 recommends performing BMD (DXA) testing in CKD G3a–G5D when evidence of {{c1::CKD-MBD or osteoporosis risk factors}} is present and results will {{c2::alter management}}.
The European guideline does not recommend vaptans when SNa is {{c1::<130 mmol/L}} and recommends against them when SNa is {{c1::<125 mmol/L}}.
extra: Vaptans section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017; Spasovski et al. 2014

text: Short-term T therapy may be considered in HIV-infected men with low testosterone and {{c1::unexplained weight loss}} to promote weight and lean mass gain.
HCC surveillance in HBV patients is performed with {{c1::ultrasound}} every {{c2::6 months}}; AFP every 6 months is reserved for when ultrasound is unavailable.
In adults aged 18–49 with CKD, a statin is indicated if 10-year coronary death/MI risk is >{{c1::10%}}.
PREVENT-ASCVD 10-year risk categories: low {{c1::<3%}}, borderline {{c1::3–<5%}}, intermediate {{c1::5–<10%}}, high {{c1::≥10%}}.
extra: Section 4.2.3.2 / Table 11, Table 12 — PREVENT-ASCVD equations and risk thresholds

text: For symptomatic severe intracranial stenosis (70–99%), first-line treatment is {{c1::aggressive medical management with DAPT for 90 days plus risk factor control}}; {{c2::angioplasty/stenting}} should NOT be used as first-line therapy.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.1.1; SAMMPRIS trial

text: On-demand 2-1-1 PrEP: take {{c1::2 tablets}} 2–24 hours before sex, then {{c2::1 tablet}} at 24 hours and 48 hours after sex.
When rifampin is contraindicated by drug interactions and isoniazid cannot be used, substitute {{c1::rifabutin}}; among rifamycins, weekly {{c2::rifapentine}} has the fewest drug–drug interactions.
Ivabradine is indicated for HFrEF with LVEF {{c1::≤35%}}, sinus rhythm, resting HR {{c2::≥70 bpm}} on maximally tolerated beta blocker; dosed {{c3::5 mg BID}} titrated to 7.5 mg BID.
Sacubitril-valsartan starting dose is {{c1::49/51 mg BID}} (or {{c2::24/26 mg BID}} if low BP or ACEi-naïve); target dose is {{c3::97/103 mg BID}}.
Varenicline for smoking cessation is titrated to a maintenance dose of {{c1::1 mg twice daily (BID)}}.
extra: Section 4.5 Treatment of Tobacco Use; Table 8

text: Radical prostatectomy carries a perioperative mortality of approximately {{c1::3 per 1,000}} and serious surgical complications in approximately {{c2::50 per 1,000}} men.
extra: Discussion: Potential Harms of Treatment — USPSTF 2018

text: Severe MS is defined by MVA ≤{{c1::1.5 cm²}} and diastolic pressure half-time ≥{{c2::150 ms}}.
Statins should be discontinued {{c1::1–2 months}} before planned pregnancy in low-risk patients, but may be {{c2::continued}} in patients with {{c2::HeFH or established ASCVD}}.
extra: Section 5.1 / Tables 25–26 — Medication safety during pregnancy

text: The AJCC 7th edition uses age {{c1::45 years}} as the cutoff for DTC mortality staging (though NTCTCSG data suggest {{c2::55 years}} may be more appropriate).
extra: ATA 2015 — AJCC 7th Edition TNM Table 10; Thresholds & Doses

text: Immediate AED therapy after a first unprovoked seizure may not improve {{c1::quality of life}} (Level C).
extra: Recommendations section — Level C recommendation on AED therapy and quality of life

text: The highest-weighted IMPROVE bleeding risk factor is {{c1::active gastroduodenal ulcer}} ({{c2::4.5}} points), followed by bleeding within 3 months and platelets <50 × 10⁹/L (4 points each).
10-year ASCVD risk categories: low {{c1::<5%}}, borderline {{c2::5–<7.5%}}, intermediate {{c3::≥7.5–<20%}}, high {{c4::≥20%}}.
extra: Section 2.2 Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk; Table 3

text: Smoking cessation in PAD should combine {{c1::behavioral counseling}} with {{c2::pharmacotherapy (varenicline, bupropion, or nicotine replacement)}}.
For extended-phase anticoagulation, the reduced-dose regimen for rivaroxaban is {{c1::10 mg once daily}}.
ACR 2021 {{c1::strongly}} recommends a treat-to-target approach over usual care in bDMARD/tsDMARD-naive patients, with an initial goal of {{c2::low disease activity}} (preferred over remission).
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treat-to-target and treatment modification)

text: Standard argatroban infusion for acute HIT is {{c1::2 µg/kg/min}} IV, titrated to an aPTT of {{c2::1.5–3.0×}} baseline.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: IDSA/SHEA recommends monitoring antibiotic use with {{c1::days of therapy (DOT)}} preferentially over {{c2::defined daily dose (DDD)}} (weak, low).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XX, Recommendation 21

text: In treatment-naive active PsA, the ACR/NPF 2018 guideline recommends starting a {{c1::TNF inhibitor (TNFi) biologic}} over an oral small molecule (OSM).
extra: Table 1 / Figure 3 — Recommendations for OSM- and treatment-naive active PsA

text: The cost-effective first-line test for determining the etiology of thyrotoxicosis is {{c1::TRAb (thyrotropin receptor antibody) measurement}}.
extra: Recommendation 1 (Section B3) — etiology determination using TRAb, RAIU, or Doppler

text: AAA surveillance intervals: diameter 3.0–3.9 cm → every {{c1::3 years}}; 4.0–4.9 cm → {{c2::annually}}; ≥5.0 cm (men) or ≥4.5 cm (women) → every {{c3::6 months}}.
Modeling estimates that biennial screening from age 40-74 (vs. 50-74) averts approximately {{c1::1.8}} additional breast cancer deaths per 1,000 Black women over a lifetime.
extra: Table 2 — modeling estimates of benefits/harms biennial 40-74 vs 50-74

text: In children with {{c1::single-ventricle palliation}}, the AABB 2023 recommended RBC transfusion threshold is Hgb <{{c2::9 g/dL}}.
Surgical mortality for proximal aortic surgery falls significantly at centers performing >{{c1::20–25}} cases/year, and again at >{{c2::50}} cases/year.
For inpatient VTE prophylaxis in acutely ill hospitalized medical patients, {{c1::LMWH}} is recommended over {{c2::DOACs}} (strong recommendation).
T therapy is contraindicated without prior urologic evaluation if PSA is >{{c1::4 ng/mL}}, or >{{c2::3 ng/mL}} in men at high prostate cancer risk.
Empiric therapy for community-acquired SBP is {{c1::cefotaxime 2 g IV q12h}} for {{c2::5–7 days}}.
extra: Table 9 — antibiotics for infections in cirrhosis; AASLD 2021

text: In the standard-dose corticotropin stimulation test, a peak cortisol of {{c1::<500 nmol/L (18 μg/dL)}} indicates adrenal insufficiency.
In very high-risk CCD patients, ezetimibe (then PCSK9 monoclonal antibody) should be added if LDL-C remains ≥{{c1::70 mg/dL}} on maximally tolerated statin.
extra: Section 4.2.6 Lipid Management — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Fulminant CDI (hypotension/shock, ileus, or megacolon) is treated with {{c1::vancomycin 500 mg PO/NG QID}} plus {{c2::IV metronidazole 500 mg every 8 hours}}; rectal vancomycin is added if ileus is present.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: RAI is contraindicated in patients with {{c1::moderate-to-severe or sight-threatening active Graves' orbitopathy}}.
Macrolide monotherapy (azithromycin/clarithromycin) is {{c1::NOT adequate}} for outpatient CAP in the US due to {{c2::pneumococcal macrolide resistance}}.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 2 (antibiotic selection)

text: The standard of care for hypothyroidism is {{c1::levothyroxine (LT4) monotherapy}}; combination LT4/LT3, desiccated thyroid extract, and compounded preparations are not recommended for routine use.
IPSS side-to-side ACTH gradient >1.4 correctly lateralizes in only {{c1::56–69%}} of adults.
RSV vaccine is recommended routinely for all adults ≥{{c1::75}} years.
extra: Notes — RSV vaccination (ages ≥75), 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The recommended pediatric prednisone dose for ITP with mucosal bleeding is {{c1::2–4 mg/kg/day}} (maximum {{c2::120 mg/day}}) for 5–7 days.
Permanent pacing for sinus node dysfunction requires {{c1::symptom-bradycardia correlation}}; no minimum heart rate or pause duration alone justifies pacing.
extra: Top 10 Take-Home Messages; Section 5.4.4/Figure 6 — chronic SND management algorithm

text: Dofetilide initiation requires {{c1::3-day inpatient}} stay with {{c2::continuous ECG monitoring}} due to risk of QT prolongation and torsades de pointes.
Baveno VI criteria to safely avoid screening EGD: LSM <{{c1::20 kPa}} AND platelets >{{c2::150 K/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: Adequate prior screening (to stop at age 65) requires {{c1::3 consecutive negative cytology results}} OR {{c1::2 consecutive negative cotest/HPV results}} within the past 10 years, with the most recent test within {{c2::5 years}}.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Women Older Than 65 Years — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: For women aged 21–29, USPSTF recommends cervical cytology every {{c1::3 years}} (Grade {{c2::A}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary table — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: The standard fidaxomicin regimen for CDI is {{c1::200 mg PO twice daily}} for {{c2::10 days}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: Moderate hyponatremia is SNa {{c1::125–129 mmol/L}}; severe (US) / profound (European) hyponatremia is SNa {{c2::<125 mmol/L}}.
If overcorrection occurs (baseline SNa <120 mmol/L exceeding 6–8 mmol/L/day), SNa should be re-lowered using {{c1::electrolyte-free water (e.g., D5W)}} and/or {{c2::desmopressin}}.
extra: Treatment of Acute Hyponatremia section — Hoorn & Zietse, JASN 2017

text: {{c1::rhTSH stimulation}} is an acceptable alternative to thyroid hormone withdrawal for RAI preparation in low/intermediate-risk DTC patients {{c2::without distant metastases}}.
Initial MMI dose is {{c1::10–20 mg/day}} when free T4 is {{c1::1.5–2× ULN}}.
extra: ATA 2016 — Initial MMI dosing by free T4 level

text: In overweight/obese AF patients (BMI >27), a weight loss target of ≥{{c1::10%}} body weight is recommended to reduce AF burden and recurrence.
extra: Section 5.2 — lifestyle and risk factor modification

text: Documented group A streptococcal necrotizing fasciitis is treated with {{c1::penicillin}} PLUS {{c1::clindamycin}}, along with urgent surgical debridement.
H. pylori resistance rates in the US are <5% for {{c1::amoxicillin, tetracycline, and rifabutin}}, making them preferred backbones for empiric regimens.
extra: ACG 2024 – Section: Antibiotic susceptibility testing; Thresholds & Doses

text: In CKD G5D, the target iPTH range is approximately {{c1::2–9×}} the upper limit of normal for the assay (e.g., ~130–585 pg/mL).
Maintenance immunosuppression (MMF preferred, or azathioprine) should be continued for at least {{c1::36 months}} after achieving complete renal response before considering taper.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Chapter on Maintenance Therapy

text: In suspected CAP, {{c1::lung ultrasound (LUS)}} is an acceptable diagnostic alternative to {{c2::chest radiography}} where appropriate clinician expertise exists (conditional, low-quality evidence).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 1; Table 4

text: For left main disease, PCI is reasonable in selected patients with low-to-medium anatomic complexity, defined as SYNTAX score {{c1::<33}}.
extra: Key Recommendations — left main revascularization; Thresholds & Doses

text: HFrEF is defined as LVEF {{c1::≤40%}}; HFmrEF as LVEF {{c2::41–49%}}; HFpEF as LVEF {{c3::≥50%}}.

Routine duodenal biopsy (rather than serology-first strategy) should be considered when celiac disease prevalence in the population exceeds {{c1::5%}}, when the duodenum appears endoscopically abnormal, or in higher-risk patients.
extra: AGA 2020 IDA Guideline, Recommendation 6; Thresholds & Doses

text: Oxford criteria: >{{c1::8}} BMs on day 3 of IV steroids, or {{c1::3–8}} BMs with CRP >{{c2::45 mg/L}}, predicts an ~85% colectomy risk.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: Natalizumab ({{c1::300 mg IV every 4 weeks}}) requires anti-JC virus antibody testing every {{c2::6 months}} due to PML risk; do not use if antibody is positive.

In low-risk adults (PREVENT 10-year CVD risk <7.5%), antihypertensive pharmacotherapy is initiated at BP ≥{{c1::140/90 mm Hg}}, or at ≥130/80 mm Hg after a {{c2::3–6 month}} lifestyle intervention trial.
In syncope with ischemic or nonischemic cardiomyopathy and LVEF {{c1::≤35%}}, ICD implantation is indicated per HF guidelines; {{c2::EPS is no longer required}} before ICD in this population.
HIV PEP must be initiated within {{c1::72 hours}} of exposure and continued for {{c2::28 days}}.
