For symptomatic PAD, {{c1::single antiplatelet therapy (aspirin or clopidogrel)}} is recommended to reduce MACE; clopidogrel is an acceptable alternative to aspirin.
In severe acute pancreatitis requiring tube feeding, {{c1::enteral nutrition}} (nasogastric is acceptable) is preferred over {{c2::TPN (parenteral nutrition)}}.
After mild gallstone pancreatitis, cholecystectomy should be performed {{c1::during the same admission}} to reduce the risk of recurrence.

Protein intake targets in AKI: {{c1::0.8–1.0}} g/kg/d for non-catabolic patients not on dialysis; {{c2::1.0–1.5}} g/kg/d for patients on RRT; up to {{c3::1.7}} g/kg/d for patients on CRRT or who are hypercatabolic.
Preventive migraine therapy should be offered at ≥{{c1::6}} headache days/month regardless of disability, ≥{{c2::4}} days/month with some disability, or ≥3 days/month with severe disability.
extra: AHS 2021 — Table 4: Criteria for identifying patients for preventive treatment

text: First-line agent for acute symptomatic bradycardia from SND or AV nodal block is {{c1::atropine 0.5-1 mg IV}}, repeated every {{c2::3-5 min}} to a maximum of {{c2::3 mg}}.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: Ezetimibe {{c1::10 mg}} daily reduces LDL-C by approximately {{c2::18%}} as monotherapy and by an additional {{c3::25%}} when added to a statin.
First-line therapy for grade 2 ascites is sodium restriction to {{c1::2 g (90 mmol)/day}} plus {{c2::spironolactone ± furosemide}}.
extra: Table 5 — classification of ascites and treatment; AASLD 2021

text: In the Janszky 2018 Swedish case-crossover study (n=7,013 IE cases), the inpatient OR for IE following bronchoscopy was {{c1::16.0}}, the highest of all NDIPs studied.
extra: Janszky et al 2018 (Ref 3) / Table 3 — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Toxic megacolon is defined as transverse colon diameter >{{c1::5.5 cm}} on plain film.
extra: Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC / Figure 3 algorithm

text: Secondary SBP prophylaxis options include daily {{c1::fluoroquinolone (e.g., ciprofloxacin 500 mg)}} or {{c2::rifaximin}}; during acute variceal bleeding, use {{c3::ceftriaxone}} for 5–7 days.
Budesonide MMX is dosed at {{c1::9 mg/day}} for {{c2::8 weeks}} for mild-to-moderate UC.
extra: Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC

text: An EEG with epileptiform abnormalities increases recurrence risk after a first unprovoked seizure with a relative rate of {{c1::2.16}} (95% CI 1.07–4.38) at 1–5 years (Level A).
extra: Analysis of Evidence: Risk of seizure recurrence — epileptiform EEG

text: The 2026 ACC/AHA guideline replaces the {{c1::Pooled Cohort Equations}} with the {{c2::PREVENT-ASCVD}} equations for 10-year ASCVD risk estimation in adults aged 30–79 years.
The acute dose of rimegepant is {{c1::75 mg ODT}}, with a maximum of {{c2::one dose per 24 hours}}.
extra: AHS 2021 — Recently Approved Acute Treatments: rimegepant

text: Nephrology referral is indicated when the 5-year KFRE risk is >{{c1::3–5%}} in adults with CKD G3–G5.

USPSTF 2022 aspirin recommendations apply only to adults without {{c1::known CVD}} and not at increased {{c2::bleeding risk}} (e.g., no GI ulcer history, recent bleeding, or bleeding-risk medications).
extra: Recommendation Summary table; Practice Considerations — USPSTF 2022

text: A SAAG of {{c1::≥1.1 g/dL}} indicates portal hypertension with ~97% accuracy.
extra: Figure 2 and Table 4 — SAAG-based diagnostic algorithm; AASLD 2021

text: SBP or SBE is diagnosed when ascitic or pleural PMN count exceeds {{c1::250/mm³}}.
Recommended protein intake in CKD G3–G5 is ~{{c1::0.8 g/kg/d}}; intake >{{c2::1.3 g/kg/d}} should be avoided.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Thresholds & Doses (protein intake)

text: Adequate fluoroquinolone dosing for CAP: {{c1::levofloxacin 750 mg}} or {{c2::moxifloxacin 400 mg}}.
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations (antibiotic therapy)

text: Pituitary MRI is indicated in severe secondary hypogonadism when serum testosterone is <{{c1::150 ng/dL}} (5.2 nmol/L), or when hyperprolactinemia or panhypopituitarism is present.
In acute ICH management, prophylactic {{c1::corticosteroids}}, continuous {{c1::hyperosmolar therapy}}, and prophylactic {{c1::antiseizure medications}} are all NOT recommended.
In SCD, nocturnal enuresis warrants a sleep study when the patient is age ≥{{c1::10}} years.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Sleep-Disordered Breathing: Screening

text: Cefepime and ceftazidime are each dosed at {{c1::2 g IV q8h}} for HAP/VAP.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X & Table 3

text: To avoid osmotic demyelination in HHS, serum osmolality should fall by no more than {{c1::3.0–8.0 mOsm/kg/h}}.
For patients with a prior serious infection within {{c1::12 months}} and inadequate csDMARD response, additional csDMARDs are preferred over adding a bDMARD or tsDMARD.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: Secondary non-response to a first TNFi (relapse generally beyond initial {{c1::6 months}}) is best managed by switching to {{c2::a different TNFi}} rather than to a non-TNFi biologic.
extra: Section A (PICO 10); Table 1 — Definition of secondary non-response

text: USPSTF 2021 directs clinicians to stop lung cancer screening once a patient has not smoked for {{c1::15 years}}, develops a health problem substantially limiting life expectancy, or cannot/will not undergo {{c2::curative lung surgery}}.
The 2019 guideline conditionally recommends against treat-to-target using ASDAS thresholds of <{{c1::1.3}} (inactive disease) or <{{c2::2.1}} (low disease activity).
The physiologic criterion for PPF includes an absolute decline in Hb-corrected DLCO of ≥{{c1::10%}} predicted within 1 year.
extra: Table 4 — Definition of Progressive Pulmonary Fibrosis; Thresholds & Doses

text: In AF with HFrEF, the preferred antiarrhythmic agents are {{c1::amiodarone}} and {{c1::dofetilide}}; class IC agents, dronedarone, and sotalol should be {{c2::avoided}}.
A primary-prevention ICD is indicated in post-MI patients with LVEF {{c1::≤30%}} even when asymptomatic, provided ≥{{c2::40 days}} post-MI and ≥{{c3::90 days}} post-revascularization.
extra: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA HF Guideline — Section 7.3 / ICD recommendations

text: In renal transplant recipients, ASB should NOT be screened or treated when the patient is {{c1::>1 month}} post-transplant; evidence is insufficient for those {{c1::<1 month}} post-transplant.
The rate of bacteriuria acquisition with an indwelling urinary catheter is approximately {{c1::3%–5%}} per catheter day, reaching a prevalence of ~{{c2::100%}} with long-term catheterization.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Thresholds & Doses; Section XI (Indwelling catheters)

text: The literature search window for the ADA Standards of Care 2026 edition spans {{c1::1 June 2024}} to {{c2::18 July 2025}}, using PubMed, Medline, and EMBASE (English-language only).
extra: Methodology and Procedure — systematic review process and search dates

text: Outpatient PID treatment consists of ceftriaxone 500 mg IM × 1 PLUS doxycycline 100 mg BID × 14 days PLUS {{c1::metronidazole 500 mg BID × 14 days}} (metronidazole now routinely added).
extra: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease, Recommended Regimens — addition of metronidazole to all PID regimens, CDC STI Guidelines 2021
KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (GFR categories)

text: Vasoreactivity testing is performed only in {{c1::IPAH/HPAH/DPAH}} using inhaled NO, inhaled iloprost, or IV epoprostenol.
extra: Recommendation Table 7 — vasoreactivity testing and CCB therapy criteria

text: For anti-IL5/5R biologic eligibility in severe asthma, blood eosinophil count ≥{{c1::150}}/μL suggests Type 2 inflammation; ≥{{c2::300}}/μL is a common threshold for prescribing.
The first-line vasopressor in septic shock is {{c1::norepinephrine}}.
In the 3HP regimen, children aged 2–11 years receive isoniazid {{c1::25 mg/kg}} (max 900 mg), while adults and children ≥12 years receive {{c2::15 mg/kg}} (max 900 mg).
Finerenone should be initiated only if K+ ≤{{c1::4.8}} mmol/l and held if K+ >{{c2::5.5}} mmol/l.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.8.1 and Figure 26

text: Patients at high IE risk — where preventive interventions should be focused — include those with {{c1::prosthetic valves}}, {{c2::prior IE}}, certain congenital heart disease, or valve repair with prosthetic material.
extra: AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory — Key Recommendations

text: Healthcare personnel born in 1957 or later require {{c1::2 doses}} of MMR vaccine.
extra: Adult Schedule Notes — MMR, 2025 ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: After large-volume paracentesis of >5 L, albumin should be given at {{c1::6–8 g per liter of ascites removed}} to prevent post-paracentesis circulatory dysfunction.
Molnupiravir dosing: {{c1::800 mg PO every 12 hours × 5 days}}; patients should use contraception during treatment and for {{c2::3 months}} after.
extra: How to Approach a Patient with Mild to Moderate COVID-19 — antiviral selection algorithm

text: In patients with atherosclerotic stroke/TIA, the LDL-C target is {{c1::<70 mg/dL}}, supported by the {{c2::TST}} trial.
A karyotype should be obtained in primary hypogonadism of unknown etiology when testicular volume is <{{c1::6 mL}}, to evaluate for {{c2::Klinefelter syndrome}}.
To estimate 10-year CVD risk when considering aspirin in adults aged 40–59, USPSTF 2022 recommends using the {{c1::ACC/AHA Pooled Cohort Equations}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Treatment or Intervention — USPSTF 2022

text: VCTE liver stiffness interpretation: <{{c1::8}} kPa rules out advanced fibrosis; {{c1::8–12}} kPa suggests possible fibrotic NASH; >{{c1::12}} kPa indicates advanced fibrosis; ≥{{c1::20}} kPa suggests cirrhosis (with FIB-4 >3.48).
For general adults, USPSTF-recommended validated depression screening tools include the {{c1::PHQ (any version)}} and the {{c2::CES-D}}.
AAN 2018 recommends offering DMT to patients with CIS (single demyelinating event) plus {{c1::≥2 brain lesions characteristic of MS}} who elect therapy (Level B).
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Recommendation 7

text: An adequate trial of an oral preventive migraine medication requires at least {{c1::8 weeks}} at target dose before declaring treatment failure.
extra: AHS 2021 — Preventive Treatment: Developing Treatment Plans

text: The pediatric blood pressure target in glomerular disease is a 24-hour MAP ≤{{c1::50th percentile for age/sex/height}}.
For GOLD Group E, initial therapy is LABA+LAMA; consider adding {{c1::ICS}} if blood eosinophils ≥{{c2::300}} cells/µL.
extra: GOLD 2026, Chapter 3, Figure 3.8 — Initial pharmacological treatment algorithm (Group E)

text: Spironolactone is started at {{c1::12.5–25 mg daily}} and uptitrated to a target of {{c2::25–50 mg daily}} in HFrEF.
{{c1::Idarucizumab}} {{c2::5 g IV}} (two 2.5 g vials) is used to reverse {{c3::dabigatran}}.
Secondary prophylaxis after AVH consists of combination {{c1::NSBB + serial EVL}} every {{c2::2–4 weeks}} until variceal eradication.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Prevention of recurrent hemorrhage

text: Carotid revascularization for symptomatic severe stenosis should be performed within {{c1::2 weeks}} of the index stroke or TIA.
extra: 2021 AHA/ASA Stroke/TIA Prevention Guideline — Section 5.1.2.1 (Extracranial carotid)

text: Fondaparinux SC daily dosing for HIT by weight: <50 kg → {{c1::5 mg}}; 50–100 kg → {{c2::7.5 mg}}; >100 kg → {{c3::10 mg}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: An alternative to fondaparinux for superficial vein thrombosis when parenteral therapy is refused is {{c1::rivaroxaban 10 mg daily}} for 45 days.
The 2021 USPSTF assigns a Grade {{c1::B}} recommendation to colorectal cancer screening for average-risk adults aged {{c2::45-49}} years.
Depemokimab (long-acting anti-IL5) is dosed at {{c1::100 mg SC every 26 weeks}} for patients ≥{{c2::12}} years.
An optional corticosteroid adjunct for cellulitis in nondiabetic adults is {{c1::prednisone 40 mg}} daily × {{c2::7 days}}.
IV {{c1::erythromycin}} {{c2::125–250 mg}}, given 30–120 minutes before endoscopy, improves visualization in AVH.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Table 5

text: Glucagon dosing for beta-blocker overdose: {{c1::3-10 mg IV bolus}}, then {{c2::3-5 mg/h}} infusion.
extra: Section 5.3.2/Table 8 — acute medical management drug doses for bradycardia

text: Congenital hypothyroidism should be treated immediately upon positive newborn screen with LT4 {{c1::10–15 μg/kg/d}}, aiming to normalize T4 within {{c2::2–4 weeks}}.
In sepsis resuscitation, the SSC 2021 recommends avoiding {{c1::hydroxyethyl starches}} and {{c2::gelatins}}.
extra: Initial Resuscitation — fluid type recommendations; SSC 2021

text: The most common ARR cutoff when PAC is in ng/dL and PRA in ng/mL/h is {{c1::30}}; when PAC is in pmol/L, the cutoff is {{c2::750}}.
In AIS management, treat hyperglycemia >{{c1::180 mg/dL}} and correct hypoglycemia when glucose falls below {{c2::60 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.5 — Blood Glucose Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: IDSA/SHEA recommends promoting timely {{c1::IV-to-oral (PO) conversion}} as an ASP strategy (strong, moderate).
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline — Section XI, Recommendation 12

text: Mepolizumab for EGPA (adults) is dosed at {{c1::300 mg}} SC every {{c2::4 weeks}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 1 (drug dosing regimens)

text: A biochemical incomplete response is defined as suppressed Tg {{c1::≥1 ng/mL}} or stimulated Tg {{c2::≥10 ng/mL}}.
TC-325 hemostatic powder spray is indicated for {{c1::actively bleeding ulcers}} and should NOT be used for {{c2::nonbleeding lesions}}.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Choice of endoscopic hemostatic therapy / Table 7

text: In confirmed PA, adrenal CT is obtained first to exclude {{c1::adrenocortical carcinoma}} and to guide {{c2::adrenal venous sampling (AVS)}}.

The lifetime risk of death from prostate cancer is {{c1::4.2%}} in African American men, compared with {{c2::2.3%}} in white men; USPSTF recommends individualized shared decision-making for this group but does not recommend screening before age 55.
extra: Clinical Considerations: African American Men — USPSTF 2018

text: The 2021 USPSTF assigns a Grade {{c1::B}} recommendation to annual LDCT lung cancer screening in high-risk adults.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — Grade B assignment

text: Anti-GBM disease is treated without delay with {{c1::cyclophosphamide}} (2–3 months) plus {{c1::glucocorticoids}} (6 months) plus daily {{c1::plasma exchange}} until antibodies are undetectable.
An LSM >{{c1::25 kPa}} by transient elastography rules in CSPH in non-obese patients.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: In adults, routine monitoring of serum calcium, phosphate, PTH, and alkaline phosphatase should begin at CKD stage {{c1::G3a}}.
Despite higher CRC incidence and mortality in Black adults, the 2021 USPSTF applies the {{c1::same starting age of 45}} without a separate race-based recommendation.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening in Black Adults

text:
Classic OH: SBP fall ≥20/DBP ≥10 mm Hg within {{c1::3 minutes}} of standing.
Delayed OH: same fall developing {{c2::>3 minutes}} after standing.
Initial OH: transient fall within {{c3::15 seconds}} of standing.
MRAs are contraindicated in HFrEF if eGFR is {{c1::≤30 mL/min/1.73 m²}} or serum potassium is {{c2::≥5.0 mEq/L}}.
In patients with AF and VHD, NOACs are contraindicated in {{c1::rheumatic mitral stenosis}} and {{c2::mechanical prosthetic valves}}; VKA (target INR 2.5) is required instead.
Acetaminophen, {{c1::duloxetine}}, and {{c1::tramadol}} are conditionally recommended for OA, reserved for patients with NSAID contraindications or inadequate response.
extra: Table 2 / Pharmacologic management — acetaminophen, duloxetine, tramadol

text: High-risk neutropenia, for which there is NO recommendation for or against ASB screening, is defined as ANC {{c1::<100 cells/mm³}} for ≥{{c2::7 days}} following cytotoxic chemotherapy.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Thresholds & Doses; Key Recommendations (neutropenia)

text: The cumulative cyclophosphamide dose cap in membranous nephropathy is ~{{c1::25–36 g}}, reduced to {{c1::10 g}} if fertility preservation is required.

Cardiac rhythm monitor selection should match symptom frequency: use a Holter monitor for {{c1::daily}} symptoms and an implantable cardiac monitor for {{c2::infrequent recurrent unexplained}} syncope.

In axial SpA patients with contraindications to TNFi due to CHF or demyelinating disease, prefer {{c1::secukinumab or ixekizumab}}; if the contraindication is infection risk or TB, prefer {{c2::sulfasalazine}}.
Before CABG, hold clopidogrel or ticagrelor for ≥{{c1::3–5 days}} and prasugrel for ≥{{c2::7 days}}.
An HCV RNA at {{c1::week 4}} of therapy should be considered when there is concern for {{c2::suboptimal DAA adherence}}.
extra: Initial Visit algorithm — medication dispensing and adherence footnote

text: PA screening is indicated in resistant hypertension defined as BP >140/90 on {{c1::3}} antihypertensive drugs including a {{c2::diuretic}}.
After diagnosis of acute pancreatitis is established, serial amylase/lipase measurements {{c1::should NOT be followed}}, as they do not predict severity, prognosis, or readiness to refeed.

Outpatients and inpatients with {{c1::nonsevere}} CAP reaching clinical stability should be treated with {{c2::<5 days}} of antibiotics (minimum 3 days) rather than ≥5 days (conditional recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 3; Table 6

text: Prasugrel is contraindicated in patients with a history of {{c1::prior stroke or TIA}}.
CT colonography is performed every {{c1::5 years}} for colorectal cancer screening.
In HCM, a major SCD risk factor is unexplained syncope within the prior {{c1::6 months}}.
extra: Section 7.4, Table 8 — HCM SCD risk factors

text: Finerenone (nonsteroidal MRA) should be added in T2D with eGFR >{{c1::25}} ml/min/1.73 m², normal K+, and persistent albuminuria despite maximum RASi (2A).
Ablation is the preferred treatment for HCC ≤{{c1::3 cm}}; resection is superior for larger tumors.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Surgical Resection / Figure 10

text: TBLC is relatively contraindicated when DLCO is {{c1::<35%}} predicted or sPAP is {{c2::>40 mm Hg}}.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF — TBLC relative contraindications

text: For AS patients {{c1::>80 years}} or with life expectancy <10 years, {{c2::TAVI}} is the preferred approach.
For methotrexate intolerance, switch to {{c1::subcutaneous}} administration or split dosing (with increased folate) before changing to a different DMARD.
The SSC 2021 recommends {{c1::balanced crystalloids}} as first-line resuscitation fluid in sepsis; {{c2::albumin}} is reserved for patients receiving large crystalloid volumes.
extra: Initial Resuscitation — fluid type recommendations; SSC 2021

text: CHEST 2021 recommends anticoagulating cerebral vein/venous sinus thrombosis for at least {{c1::3 months}} (strong recommendation despite low-certainty evidence).
Referral to a specialist is recommended for atypical PMR presentations including age {{c1::<60 years}}, peripheral arthritis, or low inflammatory markers.
Adults with primary adrenal insufficiency are treated with hydrocortisone {{c1::15–25 mg/day}} in {{c2::2–3 divided oral doses}}, with the largest dose on awakening.
Before mucosa-breaching urologic procedures, targeted antimicrobial therapy should be administered {{c1::30–60 minutes}} before the procedure, ideally as {{c2::1–2 doses}}.
Before elective nonurologic surgery — including orthopedic implant procedures — ASB should {{c1::NOT}} be screened for or treated; rely on {{c2::standard perioperative prophylaxis}}.

Optimized BQT components and doses (14 days):
Bismuth {{c1::≥300 mg QID}}
Metronidazole {{c2::1.5–2 g/day in 3–4 divided doses}}
Tetracycline {{c3::500 mg QID}}
PPI standard-dose BID.
In patients with a prior lymphoproliferative disorder for which it is approved, {{c1::rituximab}} is the preferred bDMARD.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: In diabetic patients with multivessel CAD, {{c1::CABG}} is preferred over PCI to reduce mortality; PCI is reserved for patients who are {{c2::poor surgical candidates}}.
extra: Section 8.2 — CABG preferred over PCI in diabetic multivessel disease (FREEDOM)

text: Prophylactic anticoagulation should be considered in membranous nephropathy when serum albumin is <{{c1::20–25 g/l}}, especially with proteinuria >10 g/d, BMI >35, or other thrombotic risk factors.
Early surgery in IE is indicated for vegetation >{{c1::10 mm}} with severe valve dysfunction; embolic risk is highest in the first {{c2::2 weeks}} of antibiotic therapy.
PA screening with ARR is indicated in patients with sustained BP >{{c1::150/100}} mm Hg on three separate occasions.
With a high-probability 4Ts score, the functional assay may be omitted if the ELISA optical density is {{c1::≥2.0}} units.
Pretreatment labs required before or at HCV treatment initiation include {{c1::AST}}, {{c1::ALT}}, {{c1::platelets}}, and {{c1::HBsAg}}.
extra: Initial Visit algorithm — labs (AST/ALT/PLT/HBsAg) and medication dispensing

text: Very severe AS is defined by Vmax ≥{{c1::5 m/s}} or mean gradient ≥{{c2::60 mm Hg}}.
Adult inpatients with {{c1::nonsevere}} CAP: systemic corticosteroids should {{c2::NOT}} be administered ({{c3::strong}} recommendation).
extra: 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline — Summary of Recommendations, Question 4

text: For global IBS symptoms, {{c1::TCAs}} are recommended over {{c2::SSRIs}}, starting at a low dose (e.g., amitriptyline 10 mg) and titrating up (NNT 4.5).
extra: Recommendation on TCAs and gut-directed psychotherapy — ACG 2021 IBS Guideline

text: A key change from the 2016 USPSTF statement is that women in their 40s should now receive {{c1::universal biennial screening}} starting at age 40, rather than {{c2::individualized decision-making}}.
extra: Clinician Summary / What's new — change from 2016 (individualized 40-49 → universal biennial 40-49)

text: In OA management, acetaminophen maximum dosage is {{c1::3 g}} daily in divided doses, with monitoring for {{c2::hepatotoxicity}} with regular use.
In a patient with LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL and clinical ASCVD, the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<55 mg/dL}}.
extra: Section 4.2.4.3 / Figure 7 — Tiered LDL-C goals for severe hypercholesterolemia

text: CHEST 2021 recommends against extended-phase anticoagulation after VTE provoked by a {{c1::major transient risk factor}} (strong, moderate-certainty).
COPD is confirmed by post-bronchodilator {{c1::FEV1/FVC < 0.7}} on spirometry in symptomatic patients with risk factor exposure.
IDSA recommends against {{c1::hydroxychloroquine}}, {{c2::lopinavir/ritonavir}}, {{c3::ivermectin}} (ambulatory), and colchicine (hospitalized) for treatment of COVID-19.
Black women have approximately {{c1::40%}} higher breast cancer mortality compared with White women, a key disparity motivating the 2024 USPSTF's universal start at age 40.
extra: Disparities section — Black women 40% higher breast cancer mortality

text: After PCI in selected SIHD patients, transition to {{c1::P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy}} after {{c2::1–3 months}} of DAPT is acceptable to reduce bleeding.
extra: Section 14.2 / Figure 7 / Table 9 — DAPT duration and P2Y12 monotherapy after PCI

text: For superficial vein thrombosis at increased risk of progression, CHEST 2021 recommends {{c1::fondaparinux 2.5 mg daily}} for {{c2::45 days}} as preferred therapy.
Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) are {{c1::contraindicated}} in post-liver-transplant patients, and should be discontinued ≥{{c2::3 months}} prior to transplant if used as bridge therapy.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Systemic Therapy / Figure 16

text: Initial combination therapy for low/intermediate-risk IPAH/HPAH/DPAH without cardiopulmonary comorbidities is {{c1::ERA + PDE5i}} (e.g., ambrisentan + tadalafil or macitentan + tadalafil).
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10, Figure 9 — PAH treatment algorithm and combination therapy

text: For nosocomial SBP or high MDRO risk, empiric therapy should be broadened to {{c1::piperacillin-tazobactam ± vancomycin/daptomycin/meropenem}}.
extra: Table 9 — antibiotics for infections in cirrhosis; AASLD 2021

text: ACEi or ARB at maximum tolerated dose is recommended for CKD with {{c1::A2 or A3}} albuminuria, regardless of diabetes status.
To avoid osmotic demyelination syndrome (ODS), serum sodium should be corrected by no more than {{c1::8–10 mEq/L per 24 hours}} (max {{c2::4–6 mEq/L/day}} in high-ODS-risk patients).
Vitamin E {{c1::800 IU/day}} (rrr α-tocopherol) may be considered for biopsy-proven NASH in {{c2::non-diabetic}} adults (PIVENS regimen).
Per KDIGO 2022, SGLT2i should be initiated in T2D with CKD when eGFR is ≥{{c1::20}} ml/min/1.73 m² for kidney and cardiovascular protection (1A).

Adults <40 years should be treated with an oral bisphosphonate if they have a prior osteoporotic fracture OR are continuing GC ≥7.5 mg/day for ≥6 months with Z-score <{{c1::-3}} or bone loss >{{c2::10%/year}}.
IV options for MSSA SSTI include {{c1::nafcillin/oxacillin}} 1–2 g IV q4h or {{c2::cefazolin}} 1 g IV q8h.
In CTEPH, the treatment of choice for surgically accessible disease is {{c1::pulmonary endarterectomy (PEA)}}; inoperable or residual disease is managed with {{c2::BPA + riociguat}}.
extra: Recommendation Table 24 / Figures 14–15 — CTEPH multimodality management

text: MSM should be screened for STIs (at all exposed anatomic sites) at least {{c1::annually}}, or every {{c2::3–6 months}} if at higher risk or on PrEP.
extra: STI Detection Among Special Populations, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: For long-term secondary prevention post-MI (1-3 years out), ticagrelor is used at {{c1::60 mg twice daily}} (PEGASUS trial).
extra: Section 4.3.1 Antiplatelet Therapy — 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline

text: Patients with a Glasgow-Blatchford score of {{c1::0–1}} may be discharged from the ED with outpatient follow-up if there is no other reason for admission.
extra: ACG 2021 UGIB — Risk stratification section / Table 2

text: For sepsis-induced hypoperfusion or septic shock, give at least {{c1::30 mL/kg}} IV crystalloid within the first {{c2::3 hours}}.
Surgery for primary MR (repair preferred) is triggered by LVEF ≤{{c1::60%}} or LVESD ≥{{c2::40 mm}} in asymptomatic patients.
Asymptomatic bacteriuria (ASB) is defined as ≥{{c1::10⁵ CFU/mL}} of ≥1 bacterial species in urine without signs or symptoms of UTI, irrespective of {{c2::pyuria}}.
Transcatheter edge-to-edge MV repair for secondary MR in HFrEF requires LVEF {{c1::20–50%}}, LVESD {{c2::≤70 mm}}, and PASP {{c3::≤70 mm Hg}} (COAPT criteria).
Adrenal crisis in adults is treated with hydrocortisone {{c1::100 mg IV/IM bolus}}, followed by {{c2::200 mg/24 h continuous infusion (or 50 mg q6h)}}, plus rapid isotonic saline resuscitation.
Per 2021 USPSTF, colorectal cancer screening should be {{c1::discontinued}} after age {{c2::85}} years.
extra: Practice Considerations: Starting and Stopping Ages

text: Before initiating thyroid hormone in myxedema coma, {{c1::empiric stress-dose glucocorticoids}} should be given to prevent adrenal crisis.
Before administering IVT for AIS, blood pressure must be reduced to <{{c1::185/110 mm Hg}}.
extra: Section 4.3 — Blood Pressure Management; Thresholds & Doses

text: For multiple CDI recurrences, a vancomycin + rifaximin chaser consists of vancomycin 125 mg PO QID × 10 days, followed by {{c1::rifaximin 400 mg PO TID × 20 days}}.
extra: IDSA/SHEA 2021 — Table: Recommendations for the Treatment of CDI in Adults

text: First-degree AV block is defined as a PR interval >{{c1::200 ms}}; severe first-degree AV block (pseudo-pacemaker syndrome) is defined as PR >{{c2::300 ms}}.
extra: Section 2.2/Table 3 — definitions of SND, AV block grades, and conduction disorders

text: HbA1c should be monitored {{c1::twice yearly}} if stable, and up to {{c2::4 times yearly}} if off target or after a therapy change.
In pregnant women with PAI, hydrocortisone dose should be increased by {{c1::20–40%}} from approximately {{c2::24 weeks}} gestation.
RSV vaccine is recommended via {{c1::shared clinical decision-making}} for adults aged {{c2::50–74}} years with risk factors.
extra: Notes — RSV vaccination (ages 50–74), 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: The Euro-Lupus cyclophosphamide regimen for lupus nephritis is {{c1::500 mg IV every 2 weeks × 6 doses}} (total 3 months).

In adults with newly diagnosed ITP who are asymptomatic or have minor mucocutaneous bleeding, observe rather than treat with corticosteroids if platelet count is {{c1::≥30 ×10⁹/L}} (strong recommendation).
extra: Recommendations 1a–1b — corticosteroids vs observation thresholds in adults

text: Adults at risk for mpox should receive a {{c1::2-dose JYNNEOS}} series.
extra: Notes — Mpox vaccination; Table 1, 2025 CDC/ACIP Adult Immunization Schedule

text: Vitamin {{c1::B12}} levels should be monitored in patients on metformin for more than {{c2::4 years}}.
For DVT prophylaxis in immobile stroke patients, use {{c1::intermittent pneumatic compression (IPC)}}; avoid {{c2::elastic/graded compression stockings}} due to skin injury risk.
For frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent nephrotic syndrome in children, sick-day dosing during a URI is prednisone {{c1::0.5 mg/kg/d}} for {{c2::5–7 days}}.
For patients on maximally tolerated methotrexate who are not at target, addition of a {{c1::bDMARD or tsDMARD}} is conditionally preferred over triple therapy.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treat-to-target and treatment modification); Discussion section

text: HCC surveillance in at-risk patients should be performed using {{c1::ultrasound plus AFP}} every {{c2::6 months}}.

When intervention is required for infected necrosis, the preferred approach is a minimally invasive {{c1::step-up strategy}} ({{c2::percutaneous or endoscopic drainage}} first, escalating only if needed).
In the 3HR regimen, adults receive isoniazid {{c1::5 mg/kg}} (max 300 mg) plus rifampin {{c2::10 mg/kg}} (max 600 mg) daily for 3 months.
For H. pylori patients who fail optimized BQT, the preferred salvage regimen is {{c1::rifabutin triple therapy}} for 14 days.
A {{c1::80%}} Professional Practice Committee (PPC) consensus vote is required to approve any ADA Standards of Care recommendation.
extra: Evidence to Recommendations — 80% PPC consensus voting threshold

text: Primary non-response to a first TNFi (no meaningful improvement at {{c1::3–6 months}}) is best managed by switching to {{c2::secukinumab or ixekizumab}} rather than to a different TNFi.
extra: Section A (PICO 10); Table 1 — Definition of primary non-response

text: Initial treatment of childhood nephrotic syndrome is prednisone {{c1::60 mg/m²/d}} (max {{c2::60 mg/d}}) for 4–6 weeks, followed by alternate-day tapering, for a total of 8–12 weeks.
Per 2021 USPSTF, screening colonoscopy is associated with approximately {{c1::14.6}} major bleeds and {{c2::3.1}} perforations per 10,000 procedures.
Preemptive TIPS should be placed within {{c1::24–72 hours}} of AVH in patients who are CTP {{c2::B with active bleeding on endoscopy}} or CTP {{c2::C (score 10–13)}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Section: AVH initial bleed / Figure 4

text: First-line antihypertensive therapy in TAA is {{c1::beta blockers}}; {{c2::ARBs}} are preferred for combination therapy, especially in Marfan syndrome.
The hemoglobin A1c goal in CCD is generally {{c1::<7%}}, relaxed to {{c2::<8% or <8.5%}} in older adults (>65 y) with significant comorbidities.
Prophylactic antiviral therapy is strongly recommended for {{c1::HBcAb-positive}} patients starting rituximab, regardless of HBsAg status.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: Primary PCI is preferred over fibrinolysis for STEMI when it can be achieved within {{c1::120 minutes}} of first medical contact.

Smoking cessation with combined counseling plus pharmacotherapy ({{c1::varenicline}}, bupropion, or NRT) is the single most effective intervention to alter COPD natural history; {{c2::e-cigarettes}} are not recommended for cessation.
The IM methylprednisolone starting dose in PMR is {{c1::120 mg}} every {{c2::3 weeks}} (through week 9), as an alternative to oral glucocorticoids.
HPA axis recovery takes approximately {{c1::6–12 months}} after resection of an ACTH-producing tumor, and approximately {{c2::18 months}} after unilateral adrenalectomy.
After I&D of a purulent SSTI, add anti-MRSA antibiotics when any of the following are present: {{c1::SIRS}}, immunocompromised state, failed initial therapy, or severe disease.
KDIGO 2012 suggests considering oral {{c1::N-acetylcysteine (NAC)}} with IV crystalloids for CI-AKI prevention (2D), but recommends against NAC for prevention of {{c2::postsurgical}} AKI (1A).
Refractory ascites is defined as failure of {{c1::spironolactone 400 mg/day + furosemide 160 mg/day}} for ≥1 week with sodium restriction, with weight loss <{{c2::0.8 kg}} over 4 days.
A major transient risk factor (e.g., surgery >30 min, hospital bed-rest ≥3 days) must occur within {{c1::3 months}} before VTE to qualify as a provoking factor.
After ICH in a patient with atrial fibrillation, anticoagulation may be resumed approximately {{c1::7–8 weeks}} after the hemorrhage.
Gynecomastia occurs in ~{{c1::7%}} of patients on spironolactone <50 mg/day vs ~{{c2::52%}} at >150 mg/day.
Simvastatin maximum dose is {{c1::20 mg/day}} in decompensated cirrhosis (CTP B–C); avoid entirely if bilirubin >{{c2::3 mg/dL}}.

Endocarditis prophylaxis before dental procedures involving gingival manipulation is recommended only for: {{c1::prosthetic valves}}, prior IE, unrepaired cyanotic CHD, repaired CHD with prosthetic material, or heart transplant with valvulopathy.
Dual-chamber permanent pacing for vasovagal syncope is reserved for selected patients aged {{c1::≥40 years}} with recurrent syncope and documented spontaneous prolonged {{c2::asystolic pauses}}.
An eGFR decline of up to {{c1::30%}} after starting an ACEi or ARB is acceptable; persistent decline >30% warrants reassessment.
A key change from the 2012 ACR guideline is that the 2019 guideline now {{c1::strongly recommends AGAINST}} glucosamine for OA (previously conditionally recommended).
extra: Pharmacologic management — Glucosamine and chondroitin sulfate sections — strong recommendation against (change from 2012)

text: Late latent or unknown-duration syphilis requires benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM {{c1::weekly × 3 doses}} (7.2 million units total).
extra: Syphilis — Latent section, CDC STI Guidelines 2021

text: AASLD 2023 endorses the {{c1::BCLC 2022}} staging framework for HCC, and recommends all patients be discussed at a {{c2::multidisciplinary tumor board}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance — Staging section / Figure 9

text: When enoxaparin is used with fibrinolysis in patients aged ≥75 years, the dose is {{c1::0.75 mg/kg SC q12h with no IV bolus}}.
At initial workup for Crohn's disease, {{c1::CTE or MRE}} is recommended for small bowel imaging.
AABB 2023 makes {{c1::no recommendation}} for or against a restrictive vs liberal transfusion strategy in patients with {{c2::acute myocardial infarction}}, pending further trial data.
In adults ≥40 starting long-term GC therapy, initial fracture risk assessment with FRAX (GC-adjusted) and BMD should be performed within {{c1::6 months}} of GC initiation.
Albuminuria categories: A1 = {{c1::<30 mg/g}}, A2 = {{c2::30–300 mg/g}}, A3 = {{c3::>300 mg/g}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 CKD Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (albuminuria categories)

text: Prophylactic antiviral therapy is indicated for HBcAb-positive patients who are also {{c1::HBsAg-positive}} when starting any bDMARD or tsDMARD.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific patient populations)

text: Naltrexone-bupropion ER should be avoided in patients with {{c1::seizure disorders}} and those taking concomitant {{c2::opiates}}.

Propofol infusion syndrome risk is increased when the infusion rate exceeds {{c1::80 μg/kg/min}} or duration exceeds {{c2::48 hours}}, and it manifests with metabolic acidosis, rhabdomyolysis, and cardiac failure.
extra: Brophy et al., Neurocrit Care 2012;17:3–23 — Refractory SE section; Thresholds & Doses

text: PA screening is indicated in hypertension plus any of the following: {{c1::hypokalemia}}, adrenal {{c2::incidentaloma}}, sleep apnea, or a first-degree relative with PA.
In selected children, a nonbiopsy diagnosis of celiac disease is reasonable when TTG IgA is >{{c1::10× ULN}} confirmed by a positive {{c2::EMA}} on a second blood sample.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac — Diagnosis / Figure 1; Testing in Children section

text: The four confirmatory tests for PA are: oral {{c1::sodium load}}, {{c1::saline infusion}}, {{c1::fludrocortisone suppression}}, and {{c1::captopril challenge}}.
When tapering combination therapy, gradually discontinue {{c1::methotrexate}} rather than the bDMARD/tsDMARD; in triple therapy, discontinue {{c2::sulfasalazine}} rather than hydroxychloroquine.
Among DOACs for acute HIT, {{c1::rivaroxaban}} is the preferred agent.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 3.1 and Table 2

text: Annual LDCT lung cancer screening is recommended for smokers aged {{c1::50–80}} years with ≥{{c2::20}} pack-years who currently smoke or quit within {{c3::15}} years.
USPSTF recommends against (Grade D) cervical cancer screening in women younger than {{c1::21}} years, regardless of {{c2::sexual history}}.
extra: Recommendation Summary table — 2024 USPSTF Cervical Cancer Screening

text: AIR-only regimen for adults/adolescents: {{c1::budesonide-formoterol 160/4.5 mcg}} (200/6 metered), 1 inhalation as needed; seek care if >{{c2::12}} inhalations in 24 hours.
